sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • "Energy depletion" is when Operator energy is at 25 energy or lower.

• Activation is maintained as long as energy remains below 25.

• At max rank, your Amp needs a minimum of 36% Critical Chance to reach 100%.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be bought from Vox Solaris for 7,500 Standing 7,500 upon reaching the rank of Hand .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Amps can still destroy Kuva clouds.

• Rank 3 conversion percent is actually 96% , not 98% as shown in-game.

• Thrax enemies are immune to non-Void damage, making this arcane a very poor choice for use in any mission where they appear.

• When using multiple conversion arcanes, the damage is determined by base damage of the amp multiplied by the conversion percent of the arcane divided by the sum of all the conversion percent. This value is then multiplied by the lesser of the following two values: the sum of the conversion % or 98%.
 Base Damage ⋅ Conversion % ∑ Conversion % ⋅ m i n ( ∑ Conversion % , 98 % )
 • For example, using a max rank Virtuos Forge (96% conversion) with a rank 2 Virtuos Trojan (72% conversion) on a Rahn Prism will result in

 1000 * 96% / (96% + 72%) * 98% = 560 Heat damage and
 1000 * 72% / (96% + 72%) * 98% = 420 Viral damage.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be bought from The Quills for 2,500 Standing 2,500 upon reaching the rank of Mote .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The Amp will still need to go through it's recharge delay before the enhanced recharging will have an effect.
 • The standard recharge delay for Amps 2 seconds. As such, Virtuos Null has to be at least rank 2 to provide a substantial effect.

• Juttni and Plaga Braces reduce recharge delay, allowing more mileage from this arcane.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be bought from The Quills for 10000 Standing 10000 upon reaching the rank of Architect .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The increase in critical hit chance is multiplicative . As an example, using a Raplak Prism with a Lohrin Brace and Rank 3 Virtuos Shadow will result in:
 • CritChance = (38% + 12%) × (1 + 0.6) = 80%

• Effect can be triggered by Void Slinging into an Eidolon 's head.
 • Works well with Magus Cloud

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be purchased from The Quills for 10,000 Standing 10,000 upon reaching the rank of Instrument .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The critical damage multiplier bonus is multiplicative .

• This arcane pairs very well with the Lohrin Brace / Certus Brace , Shraksun Scaffold , and Granmu Prism due to their innately high critical chance and critical damage complementing their already high base damage. The Shraksun Scaffold and Granmu Prism's area-of-effect attacks in particular allows them to hit more enemies, allowing for more chances to deal critical hits.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • May appear as a Steel Path Circuit Tier 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7, or 8 reward on a weekly rotational basis.

• May be sold by Acrithis for 20 Pathos Clamp on a daily rotational basis, requiring Drifter Intrinsics Opportunity Rank 9 for the arcanes to appear in her shop.

• May drop from Undercroft portals in the Duviri Experience while on The Steel Path (2.78%).

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Trading Longbow Sharpshot requires the trader to be Mastery Rank 11 or higher.

• Damage bonus is multiplicative to mods like Serration .

• Cannot be equipped on crossbows such as Attica , Nagantaka ( Prime ), and Zhuge ( Prime ).

• As long as every consecutive shot is a headshot, the buff is always active.

• If the buff is not active, the first headshot does not benefit from it, even if the bow has multishot.

• Where Galvanized Aptitude adds with damage mods, Longbow Sharpshot will not multiply GunCO 's bonus portion.
 • Explanation: Galvanized Aptitude does not always add with damage mods like Serration , but with weapon cases where it does add such as Daikyu , Galvanized Aptitude's entire contribution to damage is a recalculation of all bonuses minus +damage, thus making it mathematically additive to that particular bonus. However, this recalculation is outdated and does not include many damage bonuses, one of which is Longbow Sharpshot's multiplying effect. Its additive formula regarding Longbow Sharpshot is as follows:
 • Damage bonus% = [(1 + Serration) × (1 + Longbow Sharpshot)] + Galvanized Aptitude

evidence.excerpt:
Acquisition
[ edit | edit source ]
 • May appear as a Steel Path Circuit Tier 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7, or 8 reward on a weekly rotational basis.

However, this recalculation is outdated and does not include many damage bonuses, one of which is Longbow Sharpshot's multiplying effect. Its additive formula regarding Longbow Sharpshot is as follows:

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be bought from Rude Zuud for 10,000 Standing 10,000 per arcane upon reaching the rank of Old Mate .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Channeled Abilities (e.g., Exalted Blade , Renewal ) that are activated during the Pax Bolt buff will keep the Ability Strength bonus on them for as long as they are kept active, even after Pax Bolt has expired.
 • This does not apply to the Ability Efficiency part of the buff. The bonus Ability Efficiency will be removed and updated the moment that Pax Bolt expires.

• Subsequent Pax Bolt buffs will temporarily reduce the energy drain for any currently active channeled abilities for the duration of Pax Bolt. Whether this is intentional or not is unknown.

• Similar to other headshot kill conditions, the enemy must be killed by a headshot through weapon damage, and not any damage-over-time status effect such as Slash .

• The Ability Strength and Ability Efficiency gained are additive with other mods. For example, a Warframe equipped with maxed Intensify and receives a Rank 3 Pax Bolt buff would have +60% Ability Strength.

• Note that Ability Efficiency still caps out at 175% even with Pax Bolt equipped, though channeled abilities use uncapped values.

• Works oddly with Wisp's Reservoirs ; the bonus strength will apply, but if the player already has a weaker mote active, touching the new buffed mote will simply refresh it rather than change its strength. In order to take full advantage of Pax Bolt, you must wait for your current buffs to expire.

• The Ability Strength and Ability Efficiency gained are additive with other mods. For example, a Warframe equipped with maxed Intensify and receives a Rank 3 Pax Bolt buff would have +60% Ability Strength.

• Note that Ability Efficiency still caps out at 175% even with Pax Bolt equipped, though channeled abilities use uncapped values.

• Works oddly with Wisp's Reservoirs ; the bonus strength will apply, but if the player already has a weaker mote active, touching the new buffed mote will simply refresh it rather than change its strength. In order to take full advantage of Pax Bolt, you must wait for your current buffs to expire.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be bought from Rude Zuud for 10,000 Standing 10,000 per arcane upon reaching the rank of Old Mate .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Recharge rate is not affected by mods or abilities. Recharge rate depends on the chamber part:

 Recharge Rate

 | Chamber
 |
 | Primary |
 | Secondary
 |

 | Catchmoon
 |
 | 5 ammo/sec |
 | 50 ammo/sec
 |

 | Gaze
 |
 | 50 ammo/sec |
 | 50 ammo/sec
 |

 | Rattleguts
 |
 | 50 ammo/sec |
 | 50 ammo/sec
 |

 | Sporelacer
 |
 | 5 ammo/sec |
 | 25 ammo/sec
 |

 | Tombfinger
 |
 | 50 ammo/sec |
 | 50 ammo/sec
 |

 | Vermisplicer
 |
 | 50 ammo/sec |
 | 25 ammo/sec
 |

 • Similar to Archguns and the Cycron , base recharge delay is equal to the reload time displayed on the weapon.
 • Reduced reload time (recharge delay) is shown in the Arsenal upon equipping Pax Charge.

• Recharge delay is further reduced (stacking additively) by reload speed mods like Quickdraw and Stunning Speed .

• Reduces the recharge delay by increasing reload speed. At max rank, it increases the reload speed by 50%, which reduces the recharge delay by 33.3%. See Reload Speed for the details.
 • For example, with a Kitgun that has a reload time of 2.1 seconds:
 • Unranked Pax Charge will result in a recharge delay of 2.1 1 + 12.5 % = 1.87 seconds, a reduction of 11.1%.

• Rank 3 Pax Charge will result in a recharge delay of 2.1 1 + 50 % = 1.4 seconds, a reduction of 33.3%.

• Prevents the weapon from receiving benefit from reload-triggered mods or Arcanes such as Pressurized Magazine or Arcane Awakening .

• Recharge delay is further reduced (stacking additively) by reload speed mods like Quickdraw and Stunning Speed .

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be bought from Rude Zuud for 10,000 Standing 10,000 per arcane upon reaching the rank of Old Mate with Solaris United . Like all arcanes, Pax arcanes come fully built at Rank 0.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • In order to activate the effect of Pax Seeker, the enemy must be headshot killed by the Kitgun, and not any damage-over-time status effect such as Slash .
 • Electricity and Gas status procs can target specific body parts, and as such can trigger Pax Seeker if inflicted by a headshot.
 • The enemy has to die from a headshot status proc , an immediate kill via projectile hit will not trigger the arcane.

• A new enemy walking into an old Gas cloud will not trigger the arcane, because it is no longer a headshot.

• Projectile damage is determined by the following:
 { Gaze = 2 ⋅ [ ( Barrel Base Damage + Grip Damage Bonus ) ⋅ ( 1 + Base Damage Buffs ) ⋅ ( 1 + Multishot Buffs ) ] + Grip Damage Bonus Rattleguts = 2 ⋅ [ ( Barrel Base Damage + Grip Damage Bonus ) ⋅ ( 1 + Base Damage Buffs ) ⋅ ( 1 + Multishot Buffs ) ] + Grip Damage Bonus Tombfinger = 2 ⋅ [ ( Barrel Base Damage ) ⋅ ( 1 + Base Damage Buffs ) ⋅ ( 1 + Multishot Buffs ) ] + Grip Damage Bonus Catchmoon = [ ( Barrel Base Damage ) ⋅ ( 1 + Base Damage Buffs ) ⋅ ( 1 + Multishot Buffs ) ] + Grip Damage Bonus
 • Multishot's damage bonus is calculated in integer multiples. e.g. a +150% multishot bonus will result in a damage multiplier of 2x with a 50% chance to deal 3x damage.

• Elemental damage bonuses are applied after all other bonuses.

• Galvanized Aptitude / Galvanized Shot apply multiplicatively to the final damage.

• Projectiles have identical Critical stats to the kitgun.
 • Projectiles receive double the bonus from Critical Chance and Critical Damage mods.

• Projectile damage benefits from buff abilities, like Rhino 's Roar , Mirage 's Eclipse and Harrow 's Covenant .

• Projectiles have a damage distribution of 50% Slash and 50% Puncture , along with any modded elements.

• Projectiles also aim for the head, and can benefit from enemy Weakpoint damage multipliers.
 • Projectiles generated by Catchmoon (Primary) cannot benefit from headshot bonuses.

• Status chance mods affect the projectiles, which can proc Slash , Puncture , and any status the modded weapon may inflict.

• Each projectile has a forced Impact status effect
 • Can benefit from the Internal Bleeding / Hemorrhage .

• The projectiles will disappear if they hit a wall or ceiling; this combined with their upwards emergence and slow homing can make the projectiles struggle to hit enemies in tight interior spaces.

• Projectiles are unaffected by Punch Through .

• Projectiles can target and damage enemies across the material plane and Rift Plane .

• A projectile can be controlled using Ivara 's Navigator .

• Extra-hit abilities (e.g. Cyte-09 's Resupply , Xaku 's Xata's Whisper ) are detrimental to Pax Seeker.
 • Killing an enemy with the extra hit will not trigger the arcane.

• Extra hit buffs do not apply to the projectiles.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be bought from Rude Zuud for 10,000 Standing 10,000 per arcane upon reaching the rank of Old Mate .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Aim Glide duration bonus is applied after mods like Patagium .

 • For example: 3s x ( 1 + 90% ) + 5s = 10.7 seconds of gliding.

• Has an unlisted effect which will make the player automatically start hovering in the air like Zephyr when firing the kitgun while in the air in the sense that you will still slowly fall to the ground even when you are not aim gliding. This will cause the player to slowly float up a bit then slowly drift down which can be inconvenient.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Explosions have a radial falloff of ~90%

• Explosions have a 20% chance to proc Heat status.

• Residual Arcanes can only have two active puddles at a time. For example; creating two puddles with a primary kitgun will prevent both your primary and secondary kitgun from creating any until the first two expire.

• Will deal heat explosions that do not add or combine with weapon elemental effects, retaining heat damage on explosions.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Status Effects from any source can be affected by this arcane, including statuses applied on the attack that triggered Melee Afflictions .

• Toxin and Electricity mods can increase the damage of the stacks added by Melee Afflictions . Heat mods cannot.

• Faction Damage Bonuses and Status Damage Bonuses can highly increase the damage from Melee Afflictions .
 • These bonuses exponentially scale. Probably a bug.

• When equipped on Exalted weapons , Melee Afflictions :
 • can stack Arcane Impetus

• can stack Arcane Ice Storm

• can trigger Archon Vitality , however Archon Vitality doesn't double but adds only one additional stack (from +6 to +7).

• can trigger Archon Continuity on targets afflicted by toxin (only results in 1 stack of Corrosive ).

• can trigger Archon Stretch on targets afflicted by electric.

• does not trigger Archon Flow by stacking Cold .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Critical Chance bonus is additive to mods like True Steel .

• Only provides 1 stack per melee input, regardless of how many enemies are hit.

• At all ranks, it requires 10 hits to reach the maximum critical chance bonus listed.

• Only consumes stacks if the heavy attack connects with an enemy. Whiffing a heavy attack will preserve the critical chance bonus.

• The bonus only procs on normal melee attacks. Slams and heavy attacks do not.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Melee Crescendo can be triggered by any Finisher be it Melee, Exalted Weapons , Parazon , Ash 's Teleport or Blade Storm .

• On channeled Exalted Weapons (e.g. Excalibur 's Exalted Blade ), Melee Crescendo will only gain stacks while the ability is active. Stacks will persist while deactivated.

• Trading Melee Crescendo requires the trader to be Mastery Rank 11 or higher.

• At max rank, it takes 37 kills for permanent 12x combo (220 / 6 = 36.67).

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Bonus Critical Multiplier is determined by the following:
 r o u n d ( Puncture Status Chance × 0.1 × Critical Multiplier Bonus )
 • Buff is rounded to one decimal place.

• This means that with a maxed Melee Doughty and 114.6% chance of applying a Puncture gives:
 • 114.6 * 0.1 * 1 = +11.5x Critical Multiplier.

• Capped at +50x Critical Multiplier.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Creates a separate damage instance that will reroll the chance to apply status and also the critical tier.
 • It is possible to create a duplicated hit that is either non critical, tier 1 (yellow) or tier 2 (orange).

• The effect can proc on Exodia Contagion , unless the first burst of damage kills the enemy (or one shot it).

• Forced status procs will apply to the duplicated hit if they originate from the melee weapon.
 • Duplicated hit will not apply a forced status from Seeking Talons .

• With rank 5 Melee Duplicate the formula for the most optimal Critical Chance is following:

 Critical Chance = (3 × Critical Damage Multiplier − 4) ÷ (2 × Critical Damage Multiplier − 2)
 • • With a low Critical Damage multiplier, other Arcanes such as Melee Exposure will likely provide more damage than Melee Duplicate.

• This formula isn't entirely accurate for weapons with low total crit damage multipliers (shouldn't ever be relevant but should at least be noted).

• Trading Melee Duplicate requires the trader to be Mastery Rank 11 or higher.

sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Transference nor Operator abilities count as an Ability Cast for triggering this Arcane as Update 37.0 (2024-10-02) undocumented change/fix.

• Bonus stacks share the same duration, which refresh upon each trigger, and all will expire at once when the duration ends.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Armor bonus can stack with multiple kills with each stack having its own duration.

• Melee Fortification can be stacked up to 255 times. However, due to its 10-second duration, one must constantly keep killing to benefit effectively from the Armor buff. For the same reason, it is unlikely for this Arcane to be stacked beyond ~30x for +6300 Armor.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Damage is affected by Condition Overload and critical multiplier .

• Eligible status effects that can be spread by Melee Influence include:
 • Primary Elemental ( Cold , Electricity , Heat , Toxin )

• Secondary Elemental ( Blast , Corrosive , Gas , Magnetic , Radiation , Viral )

• Spread radius extends from the position of the enemy hit.
 • Thrown weapons like Xoris and gunblades like Redeemer greatly benefit from this

• Melee Influence cannot spread the following:
 • Physical ( Impact , Puncture , Slash )

• Void , Tau , Knockdown , Stagger , Ragdoll , Lifted , and Microwave

• Melee Influence only triggers from direct melee strikes. Secondary melee effects such as Magnetic 's breakage effect and Dual Ichor Incarnon 's toxin fields cannot trigger Melee Influence.
 • Melee projectiles passing through Electric Shield , Mutalist Quanta 's alternate fire, or Conductive Sphere can also trigger Melee Influence.
 • Does not work with Glaive (Weapon Type) weapons

• Examples of this are Nepheri , Hate Incarnon Genesis , Ceramic Dagger Incarnon Genesis , or Syam 's projectiles.

• This arcane not only transfers Status Effects to nearby enemies, but also deals damage equal to the amount of the procced elemental damage present on your melee's stat screen.
 • Example: A melee with 100 base damage modded with Shocking Touch ; if Melee Influence procs, it will deal an extra 90 electricity dmg to the primary target.

• Damage spread is based on the amount of matching elemental damage after quantization.

• Performing a finisher with a forced elemental proc will not spread any Finisher damage, as 0% of the damage dealt is made up of the elemental status that was procced.

• Proccing multiple elements at the same time will show the sum of the spread damage.

• Due to the nature of Faction Damage Bonuses , they are applied twice on damage done by Melee Influence and thrice on damaging status procs caused by it.
 • A melee with 100 base damage modded with Shocking Touch and with a 55% Increased Faction Damage Bonus, will deal 294 Hit damage, with a 228 Electricity Proc Damage. If Melee Influence procs, the spread Electricity procs will deal 353 damage.

• Examples: A Skana modded with Pressure Point , Vicious Frost , Virulent Scourge , and Voltaic Strike .
 • A hit that procs Electricity will do 120 × (1 + 1.2) × (0.6) = 158.4 Electricity damage to all enemies in range and apply an Electricity proc dealing 120 × (1 + 1.2) × (1 + 0.6) ÷ 2 = 211.2 Electricity damage per tick.

• A hit that procs Viral will do 120 × (1 + 1.2) × (0.6 + 0.6) = 316.8 Viral damage to all enemies in range and apply a Viral proc.

• A hit that procs Slash will do nothing.

• A hit that procs Electricity and Viral will do 120 × (1 + 1.2) × (0.6) = 158.4 Electricity damage and 120 × (1 + 1.2) × (0.6 + 0.6) = 316.8 Viral damage to all enemies in range, then apply a Viral proc and an Electricity proc dealing 120 × (1 + 1.2) × (1 + 0.6) ÷ 2 = 211.2 Electricity damage per tick.

• It is generally optimal to focus one elemental type as adding additional types makes it less likely for your weapon's highest damage type to trigger.

• The AoE ignores the Rift Plane , affecting all enemies within range regardless of what plane the attack was made from.

• Elemental damage gained through abilities such as Smite Infusion will also be spread by Melee Influence.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Damage bonus is additive to mods like Pressure Point .

• Damage bonus is shown in the buff UI.

• Requires 2800 shields to achieve the max bonus ( 2200 shields with max overshields active ( 1600 for Harrow )).

• Damage bonus only increases after hitting a new multiple of 200 shields or overshields, it does not scale smoothly with shields/overshields.

• Damage bonus is given by the following equation:
 Damage Bonus = 30 % ⋅ ⌊ Current Shields 200 ⌋ + 15 % ⋅ ⌊ Current Overshields 200 ⌋
 • For example, a frame with 700 shields and 300 overshields give a

 30% * floor (700/200) + 15% * floor (300/200) = 105% damage bonus.
 • Max bonus can be more easily achieved using Arcane Expertise .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • There is a 1.5 second delay before the same enemy can generate additional Motes.

• Regenerates energy for Operators, Warframes, and Necramechs .

• Regenerates energy during channeling abilities such as Danse Macabre .

• Motes will float in the air and drift off into a random direction upon spawning. They will despawn after 10 seconds if not picked up.

• Motes are attracted by Vacuum and Fetch .

• Motes are not attracted by vortex abilities, such as Void Snare .

• Motes do not trigger Arcane Energize or Arcane Eruption .

• Motes are not affected by Amber Archon Shard's energy orb effectiveness.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be bought from Vox Solaris (Syndicate) for 10,000 Standing 10,000 upon reaching the rank of Shadow .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The effect will apply a permanent stacking Damage Vulnerability debuff for all Heat damage done to an enemy.
 • Can be applied multiple times with no limit, with each application stacking additively with each other.

• For example, using an Ignis Wraith modded with Serration and Hellfire against an enemy affected with 3 stacks of a max rank Magus Accelerant will deal:

 35 * (1 + 165% ) * (1 + 90% ) * (1 + 3 * 65% ) = 519.8637 damage before any damage reduction or resistances.
 • Has no effect on Eidolons .

evidence.excerpt:

• Exalted Weapons don't benefit from the increased Critical Damage but also don't consume charges.

• Pseudo Exalted Weapons don't benefit from the increased Critical Damage but still consume charges after 5 seconds.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Pulled enemies are moved 5 meters closer to the Warframe.
 • The pull does not ragdoll enemies: they simply "slide" towards the Warframe.

• The pull does not alert enemies.

• Speed of the pull is affected by slowing and speeding effects (e.g. Cold procs or Molecular Prime ), but distance of the pull remains the same.
 • Frozen enemies are considered to not be slowed or sped up.

• Upon successive trigger, pulled enemies will be immediately moved in the new direction and set distance.

• Transference Static does not trigger the arcane's effect.

• This arcane does not affect enemies with Overguard .

• This arcane does not require enemies to be in Line of Sight .

[ edit | edit source ]
 Magus Cloud can be purchased from Onkko for 10,000 Standing 10,000 , requiring Rank 4 - Instrument with The Quills .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The operator's Void Mode ability triggers a buff that increases Void Sling radius.
 • It affects hitbox radius , not dashing distance.

• Increases hitbox radius from 2 meters to 8 meters.

• The width increase stacks additively with other increases.

• Duration can be triggered again with no cooldown.

• The maximum possible radius of Void Sling is 12 meters, achievable by combining Magus Cloud with Sling Stun .

• See Void Sling#Enhancements for related upgrades.

sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be bought from Vox Solaris for 10,000 Standing 10,000 upon reaching the rank of Instrument .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The effect will apply a permanent stacking Damage Vulnerability debuff for all Puncture damage done to an enemy.
 • Can be applied multiple times with no limit, with each application stacking additively with each other.

• For example, using an AX-52 modded with Serration and Piercing Caliber against an enemy affected with 3 stacks of a max rank Magus Destruct will deal:

 40 * (1 + 165% ) * (1 + 120% ) * (1 + 3 * 65% ) = 687.94 damage before any damage reduction or resistances.
 • Has no effect on Eidolons .

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be bought from Vox Solaris (Syndicate) for 10,000 Standing 10,000 upon reaching the rank of Instrument .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Tethered enemies are completely incapacitated: they cannot move nor attack.
 • Unaware enemies that become tethered remain unaware until the effect wears off, similarly to Rhino Stomp .

• Up to 2 tether mines can be active at once. If attempting to create more than 2 mines, the oldest mine created will be removed.

• Can be used with Magus Revert once every 3 seconds to drop tether mines without consuming energy from Void Dashing.

• Elite targets of subsequent tethers (like Demolishers or Acolytes) will "adapt" to the effects, lowering the tether duration from 4 seconds to 1 second, making the Arcane much weaker when used on the same target several times.

• Can be used with Magus Revert once every 3 seconds to drop tether mines without consuming energy from Void Dashing.

• Elite targets of subsequent tethers (like Demolishers or Acolytes) will "adapt" to the effects, lowering the tether duration from 4 seconds to 1 second, making the Arcane much weaker when used on the same target several times.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be bought from Vox Solaris (Syndicate) for 10,000 Standing 10,000 upon reaching the rank of Shadow .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Stacks are additive to each other. E.g. 4 stacks of a rank 5 Magus Melt will give 4 * 30 = 120 % bonus Heat damage.

• All stacks will be consumed at once when Magus Melt's duration expires.

• Void Slinging will refresh Magus Melt's duration even when at the maximum number of stacks.

• The Heat damage from Magus Melt can combine with other elements such as Electricity from Virtuos Surge to deal Radiation damage.

• Has no effect against the spectral form of Thrax Centurion or Thrax Legatus .

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be bought from Vox Solaris (Syndicate) for 10,000 Standing 10,000 upon reaching the rank of Shadow .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • While only Robotic enemies can be overloaded, the following discharge can damage any other enemy in the vicinity.
 • The discharge does not ignore Armor .

• This effect does not stack: one must wait for the discharge to happen to overload the same enemy again.

• Killing an overloaded enemy will trigger the discharge sooner.

• The 3-second stun does not affect enemies with Overguard , but the delayed (or on-death) discharge does. The discharge's damage itself can also damage enemy Overguard properly.

• For Grineer , affects Rollers , Regulators , Seeker Drones , and most variants, including their Narmer variants.

• For Corpus , affects virtually all non-boss Moas , Ospreys , Bursas , Ratels , Raptors , and Raknoids ; as well as Lynx , 002-Er , M-W.a.m ., Ambulas , its Terra counterparts, and Sol & Lockjaw . It does not affect Kyta Raknoids, almost any type of Crewman, or most Corpus bosses, such as Profit-Taker Orb or Exploiter Orb . These also apply to relevant Narmer variants.
 • For Amalgams , Magus Overload affects Moas , Ospreys , Satyrs , Kucumatz and Alkonosts , but not Machinists , Heqets , or the Spectralysts the latter summon. This applies to all variants of these enemies.

• Does not affect any Infested or Techrot enemies.

• For Orokin , affects Corrupted Moas and Orokin Drones. The former interact as expected, but the latter don't consistently follow the "stunned for three seconds, then explode" rule; sometimes Drones will discharge immediately on contact with a Void Sling, bypassing the stunned period entirely, while other times, they will remain stunned without ever discharging until Slung through again.

• For Sentients , affects most non-boss enemies, including Battalysts , Brachiolysts , Conculysts , Vomvalysts , Ortholysts , Symbilysts , and likely more; but does not affect Sentient-affiliated bosses, such as Eidolons or the Ropalolyst .

• For Murmur, affects Culverins , Arcocanids , Voidrigs and Bonewidows, but not any other enemies.

• Overloaded enemies, along with being stunned, will have electricity matching the Operator or Drifter 's chosen energy color coursing through their bodies, as well as emitting electrical particles, until the stun ends and they discharge. Some enemies, such as Orokin Drones, will not have the particles attached to them, floating above them instead.

• For Corpus , affects virtually all non-boss Moas , Ospreys , Bursas , Ratels , Raptors , and Raknoids ; as well as Lynx , 002-Er , M-W.a.m ., Ambulas , its Terra counterparts, and Sol & Lockjaw . It does not affect Kyta Raknoids, almost any type of Crewman, or most Corpus bosses, such as Profit-Taker Orb or Exploiter Orb . These also apply to relevant Narmer variants.
 • For Amalgams , Magus Overload affects Moas , Ospreys , Satyrs , Kucumatz and Alkonosts , but not Machinists , Heqets , or the Spectralysts the latter summon. This applies to all variants of these enemies.

• Does not affect any Infested or Techrot enemies.

• For Orokin , affects Corrupted Moas and Orokin Drones. The former interact as expected, but the latter don't consistently follow the "stunned for three seconds, then explode" rule; sometimes Drones will discharge immediately on contact with a Void Sling, bypassing the stunned period entirely, while other times, they will remain stunned without ever discharging until Slung through again.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be bought from Little Duck (Vox Solaris (Syndicate)) for 10,000 Standing 10,000 upon reaching the rank of Shadow .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Magus Repair heals all Warframes in range, this includes your own and your teammates'.

• Magus Repair heals 5 / 10 / 15 / 20 / 22 / 25% of maximum health per pulse.
 • Pulses have a 1-second cooldown.

• Entering Void Mode triggers a pulse, unless in cooldown. With max-rank Magus Repair, it would take 3 seconds to reach 100% HP from minimum health due to the initial pulse upon entering Void Mode.

• Every successful pulse has a slight green visual effect on affected Warframes and triggers a sound effect. Pulses will not be triggered unless a Warframe is healed.

• The number of discrete pulses needed for a full heal at each rank are as follows: 20 / 10 / 7 / 5 / 5 / 4 .

• Magus Repair is able to heal Excalibur/Umbra during his sentience passive

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • At all ranks, it will take 40 Toxin procs to reach the maximum bonus.

• Stacking Primary Blight requires the Toxin proc to come from the attached primary weapon. Warframe abilities that add Toxin to your weapons, such as Saryn 's Toxic Lash, also work.

• Similar to Primary Frostbite , Primary Blight stacks expire all at once after 12 seconds of no procs, rather than expiring one stack a time.
 • This Arcane is similar to Primary Frostbite, though with bonuses biased more towards critical damage than multishot.

Notes
[ edit | edit source ]
 • At all ranks, it will take 40 Toxin procs to reach the maximum bonus.

• Stacking Primary Blight requires the Toxin proc to come from the attached primary weapon. Warframe abilities that add Toxin to your weapons, such as Saryn 's Toxic Lash, also work.

• Similar to Primary Frostbite , Primary Blight stacks expire all at once after 12 seconds of no procs, rather than expiring one stack a time.
 • This Arcane is similar to Primary Frostbite, though with bonuses biased more towards critical damage than multishot.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Weakspots created by Banshee 's Sonar do not count as weak points.

• All stacks are lost when the buff's duration expires.

• Status Chance bonus is additive to mods like Rifle Aptitude or Shotgun Savvy .

• Multiple individual pellets from a single shot(either innate to the weapon or generated via Multishot ) can build stacks (See #Bugs ).

• Can build stacks when manning Railjack turrets and hitting the weakpoints of enemy crewships .

• Outside of Energized Munitions , this arcane is additive with other sources of Ammo Efficiency. This allows certain primary weapons to have infinite ammo, mainly via Incarnon perks or weapon passives. These include:
 • Void Fuel (+40% Ammo Efficiency at full rank)

• AX-52 hip-fired shots (+60% Ammo Efficiency)

• Felarx with either Ruptured Plentitude (+70% Ammo Efficiency) or Agile Executor (+50% Ammo Efficiency)

• Braton Incarnon Genesis with Daring Reverie (+50% Ammo Efficiency)

• Boltor Incarnon Genesis with Crimson Overture (+80% or +60% Ammo Efficiency depending on the variant)

• Dera Incarnon Genesis with Crimson Overture (+80% Ammo Efficiency)

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The damage bonus stacks additively with other damage mods like Serration .

• When the buff times out, one stack is lost and the buff duration resets.

• Headshot bonus stacks additively with Target Acquired , innate headshot damage bonuses on weapons such as Kuva Chakkhurr , and snipers with bonus headshot damage from scope bonuses.
 • Cernos Prime 's headshot bonus is unique and stacks multiplicatively with Primary Deadhead's headshot bonus.

• Kills from some status procs on the target's head do not count as a headshot kill.
 • Electricity and Gas procs are able to trigger the effect when aimed at the head however, as they are able to land headshots themselves.

• 'Precision' refers to direct hit headshots (not AoE).

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • At every rank, it will take 40 Cold procs to reach the maximum bonus.

• Stacking Primary Frostbite does not require the Cold proc to come from the attached primary weapon. It can come from any of the following, even if the primary weapon is holstered:
 • Warframe abilities such as those from Frost

• Lavos infusing Cold onto his abilities

• Any weapon that can proc Cold, including Sentinel weapons

• Unlike most other weapon Arcanes, Primary Frostbite stacks expire all at once after 12 seconds of no procs, rather than expiring one stack at a time, similar to Primary Blight .

• At Rank 5 with maximum stacks, Primary Frostbite gives the same stat bonus as Vital Sense and Split Chamber combined.

• Fully Frozen enemies (10 stacks of Cold ) cannot receive a new Cold proc until the freeze wears off, meaning that Primary Frostbite will neither add a stack nor refresh its timer when Cold status is applied to such targets.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Causes enemies with weapons to perform a brief weapon jam animation identical to Shooting Gallery for about 4 seconds, slightly shorter or longer depending on the actual animation enemies will play.
 • This animation thus can be "extended" through application of Cold procs or Gloom , enough that by the time the animation ends they can potentially be stunned again.

• Effectively creates a 30 meter diameter zone around the effected enemy where enemies will be stunned.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Only activates upon refilling of the primary weapon magazine.
 • Some reloads replenish their magazine before the reload time is finished, where the arcane may prematurely activate. For example Prisma Gorgon refills at ~4/5 of its reload time, where it may lose about 0.5 seconds of the arcane's duration before it can even fire.

• Cannot activate off of weapons with battery magazines such as Flux Rifle , Bubonico or Kitguns equipped with Pax Charge .

• Can be equipped on Bows , but will not have any effect.

• Activating the Incarnon Form of an Incarnon weapon activates the arcane, but deactivation does not.

• Maximum damage bonus is calculated by the following:
 Damage Bonus = 15 ⋅ 5 ⋅ Max Magazine Size
 • Bonus is additive to base damage such as Serration .

• Some example results are:
 • 33.5% bonus from a 1-round magazine.

• 75% bonus from a 5-round magazine.

• 88.7% bonus from a 7-round magazine, almost matching either Point Blank or Vicious Spread at max rank.
 • Weapon that consume 1 ammo per shot must have at least 0.7 Fire Rate to make use of 10-second buff on all shots in continuous fire. For continuous weapons, which fire 0.5 ammo per shot, at least 1.4 Fire Rate instead.

• 100.6% bonus from a 9-round magazine.

• 150% bonus from a 20-round magazine.

• 167.7% bonus from a 25-round magazine, matching either Serration , Heavy Caliber , or Primed Point Blank at max rank.
 • Weapon that consume 1 ammo per shot must have at least 2.5 Fire Rate to make use of 10-second buff on all shots in continuous fire. For 0.5 ammo per shot weapons, at least 5.0 Fire Rate instead.

• 201.2% bonus from a 36-round magazine.

• 300% bonus from a 80-round magazine, matching Arcane Primary Charger at max rank.
 • Weapon that consume 1 ammo per shot must have at least 8.0 Fire Rate to make use of 10-second buff on all shots in continuous fire. For 0.5 ammo per shot weapons, at least 16.0 Fire Rate instead.

• 361.2% bonus from a 116-round magazine, matching Primary Deadhead , Primary Dexterity , and Primary Merciless at max stacks at max rank.
 • Weapon that consume 1 ammo per shot must have at least 11.6 Fire Rate to make use of 10-second buff on all shots in continuous fire. For 0.5 ammo per shot weapons, at least 23.2 Fire Rate instead.

• 474.3% bonus from a 200-round magazine.

• 530.3% bonus from a 250-round magazine.

• 580.9% bonus from a 300-round magazine.

• Partial reloads of a magazine will only grant the % of missing magazine as % of the full potential bonus of that magazine.
 • For example, reloading only 40 bullets of a 80 magazine (50% of the magazine) will grant 150% bonus damage (50% of the bonus).

• On weapons or mods that can reload while holstered , the buff will constantly reset until the magazine is fully loaded, then the buff can expire normally.

• Can be equipped on 1-round magazines such as Tonkor .

• Mods such as Tactical Reload and Synth Mods will cause this arcane to lose most of its effect if the weapon gets holstered.
 • The holstered reload provides only a weak, rapidly-occurring buff based on the partial reload of a single round. Holstering a weapon that has an active buff will cause that buff to be overwritten at the much lower partial reload value.

Notes
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Only activates upon refilling of the primary weapon magazine.
 • Some reloads replenish their magazine before the reload time is finished, where the arcane may prematurely activate. For example Prisma Gorgon refills at ~4/5 of its reload time, where it may lose about 0.5 seconds of the arcane's duration before it can even fire.

• Cannot activate off of weapons with battery magazines such as Flux Rifle , Bubonico or Kitguns equipped with Pax Charge .

• Can be equipped on Bows , but will not have any effect.

• Activating the Incarnon Form of an Incarnon weapon activates the arcane, but deactivation does not.

• Maximum damage bonus is calculated by the following:
 Damage Bonus = 15 ⋅ 5 ⋅ Max Magazine Size
 • Bonus is additive to base damage such as Serration .

• Some example results are:
 • 33.5% bonus from a 1-round magazine.

• 75% bonus from a 5-round magazine.

• 88.7% bonus from a 7-round magazine, almost matching either Point Blank or Vicious Spread at max rank.

 • Weapon that consume 1 ammo per shot must have at least 8.0 Fire Rate to make use of 10-second buff on all shots in continuous fire. For 0.5 ammo per shot weapons, at least 16.0 Fire Rate instead.

• 361.2% bonus from a 116-round magazine, matching Primary Deadhead , Primary Dexterity , and Primary Merciless at max stacks at max rank.
 • Weapon that consume 1 ammo per shot must have at least 11.6 Fire Rate to make use of 10-second buff on all shots in continuous fire. For 0.5 ammo per shot weapons, at least 23.2 Fire Rate instead.

• 474.3% bonus from a 200-round magazine.

• 530.3% bonus from a 250-round magazine.

• 580.9% bonus from a 300-round magazine.

• Partial reloads of a magazine will only grant the % of missing magazine as % of the full potential bonus of that magazine.
 • For example, reloading only 40 bullets of a 80 magazine (50% of the magazine) will grant 150% bonus damage (50% of the bonus).

• On weapons or mods that can reload while holstered , the buff will constantly reset until the magazine is fully loaded, then the buff can expire normally.

• Can be equipped on 1-round magazines such as Tonkor .

• Mods such as Tactical Reload and Synth Mods will cause this arcane to lose most of its effect if the weapon gets holstered.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Creates an extra instance of damage when proccing a status effect.

• More than 1 status proc on a single hit (>100% Status chance) will properly add extra damage, but will be displayed as a single damage number.

• Not affected by damage, element, physical, Galvanized Shot stacks, Synth Charge , or critical mods and bonuses.

• Affected by faction mods and bonuses once.

• Not affected by Eclipse or Garuda 's passive.

• Triggers on Toxin procs by Toxic Lash .

• Doesn not trigger on Void procs by Xata's Whisper , and Cold maggots of Dual Coda Torxica .

• Affected by Damage Vulnerability like Caliban 's Sentient Wrath , Gara 's Mass Vitrify and Splinter Storm .

• Not affected by weakspots, but is affected by enemy damage reduction. For example, striking a Nox in the face will still have Cascadia Empowered's damage reduced by its body's damage reduction.

• Not affected by weapon falloff such as on shotguns and explosions.

• Can proc off of Zenurik 's Temporal Shot bonus when used with Seeking Talons .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Damage bonus is additive to damage mods like Hornet Strike .

• At all ranks, it will take 40 Heat procs to reach the maximum damage bonus.

• The bonus damage is applied on all damage types present in the secondary, and not just Heat damage.

• Stacking Cascadia Flare does not require the Heat proc to come from the secondary: it can come from Warframe abilities such as Ember 's ability kit and Protea 's Blaze Artillery , or any weapon or companion that can proc Heat - even if the secondary is holstered.
 • This also includes the parkour mod Firewalker , which deals Heat damage with a 200% status chance, meaning that bullet-jumping near enemies will always grant a stack.

• However, Yareli cannot gain stacks from abilities while riding Merulina , only procs from weapons.

• Unlike other weapon Arcanes, Cascadia Flare stacks expire all at once after 10 seconds of no procs, rather than only 1 stack every 10 seconds.

sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Cascadia Overcharge stacks additively with mods like Pistol Gambit and Creeping Bullseye . For example, a Lex Prime with a max rank Pistol Gambit and Cascadia Overcharge would have a 25% × (1 + 120% + 300%) = 130% critical chance on attacks.

• The bonus to critical chance only applies to the secondary or exalted weapon on which it is equipped, not to other weapons or Warframe abilities.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The damage bonus stacks additively with other damage mods like Hornet Strike .

• When the buff times out, one stack is lost and the buff duration resets.

• Headshot bonus stacks additively with similar buffs, such as Prowl .

• Kills from some status procs on the target's head do not count as a headshot kill.
 • Electricity and Gas procs are able to trigger the effect when aimed at the head however, as they are able to land headshots themselves.

• 'Precision' refers to direct hit headshots (not AoE).

Notes
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The damage bonus stacks additively with other damage mods like Hornet Strike .

• When the buff times out, one stack is lost and the buff duration resets.

• Headshot bonus stacks additively with similar buffs, such as Prowl .

• Kills from some status procs on the target's head do not count as a headshot kill.
 • Electricity and Gas procs are able to trigger the effect when aimed at the head however, as they are able to land headshots themselves.

• 'Precision' refers to direct hit headshots (not AoE).

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Encumber will only apply a max of 1 additional status effect per instant of time, similar to a very short cooldown.
 • Multishot and Status Chance values over 100% do not bypass this limit, but more multishot does allow more chances of an Encumber proc until one succeeds.
 • The average Encumber chance per shot can be calculated by: 1 - (1 - 24% × min(statusChance, 1)) ^ pelletCount

• Hitting multiple targets at the same time will only allow up to one proc for only one of the affected targets per instant.
 • Explosions, Hitscan Punch Through ( Twin Kohmak ), Beam Chains ( Kuva Nukor ), Multibeams ( Ocucor , Vermisplicer (Secondary) ) and other simultaneous attacks will only proc up to once on a single target at a time.

• Projectile Multishot ( Detron ) will only proc once per small target because the projectiles typically strike within Encumber's cooldown.

• Delayed hits such as projectile rebounds ( Cyanex ), embed delays ( Kompressa ), and travel time punch-through ( Catchmoon (Primary) ) can proc Encumber multiple times per shot because their hits do not always occur at the same time.

• Forced procs ( Hystrix ) can trigger Encumber.

• As of Update 38.5, Exalted Weapons ( Mesa 's Regulators , Titania 's Dex Pixia , Dante 's Noctua Hildryn 's Balefire Charger ) can equip and trigger Encumber.
 • Procs from Exalted Weapons can activate Arcanes and mods that require status effects from abilities, such as Arcane Impetus and Archon Continuity .

• Random proc is uniformly chosen from the following and is independent from the weapon's damage distribution:
 • Impact

• Puncture

• Slash

• Heat

• Cold

• Electricity

• Toxin

• Blast

• Corrosive

• Magnetic

• Viral

• Gas

• Radiation

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Critical chance is an absolute bonus. For example, a Lato with 7 stacks of Secondary Enervate will have

 10% + (7 × 10% ) = 80% critical chance.
 • Big Critical Hits are any tier above regular Critical Hits that appear when the weapon has more than 100% critical hit chance, with orange and red damage numbers by default.

• Hitscan attacks that hit multiple enemies simultaneously (e.g., because of Multishot or Punch Through ) count as only one hit.
 • Projectile and non-chained Beam attacks that hit multiple enemies simultaneously will count as multiple hits.
 • If a weapon fires multiple projectiles per shot in a delayed fashion, such as the Kompressa and its bubbles, occasionally one shot will register as multiple hits because the game seems to use a grace period where projectiles from the same shot won't register more times.

• Similarly, the delayed explosions also provide wildly inconsistent bonuses due to the fact that the delay varies between 0.6-0.8 seconds, and if the explosions happen to overlap they will register os one hit.

• AoE attacks always count as one hit.

• When used with a Shotgun Sidearm such as the Pyrana , each pellet does not count as a separate damage instance as the pellets are tied to Multishot.

• Hits on an Arctic Eximus 's Snow Globe can build the critical chance bonus without triggering the reset, since the globe has Object -based health that cannot take critical hits. While this can build the critical chance bonus to extreme levels, the burden of preparation still outweighs the benefit as the bonus will eventually reset.

• Damage dealt by expiring Blast procs counts as a hit.
 • Blast proc explosions that occur when reaching maximum stacks or killing the target do not count as a hit.

• The average final crit chance (AFCC) over a large number of shots, with the modded crit chance of the weapon being x% (and including additional bonuses such as Arcane Avenger ), will be:

 A F C C ( x ) = { 102.45 + 0.5 x , x < 110 128.8 − 0.0075 x + 0.00244 x 2 , 110 ≤ x < 140 77.5 + 0.7 x , 140 ≤ x < 150 70 + 0.75 x , 150 ≤ x < 160 62 + 0.8 x , 160 ≤ x < 170 53.5 + 0.85 x , 170 ≤ x < 180 44.5 + 0.9 x , 180 ≤ x < 190 35 + 0.95 x , 190 ≤ x < 200 25 + x , x ≥ 200
 • AFCC values are accurate within a 0.1% up to 140% modded crit chance, and exact from 140% onwards. These results are based on the full Probability Tree Diagram for each integer-valued modded crit chance, with linear regression on the values up to 110% modded crit chance, and polynomial regression (degree 2) from 110% to 140%.

• Both critical chance bonus and reset stacks cannot trigger more than 30 times per second.

 • Multiple big critical hits registered within 1 game tick only count as 1 stack for the reset counter.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Overguard cap is 15,000 at all ranks.

• DoTs will also steal Overguard , AoE DoTs (e.g. Electricity , Gas ) and weapons (e.g. Aegrit ) are great at improving crowd control over Eximus .

• The Overguard steal effect can be " inherited " if the first source of Heat status applied to an enemy was from a secondary with this Arcane active.
 • Extra damage to Overguard is not inheritable and is dynamically applied, so the effect is lost entirely after depleting the Overguard from an enemy.

• Since Overguard 's immunity period is 0.5 seconds, any secondary with a firing rate >2.0 can maintain functional immortality as long as you can keep firing and there's overguard to steal.
 • This can be accomplished with Grimoire and Lethal Torrent by providing a 2.4 Fire Rate with its infinite ammo.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • May appear as a Steel Path Circuit Tier 1, 3, 4, 6, or 7 reward on a weekly rotational basis.

• May be sold by Acrithis for 10 Pathos Clamp s on a daily rotational basis, requiring Drifter Intrinsics Opportunity Rank 9 for the arcanes to appear in her shop.

• May drop from Undercroft portals in the Duviri Experience while on Steel Path.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The values are additive with mods like Pistol Gambit and Target Cracker .

• While the buff is active, swapping to secondary will not override the Outburst buff unless it would provide a higher buff than the active one.
 • For example, if the current buff is +100% (swapping from a 5x combo), the arcane will not override the buff if current combo level is 5x or below when the weapon swap occurs.

• Melee combo is consumed every time the player swaps to a secondary with Outburst.

• The buff does not affect any of the secondary exalted weapons if the arcane is not equipped directly on secondary exalted weapons.

• It is not possible to consume combo count from both a normal and exalted melee simultaneously, only whichever is currently active.

• The Venka Prime 's increased maximum combo count works with this arcane, allowing for a slightly larger buff than is possible with other melee weapons.

• On the other hand, the Dex Nikana 's reduced maximum combo count also works with this arcane, allowing for a slightly weaker buff but much easier to achieve (121 combo count for 220% vs 240 for regular weapons for 240%), especially with initial combo bonus.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • May appear as a Steel Path Circuit Tier 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7, or 8 reward on a weekly rotational basis.

• May be sold by Acrithis for 20 Pathos Clamp on a daily rotational basis, requiring Drifter Intrinsics Opportunity Rank 9 for the Arcanes to appear in her shop.

• May drop from Undercroft portals in the Duviri Experience while on The Steel Path (2.78%).

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Trading Secondary Shiver requires the trader to be Mastery Rank 11 or higher.

• Does not apply to radial damage.

• Despite the wording being specific to an enemy target, only the Shiver-equipped weapon receives benefit.

• Additive to Hornet Strike on most weapons, has reduced effect on some ( Ballistica series charge attacks, Incarnon-modified secondaries) and is multiplicative to Hornet Strike on others ( Cyanex , Catchmoon , Epitaph , Seer series, Sepulcrum , Tenet Spirex ), consistent with other GunCO sources such as Galvanized Shot .
 • Bonus is additive to damage per status type from Galvanized Shot .

[ edit | edit source ]
 • Gives +0.5% damage per point of energy that remains after casting an ability, up to +700% damage at max rank.
 • Repeated casts will stack damage bonus up to the cap.

• Damage bonus is multiplicative to Hornet Strike and similar bonuses.

• Damage bonus applies to all additional pellets from multishot.

• Bonus is consumed on firing the weapon, even if it does not hit an enemy.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Abilities that apply any Status Effect will grant the Ability Strength and Ability Efficiency bonuses.
 • Buff is refreshed upon any damage from abilities.

• Buff stacks for every Status Effect made by Abilities .
 • Statuses from Sickening Pulse do not stack the buff; however, it can refresh the timer.

• Unique "hidden" status effects such as Knockdown do not stack the buff.

• Buff is activated and/or refreshed by the following Companion Mods: Coolant Leak , Synergized Prospectus , Reflex Denial , Null Audit , Arc Coil , and sometimes Manifold Bond .
 • Null Audit will only apply stacks from copied Blitz/Volatile and Jade Light Eximus abilities.

• If Manifold Bond applies a Magnetic status effect and then that enemy's shields are depleted, the resulting Electricity status effect will activate the buff.

• Weapons and mods utilising Syndicate Radial Effects such as Blight , Entropy , Justice , Purity , Sequence and Truth can provide additional stacks.

• The maximum realistic number of stacks for this Arcane is 14 ; This corresponds to an 84% Ability Strength and a 42% Ability Efficiency bonus with a fully-ranked Arcane.
 • The statuses that can be applied by abilities are Impact , Puncture , Slash , Heat , Cold , Electricity , Toxin , Blast , Radiation , Gas , Corrosive , Viral , Magnetic , and Void or Tau .
 • Void status effects are only possible with an Exalted Weapon and Xata's Whisper .

• Tau status effects are mainly exclusive to Caliban ; note that Sentient Wrath does not apply Tau status if infused into another Warframe.

• In the Deepmines , it is also possible to apply Tau status effects with non-melee exalted weapons by harvesting a Vomval Trumpet , which only spawns in the Critter Liberation bounty.

• Exalted Secondary weapons ( Dex Pixia , Regulators , among others) can generate additional status effects via Secondary Encumber .

• Without utilizing the Helminth (thus excluding Void ), the maximum number of stacks is 13 stacks for 78% Ability Strength and 39% Ability Efficiency ( Bunraku , Fangs of Raksh , Damage Decoy , or Exalted Secondary weapons with Secondary Encumber alone).

Notes
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Abilities that apply any Status Effect will grant the Ability Strength and Ability Efficiency bonuses.
 • Buff is refreshed upon any damage from abilities.

• Buff stacks for every Status Effect made by Abilities .
 • Statuses from Sickening Pulse do not stack the buff; however, it can refresh the timer.

• Unique "hidden" status effects such as Knockdown do not stack the buff.

• Buff is activated and/or refreshed by the following Companion Mods: Coolant Leak , Synergized Prospectus , Reflex Denial , Null Audit , Arc Coil , and sometimes Manifold Bond .
 • Null Audit will only apply stacks from copied Blitz/Volatile and Jade Light Eximus abilities.

• If Manifold Bond applies a Magnetic status effect and then that enemy's shields are depleted, the resulting Electricity status effect will activate the buff.

• Weapons and mods utilising Syndicate Radial Effects such as Blight , Entropy , Justice , Purity , Sequence and Truth can provide additional stacks.

• The maximum realistic number of stacks for this Arcane is 14 ; This corresponds to an 84% Ability Strength and a 42% Ability Efficiency bonus with a fully-ranked Arcane.
 • The statuses that can be applied by abilities are Impact , Puncture , Slash , Heat , Cold , Electricity , Toxin , Blast , Radiation , Gas , Corrosive , Viral , Magnetic , and Void or Tau .
 • Void status effects are only possible with an Exalted Weapon and Xata's Whisper .

• Tau status effects are mainly exclusive to Caliban ; note that Sentient Wrath does not apply Tau status if infused into another Warframe.

• In the Deepmines , it is also possible to apply Tau status effects with non-melee exalted weapons by harvesting a Vomval Trumpet , which only spawns in the Critter Liberation bounty.

• Exalted Secondary weapons ( Dex Pixia , Regulators , among others) can generate additional status effects via Secondary Encumber .

• Without utilizing the Helminth (thus excluding Void ), the maximum number of stacks is 13 stacks for 78% Ability Strength and 39% Ability Efficiency ( Bunraku , Fangs of Raksh , Damage Decoy , or Exalted Secondary weapons with Secondary Encumber alone).

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • At rank 5, it will still take 250 kills to reach the maximum of 60% Ability Strength bonus.
 • Kills must be direct kills , and not kill assists, to stack up the bonus.

• Kills from all sources, not just Warframe abilities, count in raising the stacks
 • This includes kills while in Archwing mode and with Railjack turrets and abilities, though they do not seem to benefit from it.

• Companion kills are included.

• Mercy kills and kills as Operator or in a Necramech will not add to the stack.

• Unlike most stackable Arcane buffs, Molt Augmented's bonus persists until death .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Duration bonus is additive to Ability Duration mods.

• When all shields are lost, any accumulated bonus is lost.
 • Once shields begin replenishing, the duration bonus starts accumulating again.

• Naturally, Nidus , Inaros , and Kullervo do not benefit from this Arcane as they do not deploy Shields.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Energy converted to Health only translates on initial cast of a Warframe ability. It does not convert consumed Energy per second by toggled abilities such as Peacemaker and Gloom .
 • Lavos and Hildryn cannot trigger this Arcane due to not spending Energy.

• Ally heal also applies to self, effectively doubling the base self-heal amount.

• Does not affect allied NPCs.

• Does not trigger Archon Intensify 's secondary effect.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Activates using Residual Arcanes.
 • Stacks are gained on time with the area's damage ticks, and multiple areas increase the rate of stacking.

• Different elemental Residual areas will result in separate stacks.

• Orbiting globes resemble Nova 's Null Star particles with trail colors matching the Residual's element.

• Multiple globe stack additively.

• Globe damage is considered ability damage, triggering Arcane Impetus and the respective Archon Mods .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Activates using Residual Arcanes.
 • Stacks are gained on time with the area's damage ticks, and multiple areas increase the rate of stacking.

• Different elemental Residual areas will result in separate stacks.

• Full damage bonuses at ranks 0 / 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5 are 30% / 60% / 90% / 120% / 150% / 180% respectively.

• Damage bonus is additive with elemental mods with the element of the paired Residual Arcane, and combines with modded elements.
 • For example, a Battacor with fully ranked Serration , Cryo Rounds and fully ranked Theorem Demulcent with full stacks from a Residual Viremia deals:
 • 66 × (1 + 165% ) × (1 + 90% + 180% ) = 647.13

• Cryo Rounds Cold combines with Residual Viremia 's Toxin to make Viral .

• Damage bonus is additive with elemental mods with the element of the paired Residual Arcane, and combines with modded elements.
 • For example, a Battacor with fully ranked Serration , Cryo Rounds and fully ranked Theorem Demulcent with full stacks from a Residual Viremia deals:
 • 66 × (1 + 165% ) × (1 + 90% + 180% ) = 647.13

• Cryo Rounds Cold combines with Residual Viremia 's Toxin to make Viral .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 It's taking longer than I calculated. This page is actively being worked on and may not be completely correct. Please assist in making this page accurate by adding more info . For guidance on performing research, see WARFRAME Wiki:Research . More research is needed
 • Activated by standing in the AoE zones created by Residual Arcanes .
 • A stack is gained once per second while standing in a zone, in time with the zone's damage ticks.

• Standing in multiple zones at once will grant multiple stacks per second. Only 2 Residual zones may be active at a time, even when using both a primary and secondary kitgun, each with a different Residual arcane equipped.

• Different elemental Residual areas will result in separate buffs, with each of the 4 possible buffs maxing out at 15 stacks.
 • Only one buff will have its buff icon shown in the UI at a time (whichever one most recently gained a new stack), but the other buffs will still be active and last their normal duration.

• The buff grants elemental damage matching the elemental type of the Residual arcane used.
 • Bonus stacks additively with elemental bonuses modded onto Companion weapons, as well as other elemental buffs that can be applied to allied units, such as Fireball Frenzy .

• The buffs from two different Residual arcanes can be used to create combined elements.

• Duration will stop refreshing once 15 stacks have been reached.
 • Duration will only appear to stop refreshing. The damage bonus itself will remain present so long as the player continues to chain together residual zones within the arcane's duration, even after it has appeared to fall off the buff tracker.
 • Until the player allows the now untracked buff to expire, the icon tracking it on the buff bar cannot be reacquired.

• Nekros 's Shadows of the Dead retain buffs long after application, without the need to snapshot the buffs on spawn.

• Nekros 's Shadows of the Dead Eximus units' abilities can be buffed and will even inflict status procs.

• The following are counted as summons:
 • Atlas 's Rumblers

• Caliban 's Lethal Progeny

• Chroma 's Effigy

• Dante 's Paragrimms from Pageflight

• Equinox 's Duality

• Inaros ' Swarm Kavats created by Desiccation's Curse and Scarab Swarm

• Khora 's Venari

• Loki 's Decoy

• Nekros 's Shadows of the Dead

• Oraxia 's Scuttlers from Widow's Brood

• Titania 's Razorflies from Razorwing

• Wukong 's Celestial Twin

• The following do not count as summons:
 • Enemies affected by Radiation

• Ash 's Shadow Clones from Blade Storm

• Dante 's Noctua from Wordwarden

• Excalibur Umbra 's Passive

• Mirage 's Hall of Mirrors

• Nidus 's Maggots from Ravenous

• Nyx 's Mind Control

• Octavia 's Mallet

• Protea 's Blaze Artillery

• Revenant 's Enthrall

• Sevagoth 's Exalted Shadow

• Vauban 's Tesla Nervos and Minelayer

• Xaku 's The Lost: Accuse

• Yareli 's Loyal Merulina

• Companion 's Duplex Bond

• Hound 's Diversified Denial

• Helminth Charger 's Cysts from the Strain Mod Set

• Smeeta Kavat 's Mischief

• Ballistica Prime 's ghosts from its on-kill effect

• Synoid Heliocor 's specters from its heavy attack kill effect

• On Call Crew

• Specters

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be bought from Hok for 10,000 Standing 10,000 per arcane upon reaching the rank of Kin with the Ostrons .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Grants a buff that regenerates energy over time
 • The killing blow must be from the weapon's direct damage; status kills will not trigger the buff.

• Casting an ability will stop the energy generation for 1 second.
 • Channeled Abilities that drain energy over time, such as Immolation , Sound Quake , and Renewal will completely prevent energy generation for as long as they are active.

• Stacks up to three times for 15 energy/sec, for a total of 60 energy over 4 sec.

• (bug?) Obtaining the 2nd and 3rd stacks refreshes the buff, but killing an enemy while the buff is active at max stacks (x3) does not refresh the timer.

• (bug?) Obtaining the 2nd and 3rd stacks refreshes the buff, but killing an enemy while the buff is active at max stacks (x3) does not refresh the timer.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Exodia Epidemic is obtainable from limited-time events:
 • Operation: Plague Star sold Exodia Epidemic from Nakak for 5,000 Standing 5,000 + 1,500 Credits 1,500 , requiring Rank 2 - Defender with Operational Supply .

• The October 2020, 2022 and 2024 Nights of Naberus event sold Exodia Epidemic from Daughter for 50 Mother Tokens each.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Perform a bullet jump ( Ctrl   + Spacebar  ) or double jump ( Spacebar   x2) then perform a ground slam (look downward and press E  ) to create a wave of energy that applies the Rhino Stomp effect to enemies.
 • Enemies affected by this effect take increased damage from Rhino Charge but will not restore overguard while Reinforcing Stomp is equipped.

• This effect cannot activate the effects of the Saxum Set mods or Melee Afflictions .

• This effect does not add damage to weapons via Condition Overload (Mechanic) .

• Unaffected by Ability Strength , Archon Shard ability damage bonuses,

• The wave is ~ 5 meters wide, and will travel forward for ~ 31 meters, going over terrain elevations and passing through enemies, dealing 10 Blast Damage, before exploding at the end and dealing Viral damage in a ~ 7 meters radius. Enemies hit by the wave will tumble backward and be suspended in the air similarly to Rhino Stomp for a short time period, up to 4 seconds with a full set.
 • This wave has 0% status chance, 0% crit chance and 1x crit multiplier and is unaffected by most modded Damage increases.
 • Multiplicative damage scaling when Condition Overload (Mechanic) and Universal Damage Bonuses are combined.

• Flat bonuses like Proton Snap , Wrathful Advance , Arcane Avenger , and Arcane Crepuscular apply normally.

• Damage does NOT scale with Combo count.

• The explosion is treated as a melee attack dealing ~30% damage distributed as 100% Viral damage that copies your Zaw's, Crit Chance, Crit Damage and Status Chance.
 • Modded damage, status chance, crit chance, crit damage all apply appropriately.

• Damage does NOT scale with Combo count or Condition Overload (Mechanic) .

• Elemental mods such as Shocking Touch and Proton Snap have no effect and cannot inflict their status effects.

• Damage scales additively with Universal Damage Bonuses .

• Explosion can trigger Melee Duplicate and Melee Influence but will not add combo count or trigger Melee Animosity .

• Trading Exodia Epidemic requires the trader to be Mastery Rank 11 or higher.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be bought from Hok for 10,000 Standing 10,000 per arcane upon reaching the rank of Kin with the Ostrons .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The radius appears from your warframe, not your weapon.

• Damage has a 100% falloff scaling across 6m.

• The area of effect damage has 1.0 follow through.

• Affected by the combo counter, Stance damage multipliers, base damage, elemental damage and faction damage mods.
 • Follows the same scaling from combo counter as pseudo-exalted abilities like Blade Storm , Landslide , Shattered Lash , Slash Dash and Whipclaw : 1 + (combo level - 1) / 4

• Not affected by most other melee mods such as Condition Overload , and Reach .

• Not affected by the melee stealth multiplier .

• Scoring a critical hit with a weapon will not affect Exodia Force's damage.

• Unable to produce critical hits or status effects, even with flat bonuses such as those from Arcane Avenger , Cat's Eye or Covenant .

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Can be bought from Hok for 10,000 Standing 10,000 per arcane upon reaching the rank of Kin with the Ostrons .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Enemies pulled in by the effect, assuming they are not already under some kind of animation lock, are staggered and will be opened up to finishers.

• Rather than the slam itself being the trigger for pulling in enemies, the slam appears to activate an invisible buff which will, in turn, pull in and stagger enemies that enter into range, even as the player moves. The buff appears to last for roughly 1-2 seconds from testing.

• The effect is treated as an ability, which means that it will bypass Limbo 's Rift Plane mechanic and will not affect targets affected by a nullification effect.

• Combos which include slam attacks can trigger Exodia Hunt.

• Applies an implosion graphical effect in bright orange and green on targets affected by the pull.
 • Implosion effect colors might be modifiable, however this seems buggy.

 • While the buff is active, only melee damage will siphon health. This includes damage over time status effects such as Slash , Toxin , Gas , and Heat status effects.

• Ground finishers will not grant life steal buff.

• The initial finisher that triggers the buff will only grant healing if the player is the host.

• Synergizes well with Dizzying Rounds as close range finishers will be guaranteed after hitting an enemy with the augment-modded Bronco / Bronco Prime .

sections.title: Notes

[ edit | edit source ]
 • For any non-integer magazine values the magazine size is simply rounded to the nearest whole number. For example, a Dual Decurion modded with a max rank Magazine Extension will have a calculated magazine size of 32 × (1 + 60%) = 51.2 , which will simply round to 51 .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • All Archguns use their entire ammunition reserve as the magazine that regenerates over time, thus acting as both an increase in magazine capacity as well as maximum ammunition capacity.

• Does not increase reload rate. Due to this, unlike magazine capacity mods for non-Archwing weapons, Magazine Extension does not increase sustained DPS.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 The mod can be bought from The Business for 20,000 Standing 20,000 after reaching Rank 3 - Doer with Solaris United .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Can be triggered by damage dealt with Void Sling .

• The added Void damage to the Companion's weapon allows Manifold Bond to proc Void status on their Precept mods.

• Stacks with other sources of amp ammo efficiency, such as the amp arcane Eternal Logistics . Combining the two allows the amp to gain 100% ammo efficiency, granting it infinite ammo.

• Unlike Xata's Whisper it allows companions to deal damage to Eidolon shields and kill Thrax Centurion 's and Thrax Legatus ' spectral forms.

evidence.excerpt:

[ edit | edit source ]

• The added Void damage to the Companion's weapon allows Manifold Bond to proc Void status on their Precept mods.

• Stacks with other sources of amp ammo efficiency, such as the amp arcane Eternal Logistics . Combining the two allows the amp to gain 100% ammo efficiency, granting it infinite ammo.

• Unlike Xata's Whisper it allows companions to deal damage to Eidolon shields and kill Thrax Centurion 's and Thrax Legatus ' spectral forms.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Blast Shield is automatically acquired upon obtaining a MOA built with the Nychus Model .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The MOA's base Overshields are 600 , resulting in a total Overshields capacity of 3600 at max rank.

• Shields (including Overshields) are fully replenished on ability use.
 • Synergizes with Link Redirection and/or high-shield Warframes like Hildryn .

• Shield refill occurs even when mission modifiers (such as Nightmare or Sortie ) disable them.

• The shockwave knocks down any enemies that make contact with it.
 • The primary target is not knocked down.

• The shockwave deals twice the damage of the initial hit.

• Like the other Nychus-Model Mod ( Hard Engage ), Blast Shield is NOT affected by MOA's equipped weapon.
 • Unlike Hard Engage, Blast Shield is NOT affected by the equipped weapon's mods.

• The weapon itself is still useful for ranged attacks when neither mod's ability is being used.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Calculated Shot is automatically acquired upon obtaining Diriga .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Range indicates the maximum distance the Diriga attacks a target, however it will keep firing on the engaged target a few meters beyond maximum distance until it disengages.

• Keep in mind that Diriga will use this precept regardless of the weapon equipped , which has interesting consequences with certain weapons;
 • If used in tandem with weapons whose projectiles have considerable travel time, the Diriga will not switch targets until after the burst has finished or the weapon has finished its cycle.

• The Sweeper ( Prime )'s low accuracy and damage falloff are not taken into account, so the Diriga will barely damage its target, if at all. The bad scatter of the Deth Machine Rifle will make it have the same issue.

• Using the Burst Laser ( Prisma ) will result in the weapon firing in heavily skewed angles or where moving enemies were when first engaging with them.

• Even with the Diriga's signature Vulklok , given the projectile's travel time the player will likely need to have Terminal Velocity equipped in order to have a good chance at hitting moving targets beyond 40m.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Cat's Eye is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Adarza Kavat .

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Despite its description, Cat's Eye adds 60 percentage points to a weapon's critical chance after other bonuses, similar to Arcane Avenger . This type of buff is commonly referred to as an absolute or final bonus.

• Cat's Eye stacks additively with other percentage point bonuses, such as Arcane Avenger . For example, an Atterax with a max rank True Steel , Blood Rush at a 4.0x combo multiplier, Cat's Eye , and Arcane Avenger will have a 25% × (1 + 120% + 40%×(4 - 1)) + 60% + 45% = 190% critical chance.
 • Highly effective with weapons with high critical damage multipliers.

• Emplaced turrets (i.e. the Grineer Rampart ) gain the ability to critically strike while under the effect of Cat's Eye.
 • While this also applies to weapons that cannot critically strike, these weapons often have a critical multiplier of 1.0x which yields no change in damage dealt.

• Multiple instances of Cat's Eye do not stack.

• The ability triggers when an enemy is within at least 25 meters.

• The cooldown of 20 seconds starts when Cat's Eye's duration stops. This leads to an effective uptime of 10 ÷ (10 + 20) = 33.33% .
 • The effective uptime with Tek Enhance will be (10 × (1+30%)) ÷ (10 × (1+30%) + 20) = 39.39% .

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Charm is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Smeeta Kavat .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Each instance of a buff has their own timer and are stackable; they do not overwrite each other. However, the HUD only shows the timer for the latest instance of a buff.

• Every 27s there is a 40% chance to receive a random buff out of 6 buffs, getting a Triple Affinity Buff out of the 6 buff pool is 0.4*0.1 = 4% , getting the buff twice in a row is (4%) 2 = 0.16% , the duration of each buff is 120s which means at most there can only be 5 possible instances active at the same time. The chance of that is happening is (4%) 5 = 1.024×10 −5 % .
 • Buffs durations can be extended using Tek Enhance .
 • With Tek Enhance each duration can increase to 156s, meaning 6 instances are possible. The chance of this happening is (4%) 6 = 4.1×10 −7 % .

• Keeping in mind that all Affinity buffs are multiplicative, stacking an Affinity Booster(x2), an Affinity Blessing(+25% base Affinity gain), during an Affinity Weekend Booster(x2) adds up to 500% Affinity gain. Adding in the triple Affinity buff from Charm, it will stack respectively to 1,500% Affinity on one buff, 4,500% on two, 13,500% on three, 40,500% on four, 121,500% on five, and an immense 364,500% on 6 consecutive buffs. For reference, a Heavy Gunner worth 5,000 Affinity regularly would award an astounding 18,225,000 Affinity on kill.

• The Reinforced Shields buff can trigger on Warframes without Shields , but will not grant Overshields to them. It can also trigger when at maximum Overshields on other Warframes.

• The Energy refund buff can trigger on Warframes without Energy .

• The Critical Chance Bonus sets minimum Critical Chance on weapons to 200% . Weapons that already possess critical chance above this value remain unaffected.
 • When using the Vigilante Mod Set it is possible to get red crits.

• The Instant Reload buff can still be rolled even if the player unequips all weapons from their loadout.

• The Charm buff can proc while still in the loading screen and the audible sound the Smeeta makes when it procs Charm can be heard as well.
