sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Heals the user the stated amount for each status effect currently afflicting the target. For example, with a Rank 10 Healing Return (+11), attacking an enemy afflicted with Slash , Puncture , and Toxin statuses will heal 11×3 health, a total of 33 health.
 • Healing Return can be used to much avail on certain low status weapons as well:
 • High Impact damage weapons like Heliocor or Shaku will trigger Healing return with every attack, regardless of an Impact proc actually occurring.

• Certain weapons (such as dual daggers equipped with Gnashing Payara , or Gram even without a stance) trigger a "flinch" effect on enemies with every hit, which triggers Healing Return.

• Note that many Stances have at least one combo that has a force proc.

• Multiple instances of the same status effect (e.g. 4 individual Slash DoTs) will not increase the healing given.

• Both the Redeemer and Sarpa 's charge attacks will heal the user. Each individual pellet triggers an instance of healing (e.g., if 5 pellets hit an enemy afflicted with a status effect, the healing amount will be 5×11 = 55HP). Because both gunblades' charged attacks are guaranteed to proc an Impact effect, they are guaranteed to heal the user if the Healing Return mod is equipped.

• If equipped on the Deconstructor ( Prime ), this mod will allow Helios ( Prime ) to heal itself provided its target is suffering status effects.

• Does not provide any healing if your attack, despite proccing a Status Effect on the attack (for example, with 100% status chance ), kills the target instantly on the first attack.

• The AoE damage from the explosion of Exodia Contagion 's projectile will not trigger the healing effect of this mod.

• The healing effect is triggered for each enemy hit by an attack independently, so attacking a squad of enemies all with Status effects with a long Reach weapon can give significant healing over time.

• Does work with Hound weapons like the Akaten .

• Does not trigger Archon Intensify when used on exalted weapons such as Excalibur 's Exalted Blade .

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The mod's effects are only visible on Status Effects with an associated duration, either increasing the duration of the associated debuff ( Corrosive , Puncture , Cold , Magnetic , Radiation , and Viral ), or the number of ticks associated with DoT effects ( Slash , Toxin , Gas , and Heat ).
 • This mod will not affect the duration of crowd control effects generated by Status Effects (staggers from Impact , the panic duration of Heat , and the stun animation caused by Electricity ).

• However, it does affect the duration of the freeze effect applied by Cold after reaching 10 stacks.

• Increased Status Duration will slow down the Armor Strip effect of Heat . For example, with +100% status duration, the armor strip will occur every second instead of every half second. As a result, the full 50% armor strip will only be achieved after 4 seconds.

• Status Effects from the radial blasts of Syndicate Weapon Augments and Syndicate Weapons are not affected by this mod.

• Due to status duration always rounding down, there is no difference in performance between a 9th rank and 10th rank version of this mod without other sources of status duration (such as Saryn or Lavos).

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod can be installed on Exalted Melee Weapons and works on any exalted weapons.

• Any damage over time effects from Heavy Attacks will not benefit from Life Strike.

• Life Strike works on Gunblade 's heavy attack bullets, allowing lifesteal over a distance.

• Life Strike works on Glaive weapons, but only on manually-detonated explosions and heavy attack ground slams.
 • Explosions from Volatile Quick Return or Volatile Rebound do not work.

• While Life Strike can be installed on the Deconstructor ( Prime ), Helios ( Prime ) cannot manually detonate its weapon let alone perform any heavy ground slams and thus cannot gain any benefit.

• Does not trigger Archon Intensify when used on exalted weapons such as Excalibur 's Exalted Blade .

[ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Damage bonus is multiplicative to the one provided by Pressure Point and other Melee Damage buffs.
 • Damage bonus is additive with many Universal Weapon Bonuses as it gives Weapon Damage, not Melee Damage.

• Punch through applies to bodies only, but not surfaces of any kind (e.g. it cannot pass through a Shield Lancer 's shield).

• Consecutive throw bonus refers to "buffering" by holding the melee button before the Glaive is caught, otherwise the buff will be lost. In addition to the Power Throw buff combo counter, the throw animation will also change; the base throw is an open-handed forward throw, while consecutive throws alternate between a backspin throw and a side-flip under throw.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • At maximum rank, Relentless Combination increases the Melee Combo Counter by +1 for every Slash proc inflicted per enemy. A combo hit is applied for each application of Slash and additional +1 hits thereafter for every tick of Bleed damage.

• If the enemy dies while under the Bleed proc or Bleed runs out, you will stop gaining additional hits from that enemy.

• Only Slash procs caused by melee weapons count; Slash procs from primary or secondary weapons, Warframe abilities (including abilities that are affected by melee weapon mods or the Combo Counter, such as Shattered Lash or Blade Storm ), or Rending Turn will not increase the Combo Counter.
 • Exalted Blade , Diwata , Iron Staff , Valkyr Talons , and Garuda Talons can equip the mod and benefit from it.

• Slash procs from Exalted Blade 's energy waves, including ones cast from Slash Dash while Exalted Blade is active, add hits to the combo counter.

• Slash procs from thrown Glaives also add hits, even though the combo counter does not interact with Glaives.

• Note that several melee weapons are incapable of inflicting Bleed due to not dealing any Slash damage, but can still equip the mod for no benefits. This can be partially rectified by using Stance combos with forced Slash procs.

• Benefits from "Additional Combo Count Chance" mods such as True Punishment and Quickening .

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • These attacks do not gain a stealth damage bonus while invisible and striking from a distance. It is unknown if this is intended.

• These attacks can be used to teleport to enemy corpses, given that they are still visible.

• Heavy attacks executed on unalerted enemies backs will cause you to do a back finisher attack.
 • However heavy attacks on other parts of the enemy while they are unalerted will not cause any type of finisher attack.

• The mod suggests that the Rift Plane is being used to achieve the instantaneous travel to an enemy's location, however in practical terms it is strictly a teleportation mod.
 • The user is not granted any Rift effects or visual indicators for any part of the heavy attack.

• The teleport works similarly to Amar's Set , but does not stack with it.

• Reach does not increase the teleportation range.

• Can be used in conjunction with Nyx's Assimilate augment to provide an alternative form of mobility.

• Teleporting using this mod may result in getting stuck, though this mod may also be used to get free again.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Due to the nature of in-game Damage Quantization , some melee weapons have such a low base Impact value that it is rounded to 0 damage, causing Shattering Impact to not apply. However, for the examples below, each weapon coincidentally deals pure Impact on slam hits, allowing certain attacks to reduce armor anyways.
 • Destreza

• Galatine

• Galatine Prime

• Shattering Impact takes effect on every melee hit from weapons with any Impact damage. It has no effect if the melee weapon's Impact damage is zero (for example ranged attacks of Redeemer ). Note that it is not necessary to proc the Impact status effect to trigger the armor reduction.

• The amount of armor reduction per hit is a flat, constant value, independent of the amount of Impact damage inflicted. Since the reduction is applied to the target's base armor, for a given enemy type it will always take the same number of hits to completely drain the enemy's armor, regardless of its level.

• Shattering Impact is applied before percentage-based base armor strips, such as Corrosive Projection , and so the number of shots required to fully strip will be unaffected.

• Compared to Corrosive status effect, Shattering Impact has the ability to reduce a target's armor to 0 permanently, instead of just by 80% for the duration of the status effect. Corrosive, however, only needs 10 stacks of the status effect to achieve this, while Shattering Impact from weapons (other than certain Gunblades which are able to strip armor very quickly - in 2-4 ranged shots) may require a substantially higher amount of hits for the same effect on enemies with high base armor.

• Shattering Impact is not considered a status effect and hence is able to affect status effect-immune enemies, such as bosses.

• Shattering Impact does not affect Archons or Commandeered Primes .

• As of Hotfix 18.1.4 (2015-12-21) Shattering Impact doesn't reduce enemy armor when used with the charge attacks of thrown weapons. The charge attack from a Blade and Whip type weapon will however reduce enemy armor.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • With this mod equipped, all enemies within 4 meters of the player will be dealt damage when heavy attacking.
 • Deals a 1 damage Electricity proc at the start of the windup animation.

• Damage depends on which heavy attack is used to start.
 • Starting with heavy attack one (the spin) then following up with heavy attack two (the overhead strike) will deal 24% damage hitting every 0.2 seconds while in the first heavy attack animation, then a single hit dealing 800% damage while in the second heavy attack animation.

• Starting with heavy attack two (the overhead strike) then following up heavy attack one (the spin) will deal 80% damage hitting every 0.2 seconds while in the first heavy attack animation, then a single hit dealing 240% damage while in the second heavy attack animation..

• Can trigger Melee Influence without needing to have electricity damage on the weapon. (The only weapons that can equip this augment are base electric damage, regardless)

• Shock range is centered on the player, not the melee.
 • Range cannot be increased.

 • Stacks with Rending Strike and Auger Strike for a maximum increase of 290% Puncture damage.

• Mods that increase Puncture Damage only multiply the base Puncture Damage on any given weapon.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The additional Combo Count Chance seems to universally affect all sources of combo count generation.

• The Combo Duration penalty is calculated from the melee weapon's base value. Meaning it always subtracts exactly 2.5 seconds and therefore is easily counteracted by Combo Duration mods (e.g. Body Count , Swift Momentum , Rising Storm ).

• Pairs well with stances that have high damage multipliers in their combos and each pellet from Gunblade shots.
 • Crushing Ruin 's Winding Temper combo is one attack consisting of 3 hits for 300%, 200%, and 400% damage, respectively, totaling +9 hit combo if the target survives the first two hits.

• The Redeemer shoots 10 pellets with each shot, resulting in +10 hit combo if they all hit a target.

 • Weeping Wounds can further increase the Status Chance depending on the Melee Combo Counter

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Explosions do 50% of Heavy Attack damage, doesn't benefit from the combo count damage multiplier, and does not consume it.

• Explosions from this mod do not inflict guaranteed procs unlike manual detonations. (bug? except the guaranteed impact proc from Glaive Prime .) It can apply regular status proc depending of status chance.

• Enemies that are hit only by the explosion but not the weapon itself do not increase the combo count .

• After hitting and getting the regular explosion from this mod, the glaive can still be manually detonated for a Heavy Attack explosion during the same throw.

• Cannot be equipped on Deconstructor ( Prime ), due to its lack of explosive properties.

• Cannot be equipped with Quick Return , since it is considered a variant of this mod.

• Cannot be equipped with Volatile Rebound or Rebound .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Explosions do 50% of Heavy Attack damage.

• Explosions do not increase the Melee Combo counter; only the thrown weapon's direct hits on enemies count.

• Explosions from this mod do not consume Heavy Attack combo count, nor do they benefit from its damage multiplier.

• Explosion from this mod does not inflict guaranteed procs unlike manual detonations.

• Cannot be equipped on Deconstructor ( Prime ), due to its lack of explosive properties.

• Cannot be equipped with Rebound since it is considered as a variant of this mod.

• Cannot be equipped with Quick Return or Volatile Quick Return

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Explosion has linear damage falloff from 100% to 0% from the central enemy.

• Explosion requires Line of Sight .

• Explosion damage is affected by Faction Damage Mods and stealth 's 8x damage bonus

• Explosion radius cannot be modified outside of increasing Vulcan Blitz's mod rank.

• The explosion is silent , though nearby enemies that survive the explosion will be alerted to your presence.

• The explosion's colors will use the Jat Kittag's energy color, and thus can be modified.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Unlike dual stat mods (e.g. Virulent Scourge ), Weeping Wounds can increase status chance without introducing a new damage type into the mix.

• Weeping Wounds can often be significantly more powerful than a single dual stat mod in increasing pure status chance.

• Weapons with at least 19% base status chance can reach 100% status chance when using Weeping Wounds at a 12x combo multiplier.

sections.title: Drop Locations

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The increase to shield capacity provided by Necramech Redirection is applied after rank bonuses from levelling up the necramech.
 • For example, if a maxed Necramech Redirection is applied to a level 30 Voidrig , the unranked shield capacity (850) is increased by the +200 Base Shield rank bonus, giving 1050. This total is multiplied by 2 (+100% Necramech Redirection shield bonus), giving a grand total of 2100 shield capacity.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • If the Necramech's health is below the threshold when the cooldown finishes, Repair will activate on the next instance of damage to health.

• Does not provide any invulnerability mechanic while restoring health which means you can still be killed by damage sources without the use of defensive cooldowns. Especially noticeable against high level enemies where the damage can exceed the 20% margin or be high enough to negate the healing altogether.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • When Auto Breach triggers, the hacking screen will appear without puzzle and an "Auto Breach" message pops up later.

• Works for consoles in Terminal Hacking Mastery Test at Rank 5 and Data Vaults in Spy missions.

• Failing or canceling a hack will prevent Auto Breach from being used for that terminal again.

• Has a short activation delay of 0.3s, if a Cipher is used instantly after initiating a hack then Auto Breach can't trigger.

• Does not interfere with the Nightwave challenge "Machine Interference".

• Does function on the Razorback Door Console . However, the Razorback Cipher will still be consumed.

evidence.excerpt:
Drop Locations
[ edit | edit source ]

Notes
[ edit | edit source ]
 • When Auto Breach triggers, the hacking screen will appear without puzzle and an "Auto Breach" message pops up later.

• Works for consoles in Terminal Hacking Mastery Test at Rank 5 and Data Vaults in Spy missions.

• Failing or canceling a hack will prevent Auto Breach from being used for that terminal again.

• Has a short activation delay of 0.3s, if a Cipher is used instantly after initiating a hack then Auto Breach can't trigger.

• Does not interfere with the Nightwave challenge "Machine Interference".

• Does function on the Razorback Door Console . However, the Razorback Cipher will still be consumed.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • It only replenishes the magazine of the weapon and not the reserve ammo pool.

• Unlike Blood Forge , the magazine will not draw ammo from the reserve ammo pool upon performing a Mercy kill, so it essentially grants additional ammo.

• It does not work on Heavy weapons summoned by the Archgun Deployer .

• It does not work on the Incarnon Transmutation gauge.

Acquisition

 • This mod was originally awarded to players who participated in Operation Sling-Stone .

Drop Locations
[ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Has no effect on Corrupted and Narmer Corpus (e.g. Corrupted Crewman , Narmer Crewman ).

• Despite being listed as a multiplier, it will stack additively with other sources of Damage to Corpus.
 • For example, if you had a Riven Mod with x0.5 Damage to Corpus and a max rank Bane of Corpus with x1.3 Damage to Corpus, you would have a 1 - 0.5 + 0.3 = x0.8 multiplier for damage against Corpus with both mods equipped.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Biotic Rounds could be obtained by reaching Rank 25 with Nightwave during Nora's Mix Volume 8 .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • At max rank, Biotic Rounds grants +150% Viral damage, +150% Magnetic damage, and +150% status chance. In other words, the bonus shown on the mod is the bonus for each effect, not the total.
 • Viral and Magnetic bonuses are additive to similar mods like Cryo Rounds and Magnetic Capacity .

• Status chance bonus is additive to similar mods like Rifle Aptitude .

• Additional weak point kills will refresh the effect duration.

sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The +120% Critical Damage buff applies additively with other Critical Damage mods. Meaning, a base 2x crit multiplier with maxed Vital Sense , Hammer Shot and Bladed Rounds will become 8.0x.

• Upon killing an enemy, a Critical Multiplier buff icon and timer will appear next to the player's health.

• For projectiles with travel time, this mod is unaffected by whether you were aiming when the shot was fired; the Critical Multiplier increase will apply as long as you are aiming when the projectile hits.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Available for 20 x Cred from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.

• Bursting mass was originally acquired by reaching Rank 18 in Nightwave during Series 1: The Wolf of Saturn Six or reaching Rank 20 during Intermission III .
 • Beginning with Nightwave Nora’s Mix: Vol. 5 , it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Adds a separate damage instance to the orb, dealing the accumulated weapon damage in a 15m radius.
 • The explosion's damage composition matches all of the damage that passed through the orb, allowing for a rainbow of elements.

• Damage is modified by the orb and varies depending on the Mutalist Quanta's multishot. Accumulated damage will be reduced to 66% of the total and Electricity will be added but this will never combine with other elements:
 • +100% Electricity at 1 Multishot

• +366% Electricity at 2 Multishot

• +500% Electricity at 3 Multishot

• +366% Electricity at 4 Multishot (See bugs)

• For example, a Kulstar with a max rank Hornet Strike and a max rank Pathogen Rounds firing through a double-stacked orb will contribute:
 • 200 × (1 + 220% ) × (1 + 90% + 366% ) × 0.66 × 200% =
 845 Impact + 760 Toxin + 3092 Electricity =
 4697 additional damage to the Bursting Mass explosion.

• The explosion does not inflict status effects.

• Faction damage bonuses cannot contribute damage, but faction bonuses on Mutalist Quanta itself will improve the explosion.

• Critical bonus damage is not absorbed and the explosion cannot crit.

• Damage falls off linearly from full 200% damage at the center of the explosion to 0% damage at the 15m edge.

• The orb's standard Toxin explosion and its radius of 8m are unchanged.

• Projectiles created from Warframe abilities can also add damage to the orb.

• Projectiles that deal damage through a separate explosion rather than contact with the projectile itself, such as Talons and Antimatter Drop , will not contribute any damage unless they are detonated within the orb.

• Bursting Mass imposes a placement limit for orbs, permitting only one orb at any time. Extra orbs created with multishot over 3 will immediately detonate.
 • Deploying a new orb will detonate the existing orb, allowing manual detonation.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Catalyzer Link is additive with similar mods. For example, a Kuva Karak with a max rank Catalyzer Link and a max rank Galvanized Aptitude will have a 31% × (1 + 60% + 80%) = 74.4% status chance.

• While a secondary or melee weapon is equipped, Catalyzer Link's buff icon still triggers when an ability is casted, this appears to be a lingering effect to reap the benefits of the mod when you switch to your primary, as status chance does not seem to increase for secondary and melee weapons.

Drop Locations
[ edit | edit source ]

Notes
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Catalyzer Link is additive with similar mods. For example, a Kuva Karak with a max rank Catalyzer Link and a max rank Galvanized Aptitude will have a 31% × (1 + 60% + 80%) = 74.4% status chance.

• While a secondary or melee weapon is equipped, Catalyzer Link's buff icon still triggers when an ability is casted, this appears to be a lingering effect to reap the benefits of the mod when you switch to your primary, as status chance does not seem to increase for secondary and melee weapons.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Charged Chamber is multiplicative with other damage mods. For example, a Vectis installed with a max ranked Charged Chamber and Serration will deal 225 × (1 + 165%) × (1 + 40%) = 834.75 damage.

• Stacks additively with Primed Chamber for up to 140% bonus damage.

• Charged Chamber does not work with Sentinel weapons (you can fit it but there is no bonus damage).

• If used with Ammo Efficiency bonuses, it will provide bonus damage to a shot as long as the magazine counter is at Max Magazine − 1 after a shot is fired.
 • For example, a Vulkar with Energized Munitions will have the 4th, 5th, 6th, and 7th shots buffed by Charged Chamber as the magazine counter is at 6 - 1 = 5 during those shots.

• When used alongside 100% ammo efficiency, make sure one shot is missing from the magazine, since the buff doesn't apply on a completely full one.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Available for 20 x Cred from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.

• Clip Delegation was originally acquired by reaching Rank 15 in Nightwave during Nora's Mix Volume 6 .
 • Beginning with Nightwave Nora’s Mix: Dreams of the Dead , it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Stacks only considers per trigger pull. Individual pellets from Multishot or Punch Through hitting multiple targets do not grant additional stacks.

• Reload from Holster effects only apply to the first reloaded tick. Subsequent ticks will then remove all stacks. The same applies to Cyte-09's Resupply .

• For Acid Shells builds, the increased Multishot and Status Chance will make the initial target easier to kill, but will have no effect on the area of effect explosions.

[ edit | edit source ]
 • Stacks only considers per trigger pull. Individual pellets from Multishot or Punch Through hitting multiple targets do not grant additional stacks.

• Reload from Holster effects only apply to the first reloaded tick. Subsequent ticks will then remove all stacks. The same applies to Cyte-09's Resupply .

• For Acid Shells builds, the increased Multishot and Status Chance will make the initial target easier to kill, but will have no effect on the area of effect explosions.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The Explosion does 600 Blast damage in an 18m radius at max rank, with 0% Status Chance and 100% falloff.
 • Explosion requires Line of Sight , and it's range is not affected by ( Primed ) Firestorm .

• Can break crates.

• Can trigger Enkaus 's instakill effect.

• Can damage Arbitration Shield Drone s and make them explode on death.

• Explosion damage can only be increased by Faction Damage Bonus (e.g. Bane of Infested , Roar ), Extra Hit abilities (e.g. Resupply ), or Damage Vulnerability on enemies (e.g. Molecular Prime ).
 • Can hit Sonar weakpoints.

• Can pop Spores when used with Toxic Lash .

• Enemies killed by spread forced Extra Hit procs will explode and spread them again.

• Combustion Beam is only usable on primary Continuous Weapons . This includes Robotic Weapons like Artax , Cryotra , and Verglas .
 • A notable exception to this rule is the Tenet Flux Rifle , because it was derived from continuous weapon ( Flux Rifle ), even though its firing mechanics are similar to an automatic rifle.

• Because the Panthera 's secondary fire has a continuous and fixed range, Combustion Beam can also be equipped on it. Enemies killed by either the primary or secondary fire will trigger the explosion.

• Torid Incarnon Genesis is still considered just Torid , and cannot equip this mod despite having a beam fire mode.

• Combustion beam essentially functions as a weaker Molecular Prime (chain reaction effect), making it effective at clearing weak low level enemies very rapidly.

• Enemies killed in one instant or vaporized on death (e.g. Volatile Runner ) will not trigger the explosion.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The mod's effects are only visible on Status Effects with an associated duration, either increasing the duration of the associated debuff ( Puncture , Cold , Magnetic , Radiation , and Viral ), or the number of ticks associated with DoT effects ( Slash , Toxin , Gas , and Heat ).
 • This mod will not affect the duration of crowd control effects generated by Status Effects (staggers from Impact , the panic duration of Heat , and the stun animation caused by Electricity ).

• However, it does affect the duration of the freeze effect applied by Cold after reaching 10 stacks.

• Increased Status Duration will slow down the Armor Strip effect of Heat . For example, with +100% status duration, the armor strip will occur every second instead of every half second. As a result, the full 50% armor strip will only be achieved after 4 seconds.

• Status Effects from the radial blasts of Syndicate Weapon Augments and Syndicate Weapons are not affected by this mod.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod is acquired by getting 1000 Points in the Operation False Profit Event.

• It is now offered as a periodic item for sale by Baro Ki'Teer , costing 330 330 and 200,000 Credits 200,000 to purchase. Note that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and this modification may not be available on a succeeding appearance.

• After the addition of the Plains of Eidolon , the mod may also obtainable by killing Eidolon Teralyst .

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Can be combined with Rupture to provide a 210% increase to Impact damage.

• When calculating total raw damage, a weapon will require an Impact damage percentage equal to 75% of its total base damage to equal the raw damage increase provided by a standard elemental mod (which increases corresponding elemental damages by 90%). The higher the Impact damage percentage over 75%, the more viable this mod will be as opposed to an elemental mod, and vice versa for lower values. These calculations only pertain to raw damage, and do not take into consideration enemy armor/flesh/shield damage multipliers.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Available for 20 x Cred from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.

• Critical Precision was originally acquired by reaching Rank 13 in Nightwave during Nora's Mix Volume 2 .
 • Beginning with Nightwave Nora’s Mix: Vol. 7 , it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Bonus is additive with similar buff such as Point Strike and Critical Delay .

• Body shots do not deduct the critical bonus, and only one bullet from a single burst has to strike an enemy in order to keep the critical bonus. Only missing every shot will decrease the bonus by 100%.
 • For example, Missing a single burst while at a 500% bonus reduce it to 400%.

• Bullets that hit enemy corpses are not considered missed shots, however shooting the heads of corpses will not increase the critical bonus.

• The bonus is added individually for each projectile which strikes the target's head. Equipping multishot mods can therefore allow the bonus to climb significantly more quickly.

• If using the Tiberon Prime , switching fire mode to either Semi or Automatic will immediately remove the bonus in its entirety.
 • The bonus is NOT lost when switching to or attacking with any other weapon or equipment, such as secondary or melee weapons, archguns, fishing spears, tranquilizer rifles, mining lasers or codex scanners.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Available for 20 x Cred from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.

• Exposing Harpoon was originally acquired by reaching Rank 20 in Nightwave during Series 2: The Emissary or Rank 20 during Nora's Choice .
 • Beginning with Nightwave Nora’s Mix: Vol. 5 , it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Exposing Harpoon is additive with similar mods. For example, with a max rank Exposing Harpoon and Point Strike , Harpak 's primary fire will have a 20% × (1 + 300% + 150%) = 110% critical chance.

• Duration is fixed at 15 seconds and can be refreshed.

• Although the mod description specifies pulling an enemy, activation only requires hitting an enemy with the secondary fire. This works against enemies that cannot be pulled (such as bosses) and will trigger even if the target point is invulnerable at the time of the attack.
 • Does not activate when hitting the Condrix .

 • The calculation of reload time using reload speed is: Reload Time = Base Reload Time ÷ (1 + Reload Speed Bonus)
 • At max rank, a +30% reload speed bonus reduces reload times by about 23%.

• On Bow type weapons such as the Paris , Fast Hands increases the notching speed of the arrow.

 • Has no effect on Komorex , Shedu , and Trumna (Primary Fire) despite their listed radial stat.

• Since Firestorm increases blast radius, it increases total blast area with the square of the bonus and the blast volume with the cube of the bonus.

• Due to the range increase, this mod lessens the damage falloff from the epicenter of the explosion.

• This mod increases the range of self-stagger .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod is highly ideal for use in low- accuracy weapons with a high fire rate , such as the Gorgon and Supra , as their high fire rate gives them a high chance to hit, allowing them to refresh the duration of the accuracy bonus continuously.

• This mod will not activate on hitting a Nullifier 's bubble.

• Status Effects from damage types such as Toxin or Slash will continue to refresh the mod's effects so long as they are causing damage.

• Stacks additively with other sources of weapon spread modifier.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • All sniper rifles have a 2 second combo duration, with the exception of the Lanka , which has a 6 second combo duration. Harkonar Scope at max rank extends this to 14 seconds, and to 18 seconds for Lanka.

• The extended combo duration only applies to a stack if the player is already scoped when the previous stack expires. As such, the most efficient combo conservation can either be achieved by always being scoped in, or only every 14 (18) seconds, when the counter decrements.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Hata-Satya can be obtained by reaching Rank 4 - Friend with the Entrati and purchased for 20,000 Standing 20,000 from Father .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Hata-Satya is additive with similar mods. For example, a max rank, max bonus Hata-Satya and Point Strike will have a 30% × (1 + 500% + 150%) = 225% critical chance.

• Additional hits from Multishot and Punch Through also count towards the bonus.

• Bonus damage instances from Toxic Lash and Xata's Whisper count as one extra hit.

• Swapping to Incarnon Form counts as reloading the Soma Prime and will therefore end the bonus.

• Using melee , Parazon mercy finishers, or activating an Exalted Weapon ability are counted as holstering the Soma Prime and will therefore end the bonus.

• Bonus ends as soon as the reload animation begins or when using Blood Forge .

• Hits against invulnerable enemies, including Blood Altar and Gaze , do count toward the bonus.

• Hits against Object -class health, barrier-like entities (Arctic Eximus snow globes and Nullifier bubbles), and enemies "converted" into allies ( Accuse , Mind Control , Enthrall , and Parasitic Link ), do not count toward the bonus.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Though dropped infrequently, it can be obtained as a reward from Dark Sector defense missions.

• There is a relatively easy way to farm this on the Kuva Fortress tile set. In many end rooms, there are Latcher Turrets that will deploy 2 waves of 8 Latchers when your warframe is detected by the scanners. An AoE weapon such as Ignis or a weapon that chains like Atomos can make quick work of the Latchers and you can repeat this infinitely. The turret will retreat into the ground after deploying its second wave, you can spawn it again by being scanned another time.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • High Voltage is offered as a periodic item for sale by Baro Ki'Teer , costing 300 300 and 150,000 Credits 150,000 to purchase.

• This mod can be obtained as a potential cache reward in Hive Sabotage missions in 22-23 expected tries and 150±50 tries to nearly guarantee.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • High Voltage is additive with similar mods.
 • For example, a Snipetron with a max rank High Voltage and Rifle Aptitude will have a 12% × (1 + 60% + 90%) = 30% status chance.

• Can be combined with Rime Rounds , Thermite Rounds and Malignant Force to achieve a combined status chance bonus of 240%. With Rifle Aptitude and Hammer Shot , this bonus can be increased to 410%, more than quintupling the weapon's status chance.

• With the four damage + status chance mods you can reach 100% status chance with any primaries that have a base status chance of 30% or higher.

[ edit | edit source ]
 • Added in Update 10.0 (2013-09-13).

• This Mod reduces or eliminates firing noise. Enemies that are not killed quick enough will still be alerted.

• Shooting a silent Weapon within 6 meters of an enemy or at the terrain near an enemy will cause them to stop and look around, but will not change their actual alert state.

• Applying this Mod on a high damage Weapon (such as the Latron Prime ) will make it suitable for stealth combat, making it a decent alternative to a bow.

• At max rank, Hush will allow Ivara to stay invisible while Prowl is active.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Cannot produce multiple procs in a single instance of damage alongside any other Slash sources, such as a weapon's innate Slash , Hunter Munitions , or the debuff from Seeking Talons .
 • Procs of Hunter Munitions and Internal Bleeding are drawn independently, and if both proc at the same time, only 1 slash proc is applied. With both mods, if a shot both crits and applies impact status, the probability of Slash proc is 54.5% (with attack speed >= 2.5) or 79% (with attack speed < 2.5).

• Proccing impact more than once in a single instance of damage will not allow this mod to proc more than once, nor will it increase the chance of the mod proccing.

• Hard Engage works with this mod.

• Fire Rate buffs such as Gauss ' Redline , Wisp ' Haste Reservoirs and Arcane Acceleration can negatively impact this mod's performance since it may increase the weapon's fire rate past 2.5.

• If the weapon's fire rate is exactly 2.5 it won't count towards the 70% chance.

• For bows, the threshold is still depending of the Fire Rate, even if it is an hidden stat. Most bows have an unmodded Fire Rate of 1, except : Cinta with 1.5 Fire Rate, and Kuva Bramma and Nataruk with 0.667 Fire Rate.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Can be used on the Prisma Tetra and Tenet Tetra variants as well.
 • For the Tenet Tetra, this mod will not affect the grenade Alternate Fire .

• Projectiles interact strangely with punch through effects - they will not bounce off of enemies, and will often stop after hitting terrain.
 • It seems that when the projectile hits a surface it decides if it can penetrate the object or not, if it can't, it bounces off the surface. This process goes on as long as the projectile has not penetrated anything yet. The projectile will only punch through what it can penetrate, and after the first penetration, it works just like normal projectile with the bounce effect.
 • Due to the bullet penetrating thin obstacles at first hit, it is recommended not to use punch through if you plan to shoot the floor or the walls to make full use the bounce effect as it will often penetrate the walls or floor and not stay in the space.

• In general, it is recommended not to use any punch through mods.

• The mod will reduce the range of the projectiles from infinite to 100 meters. Every time the projectile bounces, that range seems to be further decreased. After just two bounces, the projectile will fizzle out after traveling less than 32 meters from the initial firing position.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Meticulous Aim could be obtained by reaching certain ranks from Nightwave :
 • Rank 23 from Series 2: The Emissary .

• Rank 14 from Intermission III .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Calculated additively with Vulkar 's headshot zoom bonus, which then calculated multiplicatively with weapon overall damage.
 • At max level of zoom with Meticulous Aim equipped, the weapon benefits from a total of 70 + 105 = 175% headshot damage bonus.

• Despite the differing descriptions, it stacks with Target Acquired for up to 70 + 60 + 105 = 235% headshot damage bonus.

• Bodyshot damage penalty is multiplicative to weapon overall damage.
 • For example, using Vulkar modded with a Serration and a maxed rank Meticulous Aim will reduce body shot damage from (225 × (1 + 1.65) = 596.25 to (225 × (1 + 1.65) × (1 - 0.45)) = 327.93 .

• "Bodyshot" means anything but a headshot, including limbs or parts that might have higher damage multipliers such as MOA backpacks.

• The -45% bodyshot damage is always applied twice to Slash , Heat , Toxin , and Blast , but will not apply to Electricity or Gas if they are from a headshot.

• Both bonus damage and damage penalty still apply whether zooming while firing or not.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Available for 20 x Cred from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.

• Napalm Grenades was originally acquired by reaching Rank 22 in Nightwave during Series 1: The Wolf of Saturn Six or reaching Rank 13 during Intermission II .
 • Beginning with Nightwave Nora’s Mix: Vol. 6 , it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Overall damage will increase, though two parts of the damage still remain. As an example, an unmodified Secura Penta equipped with nothing but Napalm Grenades will deal 325 Heat area of effect damage, as well as 325 Heat direct impact damage. If either procs, the enemy will be dealt procs equal to 162 Heat damage.
 • In addition, direct impacts will create a separate 100 Heat which procs, likely there only to guarantee that enemies hit directly will be set on fire.

• Cannot be used with Tether Grenades .

• The fire patches generated by this mod last about 5 seconds.

• When installed, Penta's grenades are changed to detonate on contact while still maintaining the manual detonation function with Alternate Fire allowing mid-air detonation and possible headshots on humanoid targets, also resulting in fire patches spawning mid-air.
 • Adhesive Blast can be installed to prevent grenades from detonating on contact by making them stick to the object that they make contact with in which they can be manually detonated or will auto-detonate about 5 seconds after attachment.

• Unlike similar weapons such as Torid , Pox , Mutalist Cernos and Ogris with Nightwatch Napalm , the fire patches generated by this mod do not deal direct damage, but instead proc Heat status effect when enemies enter the effect radius. This deals very little damage regardless of the mods on the weapon, but causes enemies to flail around. This effect works on the player as well. The Heat status effect is not affected by status duration mods.

• When used with Mirage 's Hall of Mirrors , it will create extra patches of fire.
 • The fire-patch of the user will cause Heat damage while the clone's fire-patches are only visual and will not damage anything.

• Unlike Nightwatch Napalm , the small fire patches range cannot be increased further, restricting the crowd control benefits of the bonus Heat .

• When modded to create Gas or Radiation , direct hits and the patches will still deal Heat damage and status effects, though this is just for utility or crowd control as they will deal very low damage.

sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Rather than doing 30% of damage, it creates an AoE dealing 150 Heat damage per tick.

• Damage output can only be increased by base damage mods (e.g. Serration , Heavy Caliber , Semi-Rifle Cannonade ), and faction mods (e.g. Bane of Grineer , Bane of Corpus ).
 • Damage is increased by Kuva weapon's bonus damage stat.

• Napalm cannot deal critical damage via any means.

• Napalm has 68% chance to proc Heat , ticking for 50% of napalm's damage per second for 3 seconds.
 • Heat proc damage is affected by Heat mods.

• Status chance is not affected by mods.

• Continuous Misery and Lavos 's duration passive increase the duration of the Heat proc.

• Firestorm ( Primed Firestorm ) increases the effective area.

• Given that Nullifier Crewman bubbles will deflect the trajectory of Ogris rockets, it is advisable to aim at the nearest surface of an approaching Nullifier to allow the Napalm to spawn.
 • Unlike Zenistar , but similar to Torid , Napalm will not damage Nullifier bubbles themselves, but Nullifiers who walk over the flames will become damaged, even inside their bubble.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Can be combined with Piercing Hit to provide a 210% increase to Puncture damage.

• When calculating total raw damage, a weapon will require a Puncture damage percentage equal to 75% of its total base damage to equal the raw damage increase provided by a standard elemental mod (which increases corresponding elemental damages by 90%). The higher the Puncture damage percentage over 75%, the more viable this mod will be as opposed to an elemental mod, and vice versa for lower values. These calculations only pertain to raw damage, and do not take into consideration enemy armor/flesh/shield damage multipliers.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod can be purchased unranked from Baro Ki'Teer for 2,995 2,995 and 1,000,000 Credits 1,000,000 . Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available every time.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Despite its name, Primed Chamber is not related to Primed Mods , and was in fact added to the game long before the first such mod. As a result, it is not the "Primed version" of Charged Chamber , and thus can be equipped alongside it.

• Primed Chamber is applied multiplicatively after all other modifiers from mods and abilities.
 • For example, a Vectis with a max rank Primed Chamber, Serration , Cryo Rounds , Target Acquired , and Bane of Grineer , and a subsumed Eclipse with base Ability Strength, will deal 225 × (1 + 1.0) × (1 + 1.65) × (1 + 0.90) × (1 + 0.60) × (1 + 0.30) × (1 + 0.30) = 6,127 headshot damage.

• The damage bonus applies to all Multishot hits and to Status Damage .

• Stacks additively with Charged Chamber for up to 140% bonus damage.

• If used with Ammo Efficiency bonuses, Primed Chamber will provide bonus damage to a shot as long as the magazine counter is at Max Magazine − 1 after a shot is fired.
 • For example, a Vulkar with Energized Munitions will have the 4th, 5th, 6th, and 7th shots buffed by Primed Chamber, since the magazine counter is at 6 - 1 = 5 during those shots.

• Wait to trigger any 100% ammo efficiency effects until a single shot has been consumed from the magazine, since the buff doesn't apply if the mag remains completely full after the shot.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • An unranked Serration is given upon completion of The Teacher Quest.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Serration damage is applied before any other damage type. Therefore, every other damage increasing mod benefits from Serration. E.g. a weapon with a base damage of 18 equipped with a rank 9 Serration mod (150%) would have a new base damage of 45: 18 × (1 + 1.5) = 45
 • This new base damage will be what is used to calculate elemental damages.

• When paired with other mods:
 • Multiplicatively stacks with Faction Damage Mods for a maximum of +244.5% damage.
 • +310.75% for Primed Faction Damage Mods.

• Additively stacks with Heavy Caliber for a maximum of +330% damage.

• Stacks with Heavy Caliber and Faction Damage Mods for a total of +459% damage at max rank.
 • +566.5% for Primed Faction Damage Mods.

• At higher ranks the fusion cost of this mod becomes quite costly. Upgrading other mods simultaneously makes for a better investment of your Endo compared to only enhancing this one alone.
 • It is suggested to upgrade this mod to at least rank 5 quickly though, since it is useful on a lot of weapons.

• Maxing this mod requires 20,460 Endo and 988,218 Credits 988,218 .

• Spread radius of 6m is centered on the enemy initially inflicted with the Cold Status Effect.

• Spread radius is not affected by Firestorm ( Primed ).

sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The in-game UI considers any possible multishot capabilities when showing final damage numbers, and will display the damage of the weapon as if it was fired once.
 • Example: A Braton with a level 4 Split Chamber (75%) showing 36 of the total damage, with a 25% chance that the Braton will show a total damage of 18.

• This mod also has an effect on weapons with 100% accuracy (such as the Dera ), but since the projectiles do not spread out, the extra projectile is generated inside the original projectile. This mod's effects are much more noticeable if Heavy Caliber is installed, as the accuracy penalty generated will allow the projectiles to noticeably spread.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The explosion deals 250 Blast damage to the target, as well as to nearby enemies within 10 meters of the blast.
 • The explosion inflicts self-stagger .

• Unlike the area blasts of dedicated blast weapons like Launchers, Thunderbolt does NOT have a safety distance to prevent self-stagger. Furthermore, if the shot hits and explodes close enough to you it will do a full Knockdown instead of just a self-stagger.

• The explosive damage from this mod cannot be increased with base damage, such as Serration , and elemental mods such as Hellfire , but it will be increased by Bane mods, like Bane of Infested .

• Compared to Launchers, the Thunderbolt blast has full Punch Through (like most Launchers) and zero falloff throughout its entire area of effect (unlike most Launchers), so all enemies take full damage even at the outer-most edges of the blast while behind cover.

• The blast area is increased by Firestorm or Primed Firestorm .

• The explosions are silent.

• Arrows fired at terrain and other surfaces still explode, but not arrows fired at teammates or other allies.

• When the explosion occurs, the projectile of the arrow causing that is actually "dissolved". Thus any subsequent mechanics relating to the projectile will not happen, such as punching through or the spore coming after the arrow of Mutalist Cernos .

• Cannot be equipped on Lenz .

• Can be paired with Cautious Shot to reduce stagger effects.

• The explosion will trigger effects that activate upon damaging enemies; for example, the Thunderbolt explosion created from an Evensong 's projectile will apply Puncture status effects to each enemy it hits.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Unseen Dread could be obtained by reaching Rank 25 with Nightwave during Nora's Mix Volume 7 .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Critical Damage bonus can come from any source of Invisibility , not just from this augment.

• Critical Damage bonus is additive with other crit damage mods (e.g. Vital Sense ).

• As of Hotfix 37.0.4 (2024-10-10), Multishot cannot bypass the hit requirements (i.e. attaining 200% multishot and hitting a single enemy).

• The augment description is misleading - "single projectile" means "same shot".
 • Punch Through hitting >2 enemies will trigger invisibility. Primed Shred , and Seek are especially useful as they provide a lot of body/terrain punch through.

• Multishot hitting >2 enemies will trigger invisibility. Split Flights , Heavy Caliber , and Primary Overcharge are especially useful as they increase the amount of arrows and/or their spread.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Vile Discharge could be obtained by reaching Rank 23 with Nightwave during Nora's Mix Volume 2.

 Can be purchased from Daughter during the October 2024 Nights of Naberus event for 50 Mother Token .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Discharge damage is charged by Embolist's main attack, any DoTs inflicted by it. Discharge damage itself (including any forced procs) does not charge a new Discharge.
 • Charge amount is normally 1:1 of output damage. For example, it ignores enemy health types, enemy armor, and debuff amps such as Viral , but is affected by player damage buffs such as Garuda 's passive.

• Overkill damage does not count towards the charge.

• Cascadia Empowered applies towards the charge.

• Discharge can be spammed as long as the primary fire DoTs keep charging it.

• Discharge projectile always deals 10 Impact on contact, which is affected by Damage, Element, and Faction mods, but not multishot.

• Discharge explosion is 100% Toxin regardless of modded damage types or damage used to charge the attack.
 • The explosion is not directly affected by any mods except Fulmination
 • Valence Formation is able to apply the status effects to the direct hit and explosion, but not the damage bonus. However, the proc will affect Lavos if he is within range unless he has Valence Block active.

• Explodes in a 6 meter radius with ~80% falloff.

• Does not inflict self-stagger .

• Discharge has a base fire rate of ~2.5 that is affected by fire rate mods, meaning it cannot perform other actions for 0.4 seconds after firing.

• Discharge cannot be fired while Embolist is using its primary fire or if it's reloading.

• Discharge cannot deal critical damage or Status Effects , not even with flat bonuses or pathways like Mutalist Quanta orb, but can proc Slash via Seeking Talons which counts its own ticks back toward the charge.

• Discharge ignores the Rift .

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Available for 20 x Cred from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.

• Wild Frenzy was originally acquired by reaching Rank 14 in Nightwave during Series 1: The Wolf of Saturn Six or reaching Rank 20 during Intermission II .
 • Beginning with Nightwave Nora’s Mix: Vol. 5 , it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod cannot be used with the Twin Grakatas .

• Both Grakata and Prisma Grakata will have a fire rate of 40 / 44.44 / 50 / 57.14 / 66.67 / 80 , despite the Prisma variant having a higher fire rate on its normal firing mode.

• Abilities and mods that modify fire rate have no effect on this alternate fire mode. As a result, mods like Vile Precision and Critical Delay only provide their benefit.

• Aiming will be disabled during the burst, hence the alternate fire cannot benefit from Acolyte Mods .

• Ammo is refunded into the ammo pool, not the magazine. Hence, the player still has to reload the weapon after killing two or more enemies.

• Because all remaining ammo is ejected, increasing magazine capacity such as with Magazine Warp will extend the burst.

• If buffed with unlimited ammo such as from the Reactant Buff , the weapon will only fire its magazine amount at a time, with small delays between each trigger pull.

• Any action that would cancel the salvo, such as Rolling , while there is ammo in the magazine, would render the refund effect null thus requires killing 2 enemies again in order to have the reload in effect.

evidence.excerpt:
Acquisition
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Available for 20 x Cred from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.

• Wild Frenzy was originally acquired by reaching Rank 14 in Nightwave during Series 1: The Wolf of Saturn Six or reaching Rank 20 during Intermission II .
 • Beginning with Nightwave Nora’s Mix: Vol. 5 , it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

Notes
[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod cannot be used with the Twin Grakatas .

• Both Grakata and Prisma Grakata will have a fire rate of 40 / 44.44 / 50 / 57.14 / 66.67 / 80 , despite the Prisma variant having a higher fire rate on its normal firing mode.

• Abilities and mods that modify fire rate have no effect on this alternate fire mode. As a result, mods like Vile Precision and Critical Delay only provide their benefit.

• Aiming will be disabled during the burst, hence the alternate fire cannot benefit from Acolyte Mods .

• Ammo is refunded into the ammo pool, not the magazine. Hence, the player still has to reload the weapon after killing two or more enemies.

• Because all remaining ammo is ejected, increasing magazine capacity such as with Magazine Warp will extend the burst.

• If buffed with unlimited ammo such as from the Reactant Buff , the weapon will only fire its magazine amount at a time, with small delays between each trigger pull.

• Any action that would cancel the salvo, such as Rolling , while there is ammo in the magazine, would render the refund effect null thus requires killing 2 enemies again in order to have the reload in effect.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The additional rounds do not consume additional ammunition.

• At rank 4 or higher, this mod guarantees that at least 2 rounds will be fired for every 1 round of ammunition expended.

• At maximum rank, this mod grants an 100% chance to fire one additional round (2 rounds/shot) and a 20% chance to fire two additional rounds (3 rounds/shot).
 • Equipping this mod and Lethal Torrent simultaneously will provide 180% multishot, giving the sidearm a 100% chance to fire one additional round (2 rounds/shot) and an 80% chance to fire two additional rounds (3 rounds/shot).

• For weapons that fire multiple pellets (i.e. a Bronco ) the pellet count is multiplied by the multishot value.
 • A Bronco with a rank 5 Barrel Diffusion will fire 7 × ( 1 + 1.2 ) = 15.4 pellets, meaning it has a 100% chance to fire 15 pellets and a 40% chance to fire 1 extra pellet.

• The in-game UI will consider any possible multishot capabilities when showing total damage numbers, and does not represent single-projectile damage.
 • Total damage displayed does not represent the actual damage per shot, but the expected average damage.

• Equipping this mod increases the status chance of a weapon indirectly. This is due to the possibility of shooting more than one projectile.

 • Equipping this mod and Lethal Torrent simultaneously will provide 180% multishot, giving the sidearm a 100% chance to fire one additional round (2 rounds/shot) and an 80% chance to fire two additional rounds (3 rounds/shot).

• For weapons that fire multiple pellets (i.e. a Bronco ) the pellet count is multiplied by the multishot value.
 • A Bronco with a rank 5 Barrel Diffusion will fire 7 × ( 1 + 1.2 ) = 15.4 pellets, meaning it has a 100% chance to fire 15 pellets and a 40% chance to fire 1 extra pellet.

• The in-game UI will consider any possible multishot capabilities when showing total damage numbers, and does not represent single-projectile damage.
 • Total damage displayed does not represent the actual damage per shot, but the expected average damage.

• Equipping this mod increases the status chance of a weapon indirectly. This is due to the possibility of shooting more than one projectile.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod was acquired by surviving at least 30 minutes in the Operation: Mutalist Incursions Event.

• Obtainable from re-accruing Razorback Armada
 • Was also obtainable from the Divine Will Tactical Alert.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Can be combined with No Return and Jugulus Spines to provide a 300% increase to Puncture damage.

• When calculating total raw damage, a weapon will require a Puncture damage percentage equal to 75% of its total base damage to equal the raw damage increase provided by a standard elemental mod (which increases corresponding elemental damages by 90%). The higher the Puncture damage percentage over 75%, the more viable this mod will be as opposed to an elemental mod, and vice versa for lower values. These calculations only pertain to raw damage, and do not take into consideration enemy armor/flesh/shield damage multipliers.

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: For the cannonade mod class released in Update 36.0, see Cannonade Mods. Time is relative. Choose when ready.This article refers to valid content that is currently unavailable to be acquired in the live version of the game.It may return to be available again in a future update or event. Cannonade is a pistol mod that gives hits a chance to explode, dealing 250 Blast damage to the target, as well as to nearby enemies within 10 meters of the blast (scaled by distance). Stats Rank Proc Chance Cost 0 +7.5% 6 1 +15% 7 2 +22.5% 8 3 +30% 9 Trivia Cannonade was briefly available via Transmutation between Update 18.5 (2016-03-04) and Hotfix 18.5.2 (2016-03-05). Players were allowed to keep their existing copies, though can no longer trade them. This mod is virtually identical to Thunderbolt.

sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The explosion deals 250 Blast damage to the target and any enemies within 10 meters of where the projectile lands.
 • The radius can be increased with Fulmination ( Primed ).

• The explosion inflicts self-stagger .

• The explosive damage from this mod is not increased by any base damage bonus, such as Hornet Strike or Eclipse , nor elemental mods such as Heated Charge , but it is increased by sources of Faction damage bonus such as Expel Infested and Roar .

• Cannot be used on Castanas ( Sancti ), Pox , Staticor and Talons .
 • Can be installed on Aegrit and Cantare , but does not have any effect.

• The explosions are silent.

• The explosions will only damage enemies that are within line of sight of the center of the explosion.

• The explosions themselves cannot proc Status Effects or crit .

• The explosion damage is not affected by the Extinguished Dragon Key .

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Available for 20 x Cred from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.

• Critical Mutation was originally acquired by reaching Rank 13 in Nightwave during Nora's Mix Volume 5 .
 • Beginning with Nightwave Nora’s Mix: Vol. 9 , it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Critical Chance bonus is additive with mods such as Pistol Gambit

• Critical Damage bonus is additive with mods such as Target Cracker .

• Bonus Critical Chance and Critical Damage remain if the weapon is reloaded by holster mods such as Eject Magazine or the Synth Mods Set

• Starting the reload animation and then interrupting it, such as by rolling, the grenade can still be thrown and it will not reload the weapon but it will still reduce the bonus by 30% if the grenade doesn't hit at least 3 enemies.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Available for 20 x Cred from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.

• Deadly Maneuvers was originally acquired by reaching Rank 7 in Nightwave during Series 3 .
 • Beginning with Nightwave Nora’s Mix: Vol. 6 , it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Dodging includes rolling , side dodging , and back flipping .
 • Merulina 's dash is not included.

• The critical chance is multiplicatively applied after mods on the Magnus ( Prime ). For example, with ​ Primed Pistol Gambit ​ installed the Magnus will have a total crit chance of 63.1%, and when under the effects of Deadly Maneuvers a headshot will have a critical chance of 315.5%.
 • Covenant 's crit chance is added after Deadly Maneuvers is applied, unlike other Absolute Bonuses from Cat's Eye , Arcane Avenger and orbs from Mutalist Quanta and Simulor ( Synoid ).

 Total Crit Chance = (Base Crit Chance × (1 + Relative Bonus) + Absolute Bonus) × (1 + Deadly Maneuvers) + Covenant

[ edit | edit source ]
 Fulmination was first acquired from Ergo Glast in a Tenno relay for 75 Animo Beacons during the Operation: Ambulas Reborn event.

 It returned as an offering sold by Nakak (as Operational Supply ) in Cetus during the Operation: Plague Star event for 5,000 Credits 5,000 and 1,500 Standing 1,500 Operational Supply Standing.

sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.text:

 • While it can be installed on any secondary weapon, Fulmination only has noticeable effects on the following explosive weapons:
 • Aegrit

• Akarius ( Prime )

• Angstrum ( Prisma )

• Incarnon mode of the Atomos

• Balefire Charger

• Castanas ( Sancti )

• Tombfinger

• Due to the range increase, this mod lessens the damage falloff from the epicenter of the explosion.

• This mod increases the range of self-stagger .

 • Cannot produce multiple procs in a single instance of damage alongside any other Slash sources such as a weapon's innate Slash or the debuff from Seeking Talons .

• Proccing Impact more than once in a single instance of damage will not allow this mod to proc more than once, nor will it increase the chance of the mod proccing.

• Hard Engage works with this mod.

• Fire Rate buffs such as Gauss ' Redline , Wisp's Haste Reservoirs , and Arcane Velocity can negatively impact this mod's performance since it may increase the weapon's fire rate past 2.5.

• If the weapon's fire rate is exactly 2.5, it will not receive the 2x bonus.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • An unranked Hornet Strike is given upon completion of The Teacher Quest.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Note that Hornet Strike damage is applied before every other damage type. Therefore, every other damage-increasing mod also benefits from Hornet Strike. A weapon with a base damage of 18 equipped with a rank 9 Hornet Strike mod (200%) would have a new base damage of 54: 18 × (1 + 2.0) = 54 .
 • This new base damage will be what is used to calculate elemental damages.

• Due to how it affects the base damage, this mod is necessary for all secondary weapons that aren't using damage increasing arcanes or GunCO effects.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Hydraulic Crosshairs is additive with similar mods. For example, a Sicarus with a max rank Hydraulic Crosshairs and Pistol Gambit will have a 16% × (1 + 135% + 120%) = 56.8% critical chance.

• Hydraulic Crosshairs will trigger upon headshots, i.e. any weapon hit to an enemy's head, whether the target survives or not.
 • Headshots will refresh Hydraulic Crosshairs' timer.

• If activated, Hydraulic Crosshairs will display an icon timer beside the player's health UI, and the Warframe will be briefly surrounded by an energy ribbon.

• Hydraulic Crosshairs' buff does not seem to multiply prior flat-crit% buffs such as Arcane Avenger .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Jolt is additive with similar mods. For example, a Sicarus with a max rank Jolt and Sure Shot will have a 6% × (1 + 60% + 90%) = 15% status chance.

• This mod can be combined with Frostbite , Pistol Pestilence and Scorch to achieve a combined status chance bonus of 240%. With Sure Shot and Stunning Speed , this bonus can be increased to 360%. With Saxum Spittle , Jugulus Spines , and Carnis Stinger , this bonus can be increased to 540%.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod was once acquired by getting 10 Exposure Points in the Operation: Gate Crash Event.

• Can also be acquired as an end-of-mission reward from accomplishing the Endurance challenge of the Emergency Exit Tactical Alert.

• It is offered as a periodic item for sale by Baro Ki'Teer, costing 375 375 and 150,000 Credits 150,000 to purchase. Note that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and this mod may not be available on a succeeding appearance.

• It is now available as a possible drop for capturing the Eidolon Hydrolyst .

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Can be combined with Carnis Stinger and Razor Shot to provide a 300% increase to Slash damage.

• When calculating total raw damage, a weapon will require a Slash damage percentage equal to 75% of its total base damage to equal the raw damage increase provided by a standard elemental mod (which increases corresponding elemental damages by 90%). The higher the Slash damage percentage over 75%, the more viable this mod will be as opposed to an elemental mod, and vice versa for lower values. These calculations only pertain to raw damage, and do not take into consideration enemy armor/flesh/shield damage multipliers.
 • Due to this comparison, the only secondaries to effectively use Maim as a damage increase mod are Pyrana , Akjagara Prime and Euphona Prime 's secondary shot, as all other weapons have 70% ratio or less.

• Titania 's Dex Pixia are also able to effectively use this mod, having a ratio of 80% slash.

• That being said, this mod can still be effectively used to increase the chance of slash procs in status builds, given how the type of damage amounts affects the calculations for procs.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The mod's effects are only visible on Status Effects with an associated duration, either increasing the duration of the associated debuff ( Puncture , Cold , Magnetic , Corrosive , Radiation , and Viral ), or the number of ticks associated with DoT effects ( Slash , Toxin , Gas , and Heat ).
 • This mod will not affect the duration of crowd control effects generated by Status Effects (staggers from Impact , the panic duration of Heat , and the stun animation caused by Electricity ).

• However, it does affect the duration of the freeze effect applied by Cold after reaching 10 stacks.

• Increased Status Duration will slow down the Armor Strip effect of Heat . For example, with +100% status duration, the armor strip will occur every second instead of every half second. As a result, the full 50% armor strip will only be achieved after 4 seconds.

• Status Effects from the radial blasts of Syndicate Weapon Augments and Syndicate Weapons are not affected by this mod.

sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Ruinous Extension only benefits Continuous Weapon which fire fixed-range hitscan beams or cones (specifically this mod can only be equipped on secondaries with the hidden BEAM Compatibility Tag ), these include:
 • Atomos
 • Only affects Atomos' initial beam, does not affect beam chains between targets.

• Catabolyst ( Coda )
 • Only affects Catabolyst's initial beam, does not affect throwing grenades when reloading.

• Cycron ( Tenet )
 • Only affects Tenet Cycron's initial beam, does not affect beam chains between targets.

• Embolist

• Gammacor ( Synoid )

• Secondary Gaze (Primary) kitgun
 • Only affects Gaze's' initial beam, does not affect beam chains between targets.

• Nukor ( Kuva )
 • Only affects Kuva Nukor's' initial beam, does not affect beam chains between targets.

• Spectra ( Vandal )

• This mod does not work with either the Sonicor or the Staticor ; to increase their effective ranges, use Lethal Momentum .

• Despite its Incarnon form being a beam weapon, Furis cannot equip this mod.

• Tenet Spirex can equip this mod since it has the BEAM tag despite it not being a beam weapon.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • A good mission to find Scorch Synthesis as a Target is Cassini - Saturn , a Capture Mission.

• Their Ignis can penetrate Frost 's Snow Globe .

• Damage scaling is very dramatic on a Scorch. At levels 20 and above can be lethal to a player in less than 5 seconds of continuous exposure to their Ignis, while only 3 seconds for Sentinels. Be cautious.

• Nekros' Shadows of the Dead does not prioritize Scorches despite them dealing far more damage than elite lancers, which are prioritized.

• While the Scorch still only fires its Ignis when enemies get into the pre-buffed Ignis range (around 20 meters) their flames can still reach 40 meters, damaging players behind walls and without warning.
 • This is especially deadly with Nekros , as his shadows attract the Scorch's attention and fire upon them, often hitting the player as well.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Seekers affected by Nyx 's Mind Control or Chaos will deploy Latchers which are allied, causing enemies to become distracted by them if they come nearby. Although weak to a single shot by any Grineer , a mind-controlled Seeker with duration can effectively spawn many Latchers, creating many possible entities that can redirect enemy fire.

• Shadow Seekers do not deploy Latchers.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Equipping this mod will set weapon's Fire Rate to its default, ignoring other modifiers, even negative effects (e.g. Creeping Bullseye ).
 • Mods such as Rivens and Lethal Torrent that affect Fire Rate among multiple other stats will still apply their other effects.

• Weapons with an Incarnon mode must have Semi-Auto trigger type for both firing modes in order to equip this mod, such as Bronco Incarnon Genesis , Lato Incarnon Genesis and Lex Incarnon Genesis .

• For a full list of secondary weapons and their trigger types, see Weapon Comparison - Secondary .

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Sentient Surge can be obtained by reaching Rank 9 with Nightwave during Nora's Mix Volume 4.

• Can be purchased from Daughter during the October 2025 Nights of Naberus event for 50 Mother Token .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Reloaded ammo is taken from the Ocucor's ammo reserves. This mod does not generate ammo.

• Critical Chance and Status Chance bonus increases up to 240% each, and is Additive to other crit chance and status chance mods.

• Reloading the weapon will reset the bonuses, as they are directly tied to its tendril effect which resets on reload.
 • Bonuses are also reset on Host Migration .

• Is completely inferior to Ice Storm which can give a larger increase to Magazine Capacity (40%) and a 40% increase in Cold damage for the exact same mod capacity cost.

 • Added in Update 10.0 (2013-09-13).

• This Mod only reduces or eliminates firing noise. Any corpses, returning fire, or enemies that are not killed quick enough will still cause an alert.

• Shooting a silent Weapon within 6 meters of an enemy or at the terrain near an enemy will cause them to stop and look around, but will not change their actual alert state.

• A rank 3 Suppress Mod (100% Noise reduction) will allow Ivara to stay invisible while Prowl is active.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Casting an ability will stop the energy generation for 1 second.
 • Channeled abilities that drain energy over time, such as Immolation , Sound Quake , and Renewal will completely prevent energy generation for as long as they are active and for 1 second afterwards. Abilities with variable energy drain such as Artemis Bow , Pacify & Provoke and Desecrate do not interrupt the energy generation.

• All stacks of the buff expire simultaneously at the end of the duration. Applying a new stack refreshes the buff duration.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Can be combined with Accelerated Blast and Flechette to provide a 270% increase to Puncture damage.

• This is best suited for the Cedo due to its primary fire's base of entirely Puncture damage.

• When calculating total raw damage, a weapon will require a Puncture damage percentage equal to 75% of its total base damage to equal the raw damage increase provided by a standard elemental mod (which increases corresponding elemental damages by 90%). The higher the Puncture damage percentage over 75%, the more viable this mod will be as opposed to an elemental mod, and vice versa for lower values. These calculations only pertain to raw damage and do not take into consideration enemy armor/flesh/shield damage multipliers.

 • Has no effect on Corrupted and Narmer Corpus (e.g. Corrupted Crewman , Narmer Crewman ).

• Despite being listed as a multiplier, it will stack additively with other sources of Damage to Corpus.
 • For example, if you had a Riven Mod with x0.5 Damage to Corpus and a max rank Cleanse Corpus with x1.3 Damage to Corpus, you would have a 1 - 0.5 + 0.3 = x0.8 multiplier for damage against Corpus with both mods equipped.
