Notes
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Frigid Blast is additive with similar mods. For example, a Strun with a max rank Frigid Blast and Shotgun Savvy will have a 20% × (1 + 60% + 90%) = 50% status chance.

• This mod can be combined with Toxic Barrage , Shell Shock and Scattering Inferno to achieve a combined status chance bonus of 240%. With Shotgun Savvy , this bonus can be increased to 330%.

• Can be combined with Chilling Reload and Chilling Grasp for a total of +210% additional Cold damage.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod is acquired by getting 1000 Points in the Operation False Profit Event.

• It is now offered as a periodic item for sale by Baro Ki'Teer , costing 365 365 and 220,000 Credits 220,000 to purchase. Note that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and this mod may not be available on a succeeding appearance.

• After the addition of the Plains of Eidolon , the mod may also obtainable by killing Eidolon Teralyst .

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Can be combined with Disruptor to provide a 210% increase to Impact damage.

• When calculating total raw damage, a weapon will require an Impact damage percentage equal to 75% of its total base damage to equal the raw damage increase provided by a standard elemental mod (which increases corresponding elemental damages by 90%). The higher the Impact damage percentage over 75%, the more viable this mod will be as opposed to an elemental mod, and vice versa for lower values. These calculations only pertain to raw damage, and do not take into consideration enemy armor/flesh/shield damage multipliers.
 • The only weapons that are at or above the 75% Impact damage percentage are Sobek and Sweeper / Prime .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Laser Sight is additive with similar mods. For example, a Strun with a max rank Laser Sight and Blunderbuss will have a 7.5% × (1 + 120% + 90%) = 23.25% critical chance.

• Laser Sight will trigger upon headshots, i.e. any weapon hit to an enemy's head, whether the target survives or not.
 • Headshots will refresh Laser Sight's timer.

• If activated, Laser Sight will display an icon timer beside the player's health UI, and the Warframe will be briefly surrounded by an energy ribbon.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The mod's effects are only visible on Status Effects with an associated duration, either increasing the duration of the associated debuff ( Puncture , Cold , Corrosive , Magnetic , Radiation , and Viral ), or the number of ticks associated with DoT effects ( Slash , Electricity , Toxin , Gas , and Heat ).
 • This mod will not affect the duration of crowd control effects generated by Status Effects (staggers from Impact and the panic duration of Heat ).

• However, it does affect the duration of the freeze effect applied by Cold after reaching 10 stacks.

• Increased Status Duration will slow down the Armor Strip effect of Heat . For example, with +100% status duration, the armor strip will occur every second instead of every half second. As a result, the full 50% armor strip will only be achieved after 4 seconds.

• Status Effects from the radial blasts of Syndicate Weapon Augments and Syndicate Weapons are not affected by this mod.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Cannot produce multiple procs in a single instance of damage alongside any other Magnetic sources such as a weapon's innate Magnetic .

• Proccing Impact more than once in a single instance of damage will not allow this mod to proc more than once, nor will it increase the chance of the mod proccing.

• Hard Engage works with this mod.

• Fire Rate buffs such as Gauss ' Redline , Wisp 's Haste Reservoirs , and Arcane Tempo can negatively impact this mod's performance since it may increase the weapon's fire rate past 2.5.

• If the weapon's fire rate is exactly 2.5, it will not receive the 2x bonus.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Affects base damage which is applied before every other damage type. Therefore, every other damage increasing mod is benefiting from Point Blank as well.
 • This mod combined with Vicious Spread and Blaze give off a total damage increase of +240%.

• With Primed Point Blank instead of Point Blank , this adds up for a total damage increase of +315% with Vicious Spread and Blaze.

• Due to how it affects the base damage, this mod is necessary for all shotgun weapons in order to use them optimally. Few mods can replace the effectiveness of this mod.

 • Strun Incarnon Genesis is currently the only Incarnon weapon capable of equipping this mod.

• For a full list of shotgun weapons and their trigger types, see Weapon Comparison - Primary Shotguns .

 • This Mod only reduces or eliminates firing noise. Any corpses, returning fire or enemies that are not killed quick enough will still cause an alert.

• Shooting a silent Weapon within 6 meters of an enemy or at the terrain near an enemy will cause them to stop and look around, but will not change their actual alert state.

• A rank 3 Silent Battery Mod (100% Noise reduction) will allow Ivara to stay invisible while Prowl is active.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The calculation of reload time using reload speed is: Reload Time = Base Reload Time ÷ (1 + Reload Speed Bonus)
 • For example, Tigris ' new reload time with a max-ranked Tactical Pump would be 1.8s / (1 + 60%) = 1.125 s

• Sustained DPS is increased by reload speed modifiers at the same rate as magazine size modifiers.

• Can be combined with Chilling Reload and Seeking Fury for a maximum 115% reload speed bonus, granting an approximate 53.4884% reduction of reload time.

 • For example, Tigris ' new reload time with a max-ranked Tactical Pump would be 1.8s / (1 + 60%) = 1.125 s

• Sustained DPS is increased by reload speed modifiers at the same rate as magazine size modifiers.

• Can be combined with Chilling Reload and Seeking Fury for a maximum 115% reload speed bonus, granting an approximate 53.4884% reduction of reload time.

[ edit | edit source ]
 • Originally acquired as a limited-edition reward from participating in The Cicero Crisis (50 points)

• Can be acquired as a possible drop by killing Corrupted Vor in Orokin Void missions level ≥40 such as Mithra and Mot
 • 25% drop chance; it has 100% drop, but shares this with its counterparts - Pistol Pestilence , Virulent Scourge , Malignant Force

• It was also acquired as a drop from Razorback Armada via killing the Razorback; however, this is no longer possible.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Toxic Barrage is additive with similar mods. For example, a Strun with a max rank Toxic Barrage and Shotgun Savvy will have a 20% × (1 + 60% + 90%) = 50% status chance.

• This mod can be combined with Frigid Blast , Shell Shock and Scattering Inferno to achieve a combined status chance bonus of 240%. With Shotgun Savvy , this bonus can be increased to 330%.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Every attack does 25% bonus physical damage (i.e. Impact , Puncture , and Slash )

• The first attack in every combo is a shot.

• Samba Slash consists entirely of firing the gunblade.

• All ranged attacks also deal slam damage to nearby enemies.
 • Slam damage is based on single pellet damage of each gunblade, except for Stropha where it is 800 instead of the shot damage of 700 .
 • There are two types of this Slam damage: Instances that force a Slash consist of pure Puncture , and instances that ragdoll are pure Blast .
 • On Stropha , both types of slam deal pure Impact damage, and it also does not force Slash procs.

• Slam damage is entirely unaffected by mods, except for Seismic Wave .

• The first and third attack of Automatic Rhumba inflict a slam attack with a guaranteed Slash proc. The second and fourth attacks inflict a slam attack that ragdolls enemies.

• The first and fourth attacks of Magnum Mambo inflict a slam attack that ragdolls enemies.

• The first attack of Lead Tango inflicts a slam attack that ragdolls enemies. The second attack inflicts two slams that both have a guaranteed Slash proc.

• The first attack of Samba Slash inflicts a slam attack with a guaranteed Slash proc. The second and third attacks inflict a slam attack that ragdolls enemies.

• All attacks of Full Bore inflict a slam attack that ragdolls enemies.

• The Ground Finisher inflict a slam attack that ragdolls enemies.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under Arbiters of Hexis , or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence , and spending 25,000 Standing 25,000 to purchase.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Has a duration of 20 seconds, after which it will detonate.

• Has a discharge range of 5 meters.

• Discharges every 4 seconds. Every 15 meters traveled, the sphere will discharge to an additional enemy and reduce the discharge timer by 0.25 seconds down to a minimum of 0.25 seconds at 225 meters.

• Critical hits from Coil Recharge's discharge will trigger Rotorswell 's discharge.

• On recall, the Gyratory Sphere will sling itself towards Gyre 's current position, stopping in front of her, facilitating a melee strike to the sphere to fling it at the reticle's direction.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda , or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence , and spending 25,000 Standing 25,000 to purchase.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Upon killing an enemy afflicted by Sow, the effect will be spread to other enemies within 15m of the killed enemy.
 • The spread Sow debuff uses Sow's currently remaining duration, not a fresh timer.

• The damage falloff of spread Sow is still calculated based on the enemy's distance to Sevagoth, not the distance to Sow's epicenter.

• Spread Sow can apply to already Sown enemies, recalculating the ability's damage falloff based on Sevagoth's current distance away.
 • Like normal Sow, spread Sow will only overwrite an existing Sow debuff on an enemy if the new Sow debuff will have a higher duration than the existing one, regardless of whether the new cast potentially has higher ability strength or less damage falloff.

• When casting Death's Harvest via Sevagoth's Shadow , the damage over time effect and duration are determined by the Ability Strength and Ability Duration of the Exalted Shadow, while the spreading range scales solely by the Ability Range on Sevagoth .

 • Like normal Sow, spread Sow will only overwrite an existing Sow debuff on an enemy if the new Sow debuff will have a higher duration than the existing one, regardless of whether the new cast potentially has higher ability strength or less damage falloff.

• When casting Death's Harvest via Sevagoth's Shadow , the damage over time effect and duration are determined by the Ability Strength and Ability Duration of the Exalted Shadow, while the spreading range scales solely by the Ability Range on Sevagoth .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Currently, there are few non-player sources of Radiation. The most common source is laser traps in the Orokin Void .
 • Sortie missions may contain enemies modded with Radiation as well if specified.

• Damage resistance works on both health and shields.
 • Increases effective health/shields against Radiation by 100% ÷ (100% - 45%) = 1.8182x

• Damage resistance bonus is multiplicative with other sources of damage resistance such as Adaptation and Aviator .

Notes
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Currently, there are few non-player sources of Radiation. The most common source is laser traps in the Orokin Void .
 • Sortie missions may contain enemies modded with Radiation as well if specified.

• Damage resistance works on both health and shields.
 • Increases effective health/shields against Radiation by 100% ÷ (100% - 45%) = 1.8182x

• Damage resistance bonus is multiplicative with other sources of damage resistance such as Adaptation and Aviator .

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod can be acquired from Cephalon Simaris for 100,000 Standing 100,000 .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Does not stack, only one bonus at a time.

• Additional Ability Strength is additive.

• Ability Strength bonus affects Warframe Augment Mods .

• Affects Channeled abilities, such as Exalted Blade and Razorwing , for the duration they are active.

• Affects abilities that have a duration for the whole duration. For example, the shadows summoned by Shadows of the Dead keep the increased Ability Strength , even when the shadows are healed/teleported.

• There is a very small delay between picking up an Energy Orb and Energy Conversion triggering, often related to host / client ping.

• Using abilities while standing on an Energy Orb often will not trigger Energy Conversion .

• Health Orbs picked up while using Equilibrium do not provide Ability Strength .

• Despite appearing on the player's HUD as a buff (with the same visual and auditory cue as an Arcane Enhancement triggering), it will not be nullified by Nullifier Crewmen nor by passing through Void Portals in Orokin Sabotage .

• Universal Orbs trigger this mod's effect upon collection.

• Using abilities while standing on an Energy Orb often will not trigger Energy Conversion .

• Health Orbs picked up while using Equilibrium do not provide Ability Strength .

• Despite appearing on the player's HUD as a buff (with the same visual and auditory cue as an Arcane Enhancement triggering), it will not be nullified by Nullifier Crewmen nor by passing through Void Portals in Orokin Sabotage .

• Universal Orbs trigger this mod's effect upon collection.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • An unranked Equilibrium is given upon completion of The Teacher Quest.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Allows for the pickup of orbs while the associated resource pool is full, for the purpose of conversion. For example, if Health is full but Energy is not, a Health Orb will be picked up and converted to energy.

• The final converted restoration is rounded down. For example, with a rank 10 Equilibrium, collecting a small Energy Orb worth 25 Energy will restore 27 Health, rather than 27.5 Health.

• Health converted from Energy Orbs will not heal Companions .

• The effects of Arcane Energize and Energy Pulse do not apply to energy from converted health orbs. Likewise, Arcane Pulse does not apply to health from converted energy orbs.

• Increased Health and/or Energy Orb Effectiveness from Amber Archon Shard , Restorative Bond , or Dagath 's Passive is not taken into account and will only use the orb's base values.

• As of Hotfix 13.9.3.1 (2014-07-11), Equilibrium can be acquired from Mod Packs and Transmutation .

• Equilibrium stacks with Equinox 's passive ability, which converts 10% of Health Orbs into Energy and vice versa, up to a total of 120% efficiency.

• This effect is additive with the Violet Archon Shard bonus of Health pickups give +20% (+30%) Energy. Energy pickups give +20% (+30%) Health.

• Equilibrium (and Violet Archon Shards, by extension) does work with Universal Orbs , adding a bonus replenishment of Health and Energy.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • A max rank Fast Deflection will increase a Warframe's per-second shield recharge rate from 15 + 5% of max shields to 28.5 + 9.5% of max shields.

• Shield regeneration delay reduction is capped at -80%.

• Can be combined with Fortitude for +190% shield recharge and -45% shield recharge delay, Vigilante Vigor for +150% shield recharge and -75% shield recharge delay, or both for a total of +250% shield recharge and -75% shield recharge delay.

• Fast Deflection is useful on Frames with a low health pool and thin armor rating, like Volt , Hildryn , Mag , Gyre or Caliban who rely heavily on shields to avoid damage.

• Reduces the time for the natural shield recharge that brings back the full effectiveness of Shield Gating .
 • Gauss gains shield recharge and reduced shield recharge delay scaling with his Battery; adding Fast Deflection reduces the threshold at which -75% shield recharge delay is reached and makes his shield charge much more quickly, making him very hard to kill especially in tandem with Arcane Aegis .

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The energy increase is applied to the base energy reserve of a Warframe after energy gains from Rank Bonuses are applied, but before flat bonuses such as Arcane Battery or Azure Archon Shards .
 • Energy t o t a l = ( Energy b a s e + Rank Bonuses ) × ( 1 + Flow Multiplier ) + Flat Bonuses
 • Example: A rank 0 Loki has an energy pool of 175. When equipped with a max rank Flow , the Warframe would have 175 × (1 + 100% ) = 350 maximum energy.

• A rank 30 Loki has a base energy pool of 175, plus 50 from rank bonuses, for a total of 225 base energy. When equipped with a max rank Flow , the Warframe would have (175 + 50) × (1 + 100% ) = 450 maximum energy.

• This mod is better than Streamline mod for some purposes.
 • Due to a higher energy pool, abilities can be cast more often. The additional energy allows for more uses than the reduced usage of Streamline.

• The additional energy benefits certain mods (such as Quick Thinking and Rage ).

• The higher energy cap allows toggled duration abilities like Sound Quake and Absorb to last longer.

• However, as the mod simply increases the amount of energy the player can store it isn't beneficial when energy is constantly being used. Players who frequently use abilities should consider Streamline or a combination.

• For specific Warframe base and maximum values, see Warframes Comparison .

sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Killing an enemy with a drop kick will cause enemies within 7 meters to be knocked down.

• Modifiers that do affect Gale Kick:
 • Base damage of the melee weapon.

• Base damage mods ( Pressure Point , Steel Charge , Justice Blades , Energy Channel , Scindo Manticore Skin etc.)
 • Energy Channel's bonus is permanent as long as the player does not swing their actual melee weapon to discharge the bonus damage.

• Furious Javelin

• Having an Exalted melee weapon active will make Gale Kick scale from it, including damage granted by Ability Strength.

• Modifiers that do not affect Gale Kick:
 • Physical or elemental damage mods ( Collision Force , Fever Strike , etc.)

• Faction damage mods ( Smite Corpus etc.)

• Sliding or parkour velocity mods ( Maglev , Endurance Drift etc.)

• Conditional mods ( Healing Return , Condition Overload etc.)

• Shattering Impact

• Void Strike

• Riven Mods that have a base damage boost stat

• Jump kicks normally deal impact damage equal to 20 + 100% melee base damage (including all of the above damage interactions), with Gale Kick increasing the total damage to 20 + 200%

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian , or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil , and spending 25,000 Standing 25,000 to purchase.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Regurgitated enemies bounce up to 3 times, exploding in a 6 meter AoE and leaving a 6 meter gas cloud on each bounce.
 • Explosions and gas clouds do not require Line of Sight to damage enemies.

• The bounce explosions deal the same damage as Regurgitate normally does ( 2000 + 10% of Regurgitated enemy's current HP), but dealt as Gas damage instead of Toxin damage. Each explosion also procs 2 guaranteed Gas statuses, with the final explosion additionally proccing 1 Toxin status.
 • Each explosion deals 75% less damage than the last, dealing 100% of the expected damage on the first explosion, 25% on the second, and 6.25% on the last.

• Explosion damage has no radial falloff, dealing full damage even at the edge of its range.

• Proc damage scales with the damage of the explosion that caused them, dealing 50% of that damage per second for 6 seconds.

• Like Regurgitate , each explosion permanently strips 75% armor from any enemies hit.
 • Armor is stripped before damage is dealt.

• Stacks with each bounce for a maximum of 225% armor strip after 3 bounces, allowing for a full armor strip with as low as 45% ability strength.

• Gas clouds remain for 8 seconds and inflict 250 Gas damage per second with a guaranteed Gas proc on every hit.
 • Gas clouds are not affected by the 75% damage falloff per bounce and will always deal the full 250 damage per second.

• Gas procs from the clouds have 50% reduced duration, only lasting for 3 seconds and dealing 3 ticks of 125 damage before expiring.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod can be acquired from Cephalon Simaris for 100,000 Standing 100,000 .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Picking up Health Orbs will grant 450 armor, stacking up to 3 times, for a maximum Armor bonus of 1350 .
 • The Armor bonus is added to the Warframe's total armor and does not increase the Warframe's base armor.
 • Mods like Steel Fiber that multiply a Warframe's base armor do not increase the effects of Health Conversion, while those that multiply total armor, like Ironclad Charge , do.

• The stacks are shown as glowing orange blocks orbiting the player's Warframe.

• Cogron's Tempered Benison shares its stack limit with Health Conversion, allowing up to 5 motes between the two buffs.

• Universal Orbs will also add an armor stack upon collection.
 • Energy Orbs picked up while using Equilibrium do not provide armor stacks.

• This mod allows Health Orbs to be picked up while at maximum Health and less than 3 stacks are present.

• Taking Health damage will consume a stack after 3 seconds.
 • Only damage dealt to health will consume a stack.

• The 3 second stack consume timer is not affected by Ability Duration .

• Only one stack can be consumed at a time. If additional health damage is taken within the 3-second consume time, no additional stacks will be consumed.

• Health damage from using Garuda 's Bloodletting , Hema 's reload mechanism, or Nekros ' Desecrate with Despoil augment will not consume a stack.

• Health damage from Combat Discipline , however, will consume a stack.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian , or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil , and spending 25,000 Standing 25,000 to purchase.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • When casting Shroud of Dynar , one random enemy within a 25 meter radius will be marked by flowing energy. This enemy receives increased Damage Vulnerability.
 • Targeting radius is not affected by mods.

• Damage Vulnerability is affected by Ability Strength .

• The targeted enemy is highlighted through terrain.

• The energy colour is dependent on the selected Warframe energy colour.

• Upon the death of the targeted enemy, a new enemy will be targeted if Shroud of Dynar's invisibility is still active.
 • A new enemy will not be targeted otherwise, even if Shroud of Dynar's melee buff is still active.

• Casting Fangs of Raksh on the targeted enemy will confer a bonus on its spread radius upon the enemy's death.
 • Spread radius bonus is not affected by mods but is multiplicative with Ability Range mods.
 • For example, using a maxed Stretch and Prey of Dynar results in:
 Total spread radius = Base spread radius × (1 + Prey of Dynar Bonus) × (1 + Range Mods)
 = 7 × (1 + 1.5 ) × 1.45 = 25.375

• Spread radius bonus expires upon the end of Shroud of Dynar.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Stacks with Gladiator Finesse for a total of 300% efficiency.

• Damage taken while Quick Thinking is in effect does not count as taking Health damage; as such, Rage and Hunter Adrenaline do not convert incoming damage into energy, and Vex Armor and Vexing Retaliation do not trigger their effects.

• While under the effects of Quick Thinking, strong hits will cause the Warframe to stagger. Be wary, as repeated strong hits can effectively stun-lock you in place until you die. Weak hits will not induce this effect.
 • Pain Threshold drastically reduces the stagger duration, allowing one to avoid being stun-locked.

• A max rank Primed Sure Footed will not affect the stagger.

• Quick Thinking has the lowest priority of almost all death prevention effects, such as Nidus ' passive or Phoenix Renewal . Quick Thinking will only start draining energy after all other means of preventing lethal damage are exhausted.
 • Wukong 's passive will only trigger once Quick Thinking runs out of energy to drain.

• If the player would take enough damage to lose both all their health and energy, the player will instead enter bleedout immediately and not lose any energy.

• Will not work on Hildryn or Lavos since they do not have energy capacity.

• Rage and Hunter Adrenaline will not replenish energy if a portion of health is lost during a Magnetic proc.

• Will not work if player is in Operator mode while channeled abilities are active.

 • Stacks with Gladiator Finesse for a total of 300% efficiency.

• Damage taken while Quick Thinking is in effect does not count as taking Health damage; as such, Rage and Hunter Adrenaline do not convert incoming damage into energy, and Vex Armor and Vexing Retaliation do not trigger their effects.

• While under the effects of Quick Thinking, strong hits will cause the Warframe to stagger. Be wary, as repeated strong hits can effectively stun-lock you in place until you die. Weak hits will not induce this effect.
 • Pain Threshold drastically reduces the stagger duration, allowing one to avoid being stun-locked.

• A max rank Primed Sure Footed will not affect the stagger.

• Quick Thinking has the lowest priority of almost all death prevention effects, such as Nidus ' passive or Phoenix Renewal . Quick Thinking will only start draining energy after all other means of preventing lethal damage are exhausted.
 • Wukong 's passive will only trigger once Quick Thinking runs out of energy to drain.

• If the player would take enough damage to lose both all their health and energy, the player will instead enter bleedout immediately and not lose any energy.

• Will not work on Hildryn or Lavos since they do not have energy capacity.

• Rage and Hunter Adrenaline will not replenish energy if a portion of health is lost during a Magnetic proc.

• Will not work if player is in Operator mode while channeled abilities are active.

sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Rage and Hunter Adrenaline have the same effect, stacking for a total of 85% damage conversion.

 • The amount of Energy gained from damage taken to Health is affected by Warframe Damage Reduction .

• Applies to Status Effects that deal damage over time and damage that bypasses Shields .

• Damage taken to Overguard only applies energy if the user does not have Shields (such as Inaros and Nidus ).
 • The Overguard must also come from an ally . As such, Kullervo 's Recompense does not receive this benefit despite his lack of shields.

• Warframes whose shields have been removed via subsumed Parasitic Armor (while active), or Arcane Persistence will benefit from energy conversion from Overguard .

• Does not apply to the following sources of damage:
 • Self-inflicted damage, such as Nekros 's Despoil or Combat Discipline .

• Laser traps encountered in the Orokin Void and Orokin Derelicts .

• Health lost due to the Vampire Nightmare Mode modifier, or health drained in Survival missions by falling to 0% life support.

• Energy is gained even with active Channeled Abilities .

• Nourish affects the amount of energy conversion.

 • This mod does not affect knockdown or stagger recovery time.

• Most status effects are reduced to 1.5 seconds. This effectively makes damaging status effects only 1 second. The table below shows the which damage ticks go through for each status type.

• A shield gate duration of more than 1 second will block all damaging status effects from damaging your health, except for Toxin , and during the shield gate invulnerability.

sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • An unranked Redirection is given upon completion of The Teacher Quest.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • The increase to shield capacity provided by Redirection is applied after rank bonuses from levelling up a warframe.
 • For example, if a maxed Redirection is applied to a level 30 Frost Prime , the base shield capacity (550) is increased by the +100 base shield rank bonus, giving 650. This total is multiplied by 2 (+100% Shield bonus from redirection), giving a grand total of 1300 shield capacity.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • For a short time after Update 10.6 (2013-10-30) this mod could have been acquired via Transmutation . This was due to a bug where all mods, including unreleased ones such as this one, became obtainable through transmutation. This has since been fixed, but players who obtained the mod this way were allowed to keep them. There is currently no way to obtain this mod.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Can be acquired by attaining the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence , or the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil , and spending 25,000 Standing 25,000 to purchase.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Cooldown reduction per kill is not affected by mods.

• Unlike the base ability, Dagath will only gain the critical damage bonus provided by Grave Spirit while she is in her spectral form.
 • In addition shields will restore normally while using this ability.

• Subsuming Doom for another ability will prevent players from modifying the Phantom Wrath debuff associated with Doom.

• Grave Spirit only doubles the damage stored by Phantom Wrath when in her physical form. As this augment restricts Dagath exclusively to her spectral form, this synergy is effectively removed.

• With this augment, Grave Spirit can be dispelled, such as Silence from Violence , and immediately forced into cooldown.

• Damaging abilities and weapons will apply Doom to enemies.
 • Abilities that do not deal damage on cast (e.g. Breach Surge ) will not apply the Doom effect on cast.

[ edit | edit source ]
 • Players who complete the Mars Junction task "Purchase a MK1 Weapon from the Market" will be rewarded with an unranked Streamline mod.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Update 8.2 (2013-06-28) changed Streamline mechanics to be a linear reduction in cost.

• It is possible to hit the hardcap of 175% Ability Efficiency thanks to Fleeting Expertise . All Ability Efficiency added beyond 175% is not represented on the UI, though may benefit channeled abilities if your duration is below 100%.

• There is an unreleased Primed version that increases Ability Efficiency up to 55%.

sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • May be stacked with Fortitude for complete immunity to knockdown. Another option is the Vaykor Sydon which provides additional 50% knockdown resistance when wielded.

• Includes resistance from Shield Lancer 's shield attack, Scorpion 's tether attack, Ancient 's charge attack and tether attack, Corpus Laser Barriers , Bombard rocket explosions and Arson / Caustic Eximus' fire blasts.
 • Despite being knockdown resistance, it also provides resistance to Volatile Runner 's explosions which stagger the player, rather than knocking down the player.

• A Primed version of this mod, Primed Sure Footed , is a Daily Tribute reward available at 400, 600, and 900 logins. When maximized, will resist all knockdowns (100% resistance).

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under Arbiters of Hexis , or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence , and spending 25,000 Standing 25,000 to purchase.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Combo Count Cap is not affected by mods.

• The augment requires an active Blaze Artillery , and will pick the closest one in a ?m range (possibly infinite).
 • Artillery that was picked up no longer has its 130 deg frontal cone, allowing it to target all enemies within range.
 • If there is no enemy in sight/range, the artillery will aim where the player aims (with a slight delay).

• If the picked artillery had a combo greater than 20x , it will be set to 20x .

• When Temporal Anchor ends, the artillery will be released and will function as normal (i.e. aiming in a 130 deg cone, and able to go past 20x combo).

• 2 more normal (stationary) Blaze Artillery units can be created.
 • Casting past the max count limit of 3 will replace one of the stationary artilleries.

sections.title: Acquisition

[ edit | edit source ]
 • This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda , or the rank of General under Steel Meridian , and spending 25,000 Standing 25,000 to purchase.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Enemy armor and shields are fully removed at 200% Ability Strength . With one Corrosive Projection , armor will be fully stripped at 164% Ability Strength.
 • Shield strip removes a percentage of the current maximum value, as such it has diminishing returns on subsequent casts against the same enemies. Armor strip, however, is a percentage of an enemy's total, requiring only two casts at base strength to fully strip.

• Defenses will only be stripped once enemies are crushed by both walls.

Drop Locations

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Upon completion of The Duviri Paradox and Angels of the Zariman , and unlocking The Steel Path , players can earn Atomos Incarnon Genesis from The Circuit 's Steel Path to place on their Atomos with the help of Cavalero in the Chrysalith . This Incarnon Genesis form grants it evolutions to a transformation and newfound strength.

• Purchased from Cavalero for 120 Platinum 120 , complete with the resources used in its installation. This purchase can only be made once .

sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Aya can be acquired from Bounties and missions in the Void , and occasionally appears as a Relic Pack reward as well.

 For more details, please check the subpage of Aya/Drops for an automatically generated list.

sections.title: Usage
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 All Faction Syndicates ( Steel Meridian , Arbiters of Hexis , Cephalon Suda , The Perrin Sequence , Red Veil , and New Loka ) require a sacrifice of 2 and 3 Aya to rank up to Rank 4 and Rank 5 respectively.

 Aya can be used to purchase the following Void Relics and other items from Varzia in Maroo's Bazaar .

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Bapholite can be acquired from three different sources:

 • Mining yellow mineral veins in the Cambion Drift.

• Storage containers found in the Bonus vaults found during Isolation Vault bounties .

• Infested Rolizor Cysts on the Cambion Drift can house Bapholites within.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • They can be traded at Otak in exchange for Otak Token s in quantities of 7 to 25.

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: Fishing CODEX As ravenous as the Infestation itself, this fish feeds on the remains of others. It shuns the light, preferring an enclosed cave environment. Barbisteo is an infested fish in the Cambion Drift. Biome: Cave Activity: Fass/Vome Maximum Weight: 16 kg Rarity: Common Small Medium Large Benign Infested Tumor x3 Ferment Bladder x3 Dendrite Blastoma x1 Benign Infested Tumor x4 Ferment Bladder x4 Dendrite Blastoma x1 Benign Infested Tumor x8 Ferment Bladder x6 Dendrite Blastoma x1 Trivia Chondricord, Vitreospina and Barbisteo respectively appear to be the pure, hybrid and fully infested version of the same creature. Barbisteo however, does not give the player a Spinal Core Section Barbisteo Trophy “A trophy of a prized catch.” —In-Game Description Barbisteo Trophy is a Fishing Trophy Decoration that can be crafted and displayed in the player's Orbiter. The blueprint can be purchased from Daughter for 1,500 Standing 1,500, requiring the rank of Stranger with the Entrati. Manufacturing Requirements 5,000 1 25 30 1,800 Time: 1 hrs Rush: 10 Platinum 10 Market Price: N/A Blueprints Price: 1,500 Gallery Concept art Patch History Update 29.0 (2020-08-25) Introduced. Last updated: Update 29.0 (2020-08-25)

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: “Everybody knows there's nothing like a delicious slice of Big Bytes Pizza. And it's perfect, just before On-Lyne hits the stage. My favorite bite? Pepperoni with extra cheese, of course. What's yours? Let's connect, over a slice of Big Bytes Pizza!” —Zeke from On-Lyne Big Bytes Pizza is a popular pizza chain in Höllvania. 1999 ARG On the official site, near the bottom of the page where the three images are located, you can see a tomato sticking out from behind the rightmost image. Clicking on the tomato produces a sound, and clicking anywhere after that will throw and splatter a tomato centered on where you clicked. Doing so on the image of a pizza slice near the top of the menu (The one with five pepperoni slices on it) will reveal an image depicting the molecular structure Guanine (G), one of the four bases for DNA. Images located at the bottom of the Big Bytes Pizza website. Note the tomato sticking out from under the rightmost image. The pizza image from the Big Bytes Pizza website. Splattered tomatoes reveal an image depicting the molecular structure for Guanine. Trivia The official site includes the text and a phone number, "Call to order today! 1-800-555-2983". When this number is dialed on the phone in the site for the City Wide Metro, it will play a jingle from the song "Tarantella Napoletana", which is associated with Italy and pizza in popular media.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Biotic Filters can be obtained by filleting Duroid and Aquapulmo (both from the Cambion Drift ) at Daughter in the Necralisk .

• Biotic Filters can also be bought from Daughter from the "Daily Special" in exchange for Platinum . Note that the "Daily Special" wares are rotating, and Biotic Filters may not be available.

• Biotic Filters can also be obtained through the Requiem Pylons scattered throughout the Cambion Drift .

• Biotic Filters can also be found within Auroron Entrati Caskets that spawn on structures such as Requiem Pylons.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • They can be traded at Daughter in exchange for Daughter Tokens in quantities of 1 to 8.

 • Upon completion of The Duviri Paradox and Angels of the Zariman , and unlocking The Steel Path , players can earn Bo Incarnon Genesis from The Circuit 's Steel Path to place on their Bo with the help of Cavalero in the Chrysalith . This Incarnon Genesis form grants it evolutions to a transformation and newfound strength.

• Purchased from Cavalero for 120 Platinum 120 , complete with the resources used in its installation. This purchase can only be made once .

 • Upon completion of The Duviri Paradox and Angels of the Zariman , and unlocking The Steel Path , players can earn Boar Incarnon Genesis from The Circuit 's Steel Path to place on their Boar with the help of Cavalero in the Chrysalith . This Incarnon Genesis form grants it evolutions to a transformation and newfound strength.

• Purchased from Cavalero for 120 Platinum 120 , complete with the resources used in its installation. This purchase can only be made once .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Due to its standardized fabrication the boot is the only "fish" without size and randomized weight, always being exactly 4.0kg.

• Instead of the usual uplifting music track accompanying each catch, a boot will be presented with a sarcastic banjo jingle playing in the back:

 Track 1:

 Track 2:

 Track 3:

 • Even though the player only catches one boot, as a reward they would still get a pair and will both remain in the inventory. This was later fixed.

sections.text: I want more... Operator, please hurry! This story is getting quite good!This article is a stub. You can help WARFRAME Wiki by adding a sentence or two. Please do not use this article for critical in-game information!This article is archived. The content was relevant to an obsolete version of Warframe, and has since been removed, retrieved, and/or revamped. Time is relative. Choose when ready.This article refers to valid content that is currently unavailable to be acquired in the live version of the game.It may return to be available again in a future update or event. “A rare bacterial entity that increases the spread of Infestation found on Infested Ships.” —In-Game Description Bracoid was a rare resource that can be found in any Proxima, primarily in Veil Proxima missions. It was used in upgrading the Railjack. Blueprints Requiring Bracoid Click to view the Blueprints requiring Bracoid Blueprint Type Quantity(Research) Lavan Apoc Mk III Ship Turret 16 Lavan Carcinnox Mk III Ship Turret 16 Lavan Cryophon Mk III Ship Turret 16 Lavan Engines Mk III Ship Component 16 Lavan Photor Mk III Ship Turret 16 Lavan Pulsar Mk III Ship Turret 16 Lavan Reactor Mk III Ship Component 16 Lavan Shield Array Mk III Ship Component 16 Squad Health Restore (Large) x100 Gear (6) Total 128 (+6) Research x1 x3 x10 x30 x100 Last updated: Hotfix 29.9.1 (2021-02-24) Gathering Tips Trivia Patch History Update 29.10 (2021-03-19) Removed from drop tables. We wanted to reduce the number of Resources we introduced in our original Railjack launch. They'll remain in your Inventory as we’ll be looking at possibly reintroducing them at a later date. All Blueprints that required these items have been updated to reflect viable Resources, and respective drops have been swapped to Ticor Plate where appropriate. These Resources have also been removed from Helminths Bile Feeding menu. If you spot any mentions of these Resources that we might have missed please let us know! For Tenno who have partially contributed these Resources to Wreckage, the percentage of your contribution will be honoured for the Resource that replaces it. Ex: If you contributed 60% of the needed Copernics for a Carcinnox, that 60% contribution will be carried over when the Blueprints are updated. Update 27.0 (2019-12-13) Introduced.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Eidolon Teralysts and Eidolon Gantulysts will drop one Brilliant Eidolon Shard when captured at its final stage with a charged Eidolon Lure .
 • Eidolon Gantulyst requires a Brilliant Eidolon Shard to summon that will be returned when captured.

• Eidolon Hydrolysts will drop two Brilliant Eidolon Shards when captured at its final stage with a charged Eidolon Lure.
 • Eidolon Hydrolyst requires a Brilliant Eidolon Shard to summon that will be returned when captured.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • In order to unbind a way-bound node, the node must be maxed out beforehand, and players must spend (as of Update 31.5) 750,000 Focus points along with the Brilliant Eidolon Shard to unbind it.
 • Unbinding a way-bound node allows the ability to be used regardless of what Focus school is set as the Primary school.

• A total of 10 shards is required to unbind all the current nodes.

• Brilliant Eidolon Shards can be converted into 25,000 Focus in the Orbiter 's Transference Room . To do so, players can go to Focus Conversion to the lower right corner of the screen when viewing an unlocked school's ability tree, then selecting how many Shards they want to redeem.
 • The Focus points provided by converting these does not affect the daily cap, allowing players to continue gaining Focus beyond the daily cap.

• Necessary in order to summon Eidolon Gantulysts and Eidolon Hydrolysts.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Nora Night will alert the player as the Glass Fissures spawn.

• Fissures can also spawn in open-world areas such as the Plains of Eidolon and Orb Vallis and the on-foot sections of the Empyrean game mode.
 • Glass Fissures do not spawn during Void Fissures .

• Most Bosses , including Kuva Liches , cannot be vitrified.

• Breaking the glass clusters covering Cephalite enemies will cause them to become vulnerable.

• Cephalites will explode shortly after death, dealing Slash damage to all units within range (both Tenno and enemies).

• Resonace drops are not affected by Resource Boosters or Resource Drop Chance Boosters .
 • However, they are affected by loot abilities ( Desecrate , Prowl , Retrieve ) and Warframe Augment Mods ( Ore Gaze , Pilfering Swarm , Pilfering Strangledome ).

• 5 Cephalite Resonance is consumed instantly when used to investigate the energy resonance in the crime scene.

• The player cannot carry more than 20 Cephalite Resonances.

This Incarnon Genesis form grants it evolutions to a transformation and newfound strength.

• Purchased from Cavalero for 120 Platinum 120 , complete with the resources used in its installation. This purchase can only be made once .

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Charc Electroplax are acquired by cutting up Charc Eels caught through Fishing . Go to Fisher Hai-Luk in Cetus, select the desired amount of Charc Eels available, and select the " Cut Fish " option to extract the components. Each Charc Eel cut yields one Charc Electroplax.

 Alternatively, Fisher Hai-Luk can sell Charc Electroplax in bundles of 20 among her rotating stock of " Daily Specials ", which can be bought for Platinum .

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: Fishing CODEX A triumph of Orokin bio-engineering, these hardy fish have adapted to the Deimos environment and seem oddly resistant to Infestation. They thrive in cave environments. Chondricord is an uncorrupted Orokin fish in the Cambion Drift. Biome: Cave (Hotspot) Activity: Fass/Vome Vome Residue recommended. Maximum Points: 8 Rarity: Uncommon Basic Adorned Magnificent Benign Infested Tumor x3 Tubercular Gill System x2 Spinal Core Section x2 Benign Infested Tumor x4 Tubercular Gill System x3 Spinal Core Section x2 Benign Infested Tumor x8 Tubercular Gill System x5 Spinal Core Section x2 Trivia Chondricord, Vitreospina and Barbisteo respectively appear to be the pure, hybrid and fully infested version of the same creature. Barbisteo however, does not give the player a Spinal Core Section. Chondricord Trophy “A trophy of a prized catch.” —In-Game Description Chondricord Trophy is a Fishing Trophy Decoration that can be crafted and displayed in the player's Orbiter. The blueprint can be purchased from Daughter for 5,000 Standing 5,000, requiring the rank of Associate with the Entrati. Manufacturing Requirements 5,000 1 25 30 1 Time: 1 hrs Rush: 10 Platinum 10 Market Price: N/A Blueprints Price: 5,000 Gallery Concept art Patch History Update 29.0 (2020-08-25) Introduced. Last updated: Update 29.0 (2020-08-25)

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: “A soft, malleable metal.” —In-Game Description Coprun is a common resource acquired through mining red ore veins on the Plains of Eidolon. Its main use is in the creation of Coprite Alloy. Item Checklist for Coprun Item Type Quantity Coprite Alloy x20 Resource 20 All blueprints requiring Coprite Alloy Resource 1,035 "The Copper Kid" Honoria Honoria 200 Last updated: Update 41.0 (2025-12-10) Trivia Based on its appearance and description as soft and malleable, Coprun appears to be the Orokin or Ostron name for raw copper. The name is based in the Latin word for copper, which is "cuprum".

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Cranial Foremounts can be obtained by filleting Myxostomata through Daughter in the Necralisk .

• Cranial Foremounts can also be bought from Daughter from the "Daily Special" in exchange for Platinum . Note that the "Daily Special" wares are rotating, and Cranial Foremount may not be available.

• Cranial Foremounts can also be obtained through the Requiem Obelisks scattered throughout the Cambion Drift . (This method would be more suitable for those who can't obtain Myxostomata , as Processed Vome Residue, the bait needed to acquire Myxostomata , requires rank 4 in the Entrati family, while the Requiem Obelisks do not require rank 4)

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: “Excess circuitry disposed of during the Razorback manufacturing process, these components only appear in the vicinity of high level outer-orbit Corpus facilities after a Razorback Armada has been launched.” —In-Game Description Cryptographic ALU is a special resource, used as the key component for crafting the Razorback Cipher. It is only available during the Razorback Armada event, dropping from Corpus Archwing enemies on Jupiter and Neptune or rarely from Corpus ground enemies in Corpus Ship segments of Empyrean missions. Item Checklist Foundry Crafting Item Type Cryptographic ALU Razorback Cipher Gear 4 Last updated: Hotfix 38.0.12 (2025-02-20) Farming Locations These are based on opinions and may not be 100% true. These should be viewed as advice for finding the resource until better facts are proven. Note that this resource is only available in these nodes during a Razorback Armada event. Target Planet Name Type Level Tile Set Jupiter Galilea Sabotage 15 - 20 Corpus Ship (Archwing) Neptune Salacia Mobile Defense 27 - 32 Corpus Ship (Archwing) Gathering Tips Your thirst for knowledge is as insatiable as mine.These farming locations and tips are based on opinions and may not be 100% true. These should be viewed as advice and not critical facts, and results may vary from player to player. Salacia, Neptune is the recommended node for gathering Cryptographic ALU, yielding approximately 12+ per run without a Resource Booster. Utilizing Itzal's third ability, Cosmic Crush, allows easy acquisition of ALU as it pulls in pickups to your location while killing enemies. When dropped by enemies, Cryptographic ALUs look identical to Omega Isotopes. Due to their dark color, this can make them difficult to make out against the backdrop of space. The Steel Path's resource drop chance bonus increases Cryptographic ALU's drop chance. Equipping a Beast companion and the mod Resourceful Retriever will increase the chance of doubling Cryptographic ALU by 18% Trivia ALU stands for Arithmetic Logic Unit, an electronic circuit that is a fundamental building block in many computing circuits.

evidence.excerpt: “Excess circuitry disposed of during the Razorback manufacturing process, these components only appear in the vicinity of high level outer-orbit Corpus facilities after a Razorback Armada has been launched.” —In-Game Description Cryptographic ALU is a special resource, used as the key component for crafting the Razorback Cipher. It is only available during the Razorback Armada event, dropping from Corpus Archwing enemies on Jupiter and Neptune or rarely from Corpus ground enemies in Corpus Ship segments of Empyrean missions. sections.title: Article body
sections.text: Fishing CODEX Formerly Orokin scavengers, these fish succumbed to the Infestation long ago and now greedily feast on floating spores. They are found all over the surface of Deimos. Cryptosuctus is an infested fish in the Cambion Drift. Biome: Surface Activity: Fass Maximum Weight: 16 kg Rarity: Common Small Medium Large Benign Infested Tumor x2 Ferment Bladder x2 Pustulent Cognitive Nodule x1 Benign Infested Tumor x4 Ferment Bladder x3 Pustulent Cognitive Nodule x1 Benign Infested Tumor x6 Ferment Bladder x4 Pustulent Cognitive Nodule x1 Trivia Myxostomata, Ostimyr and Cryptosuctus respectively appear to be the pure, hybrid and fully infested version of the same creature. Cryptosuctus Trophy “A trophy of a prized catch.” —In-Game Description Cryptosuctus Trophy is a Fishing Trophy Decoration that can be crafted and displayed in the player's Orbiter. The blueprint can be purchased from Daughter for 1,500 Standing 1,500, requiring the rank of Stranger with the Entrati. Manufacturing Requirements 5,000 1 25 30 1,800 Time: 1 hrs Rush: 10 Platinum 10 Market Price: N/A Blueprints Price: 1,500 Gallery Concept art Patch History Update 29.0 (2020-08-25) Introduced. Last updated: Update 29.0 (2020-08-25)

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Curse of Hearing can be obtained by:

 • Defeating The Fragmented Zelator boss in Effervo , Deimos during the Operation; which will drop 1 Curse of Hearing , or 2 on Steel Path .

• Defeating The Fragmented One superboss in Effervo , Deimos during the Operation; which will drop 2 Curse of Hearing .

sections.title: Usage
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 This section is transcluded from Operation: Gargoyle's Cry § Curse Rewards . To change it, please edit the transcluded page .
 The Curse of Knowing , Curse of Seeing , and Curse of Hearing could be contributed to a clan on a weekly basis by donating them to the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle in the Dojo.

 As with previous events, the Jahu Gargoyle also doubled as the event's trophy, and every weekly requirement of curses reached would increase the tier of the Gargoyle as follows:

 | Gold Trophy

 | 12 (4x each curse) |

 |

 | 36 (12x each curse) |

 | 72 (24x each curse)
 |

 | 30 (10x each curse) |
 | 60 (20x each curse) |
 | 90 (30x each curse) |
 | 120 (40x each curse)
 |

 | 180 (60x each curse) |
 | 270 (90x each curse) |
 | 360 (120x each curse)
 |

 | Moon |
 | 300 (100x each curse) |
 | 600 (200x each curse) |

 | 1200 (400x each curse)

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 Cuthol Tendrils are acquired by cutting up Cuthol caught through Fishing . Go to Fisher Hai-Luk , select the desired amount of Cuthol available, and select the " Cut Bait " option to extract the components. Each Cuthol cut yields one Cuthol Tendril.

 Alternatively, Fisher Hai-Luk can sell Cuthol Tendrils among her rotating stock of " Daily Specials ", which can be bought with Platinum .

 Cuthol Tendril was also made available from Nakak in Cetus during Operation: Plague Star for 750 Standing 750 Operational Supply Standing and 1,500 Credits 1,500 , with each purchase giving one unit of Cuthol Tendril.

sections.text: Fishing CODEX A grotesque crustacean that produces a potent, paralyzing toxin from its tentacles. Cuthol is a rare species of fish in the Plains of Eidolon. Biome: Pond (hotspot) Activity: Nocturnal (Night time) Cuthol Bait needed. Effective Spears: Lanzo/Ebisu/Spari Maximum Weight: 40 kg Rarity: Rare Small Medium Large 500 Standing 500 625 Standing 625 1,000 Standing 1,000 Fish Meat x5 Fish Scales x3 Fish Oil x2 Cuthol Tendrils x1 Fish Meat x8 Fish Scales x4 Fish Oil x3 Cuthol Tendrils x1 Fish Meat x11 Fish Scales x5 Fish Oil x4 Cuthol Tendrils x1 Cuthol Trophy “A trophy of a prized catch.” —In-Game Description Cuthol Trophy is a Fishing Trophy Decoration that can be crafted and displayed in the player's Orbiter. The blueprint can be purchased from Fisher Hai-Luk for 10,000 Standing 10,000, requiring the rank of Surah with the Ostron. sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Upon completion of The Duviri Paradox and Angels of the Zariman , and unlocking The Steel Path , players can earn Dera Incarnon Genesis from The Circuit 's Steel Path to place on their Dera with the help of Cavalero in the Chrysalith . This Incarnon Genesis form grants it evolutions to a transformation and newfound strength.

• Purchased from Cavalero for 120 Platinum 120 , complete with the resources used in its installation. This purchase can only be made once .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Incarnon transformation doesn't override equipped skins.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Clouds do not force toxin procs, nor will they proc toxin if the toxin damage is changed to something else.

• Clouds cannot proc physical status effects like Impact , Puncture , and Slash .

• Status effects procced by the clouds can heal with Healing Return .

• Enemies killed by clouds can spawn another cloud, and repeat the process of chaining clouds.

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: Fishing CODEX A living fossil hearkening back to the Orokin era, this elusive fish hides below the surface of Exocrine pools in the deepest caverns it can find. Duroid is an uncorrupted Orokin fish in the Cambion Drift. Biome: Cave (Hotspot) Activity: Fass/Vome Vome Residue recommended. Maximum Points: 8 Rarity: Uncommon Basic Adorned Magnificent Benign Infested Tumor x2 Tubercular Gill System x2 Biotic Filter x2 Benign Infested Tumor x4 Tubercular Gill System x4 Biotic Filter x2 Benign Infested Tumor x6 Tubercular Gill System x6 Biotic Filter x2 Trivia Duroid, Aquapulmo and Kymaeros respectively appear to be the pure, hybrid and fully infested version of the same creature. Duroid Trophy “A trophy of a prized catch.” —In-Game Description Duroid Trophy is a Fishing Trophy Decoration that can be crafted and displayed in the player's Orbiter. The blueprint can be purchased from Daughter for 5,000 Standing 5,000, requiring the rank of Associate with the Entrati. Manufacturing Requirements 5,000 1 25 30 1 Time: 1 hrs Rush: 10 Platinum 10 Market Price: N/A Blueprints Price: 5,000 Gallery Concept art Patch History Update 29.0 (2020-08-25) Introduced. Last updated: Update 29.0 (2020-08-25)

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: CODEX This robust, carnivorous plant has adapted to conditions in polluted waters. Dusklight Sarracenias are Plants found only in Grineer Shipyards, which are most commonly found on Ceres within pools of toxic waste. Usage Checklist for Dusklight Sarracenia Item Type Quantity Nightfall Apothic Gear 2 Last updated: Hotfix 38.0.12 (2025-02-20) Farming Locations These are based on opinions and may not be 100% true. These should be viewed as advice for finding the resources until better facts are proven. Target Planet Name Type Level Tile Set Ceres Pallas Exterminate 12 - 14 Grineer Shipyard Ceres Ker Sabotage 14 - 16 Grineer Shipyard Gathering Tips Oxylus' Botanist will automatically loot plants within range. Pallas, Ceres will always spawn two of these in the green water next to spawn. Ker, Ceres has a guaranteed large reactor room that will spawn around 4 of these. Trivia Sarracenia is a genus of Pitcher plants commonly known as Trumpet Pitchers, and are endemic to the North American continent. It is unknown if the Dusklight Sarracenia has any biological relation to the Lunar Pitcher. Amaryn's hint about the location of this flower mentions it grows in "100% filth", which she concludes by wondering what kind of flower grows in those conditions. This is a likely reference to darkSector, wherein a character describes the Lotus flower as a "rare beauty [that] blooms among filth and decay". Media Dusklight Sarracenia in the Codex. The location on the tile where Dusklight Sarracenia spawns. A Dusklight Sarracenia near extraction. Another Dusklight Sarracenia. 3 Dusklight Sarracenias found along the toxic waste stream to the right of Oberon and with the last being at the source of the flow. Dusklight can be found often near extraction on the Vor and Lech Kril assassination mission on Ceres Dusklight Sarracenia location in the spawn Patch History Update 39.0 (2025-06-25) Players will no longer have to scan plants to earn their extracts. Now, plants like Moonlight Dragonlily and Ruk’s Claw will have a “Hold to Collect” context action to pick them up in-mission. Oxylus’ Botanist Precept has been changed to automatically pick any plants within 50m over 2s (at max rank). This also applies to the Silver Grove Quest, which requires players to collect certain plant resources to progress. The objective descriptions have been updated to specify “gathering” instead of “scanning”. The scanning mechanic was overly complicated for new players, so this simplification should benefit Plant Extract gathering for Tenno across the board. Update 32.3 (2023-02-15) Fixed being unable to scan a Dusklight Sarracenia found in the Grineer Shipyard Tileset. Update: The Silver Grove (2016-08-19) Introduced.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Echo Voca can be found scattered across any Albrecht's Laboratories mission as an interactable pickup.

• Killing Gruzzlings have a chance to leave Echo Voca pickups.

• Tier 5 Bounties from Fibonacci have a chance to award an Echo Voca.

• Every 8 hours, one Echo Voca can be purchased from Loid using resources found in the Albrecht's Laboratories.

• Killing Gruzzlings have a chance to leave Echo Voca pickups.

• Tier 5 Bounties from Fibonacci have a chance to award an Echo Voca.

• Every 8 hours, one Echo Voca can be purchased from Loid using resources found in the Albrecht's Laboratories.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 After Vome kills Fass to begin the Vome cycle on the Cambion Drift , small zones of orange energy will appear in various locations. Entering a zone will cover the edges of the screen in orange sludge. Multiple deposits of Fass Residue will spawn inside each zone and can be destroyed to acquire 1 Fass Residue each.

 Note that if one stays in the Cambion Drift for long enough, there may be Fass Residue on the map even though Fass is active.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: [ edit | edit source ]

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Amount yielded is not affected by Resource Booster s, Loyal Retriever , or Prosperous Retriever .

• Resource deposits are not marked (e.g. Thief's Wit ).

• 6-12 Wyrm Residue, depending on squad size, (either Fass Residue or Vome Residue ) are needed to activate the bait machine in the first stage of the Isolation Vault bounty. Existing Residue will despawn, and new Residue will respawn for the players to collect.

• They can be traded at Father in exchange for Father Token s in quantities of 6 to 12. Curiously, Father will never ask for Residue in Token bundles that reward two or fewer tokens.

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: Fishing CODEX These specimens were originally parasites, breeding inside the wyrm-guts of Fass and scattered across Deimos in the debris of his explosive demise. They are drawn to his light. Flagellocanth is a rare Infested fish which can be found in the Cambion Drift. Biome: Surface (Hotspot) Best spot: 'The 'Actuary's Pride' (While Fass is up) and "Cerebrum Magna" (While Vome is up) Activity: Fass/Vome Processed Fass Residue recommended. Maximum Weight: 16 kg Rarity: Rare Small Medium Large Benign Infested Tumor x3 Ferment Bladder x3 Ocular Stem-Root x1 Benign Infested Tumor x5 Ferment Bladder x5 Ocular Stem-Root x1 Benign Infested Tumor x8 Ferment Bladder x6 Ocular Stem-Root x1 Flagellocanth Trophy “A trophy of a prized catch.” —In-Game Description Flagellocanth Trophy is a Fishing Trophy Decoration that can be crafted and displayed in the player's Orbiter. The blueprint can be purchased from Daughter for 7,500 Standing 7,500, requiring the rank of Friend with the Entrati. Manufacturing Requirements 5,000 1 25 30 1,100 Time: 1 hrs Rush: 10 Platinum 10 Market Price: N/A Blueprints Price: 7,500 Gallery Concept art Patch History Hotfix 29.6.5 (2021-01-09) Adjusted Flagellocanth hit box to allow for easier targeting. Fixed Flagellocanth caught scenes having incorrect positioning. Hotfix 29.5.5 (2020-11-27) Fixed Luminous Dye not highlighting the Flagellocanth. Update 29.5 (2020-11-19) Introduced.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Blueprint drops from certain Relics . (1x from Common and 2x from Uncommon)

• Purchased blueprint from Acrithis 's shop for 10 Pathos Clamps .

• Blueprint received as a Daily Tribute .

• Found very rarely in Rare or Reinforced Storage Containers in Zariman Ten Zero , Void , and Lua .
 • One rare storage container is guaranteed from the Lua Music Puzzle .

• Sometimes acquired from the 1999 Calendar .

• Three-Forma bundles were rewarded at certain ranks from Nightwave .
 • Ranks 7 and 27 from Series 1: The Wolf of Saturn Six

• Rank 9 from Intermission I

• Ranks 8 and 25 from Series 2: The Emissary

• Rank 22 from Intermission II

• Ranks 8 and 25 from Series 3: The Glassmaker

• Rank 26 from Intermission III

• Ranks 7, 12, and 27 from Nora's Choice

• Ranks 7 and 27 from Nora's Mix Volume 1

• Ranks 7 and 27 from Nora's Mix Volume 2

• Purchased complete from Nakak in Cetus during Operation: Plague Star for 3,000 Standing 3,000 Operational Supply Standing and 5,000 Credits 5,000 .

• Purchased complete from the Market for 20 Platinum 20

• Purchased complete from the Market as a three-Forma bundle for 35 Platinum 35 .

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Making the most of Forma means remembering the Polarities of the mods the player wishes to use. For example, weapons almost always need damage mods, such as Serration ; Serration has Madurai polarity. Therefore, it would be useful to add a Madurai polarity to any weapon that does not already have it. Consider reading the Polarization page for more information about this process.

• A Forma is required to craft nearly all Clan Research weapons, though this is most likely only for gameplay reasons as most of them don't involve any Orokin technology.

• All syndicates require Forma to advance to Rank 2, as well as in order to become neutral if the Tenno has fallen to the first negative rank.

• A Warframe, Archwing , or weapon that has been polarized at least once can have their Secondary Energy Color customized.

• Oddly enough, players can polarize Aura mod slots with the Unairu polarity, despite there currently being no Aura mods with that polarity.
 • Similarly, players can also polarize mod slots belonging to non-melee weapons with said polarity as well.

• For more flexibility it is possible to use an Omni Forma , which grants as a universal polarity.

• Players cannot add Umbra polarity to slots using mundane Forma. Existing Umbra polarities can be replaced with others, but after that, they can only be restored by using an Umbra Forma .

Notes
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Making the most of Forma means remembering the Polarities of the mods the player wishes to use. For example, weapons almost always need damage mods, such as Serration ; Serration has Madurai polarity. Therefore, it would be useful to add a Madurai polarity to any weapon that does not already have it. Consider reading the Polarization page for more information about this process.

• A Forma is required to craft nearly all Clan Research weapons, though this is most likely only for gameplay reasons as most of them don't involve any Orokin technology.

• All syndicates require Forma to advance to Rank 2, as well as in order to become neutral if the Tenno has fallen to the first negative rank.

• A Warframe, Archwing , or weapon that has been polarized at least once can have their Secondary Energy Color customized.

• Oddly enough, players can polarize Aura mod slots with the Unairu polarity, despite there currently being no Aura mods with that polarity.
 • Similarly, players can also polarize mod slots belonging to non-melee weapons with said polarity as well.

• For more flexibility it is possible to use an Omni Forma , which grants as a universal polarity.

• Players cannot add Umbra polarity to slots using mundane Forma. Existing Umbra polarities can be replaced with others, but after that, they can only be restored by using an Umbra Forma .

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: I want more... Operator, please hurry! This story is getting quite good!This article is a stub. You can help WARFRAME Wiki by adding a sentence or two. Please do not use this article for critical in-game information!This article is archived. The content was relevant to an obsolete version of Warframe, and has since been removed, retrieved, and/or revamped. Time is relative. Choose when ready.This article refers to valid content that is currently unavailable to be acquired in the live version of the game.It may return to be available again in a future update or event. “A rare Orokin material used to create a tear in space and allow safe passage through the Void.” —In-Game Description Fresnels were a rare resource that can be found in any Empyrean mission. They were used in upgrading the Railjack and for Protea's Mavv Helmet. Blueprints Requiring Fresnels Click to view the Blueprints requiring Fresnels Blueprint Type Quantity(Research) Protea Mavv Helmet Cosmetic 4 Squad Energy Restore (Large) x100 Gear (5) Vidar Apoc Mk III Ship Turret 16 Vidar Carcinnox Mk III Ship Turret 16 Vidar Cryophon Mk III Ship Turret 16 Vidar Engines Mk III Ship Component 16 Vidar Photor Mk III Ship Turret 16 Vidar Pulsar Mk III Ship Turret 16 Vidar Reactor Mk III Ship Component 16 Vidar Shield Array Mk III Ship Component 16 Zetki Apoc Mk III Ship Turret 20 Zetki Carcinnox Mk III Ship Turret 20 Zetki Cryophon Mk III Ship Turret 20 Zetki Engines Mk III Ship Component 20 Zetki Photor Mk III Ship Turret 20 Zetki Pulsar Mk III Ship Turret 20 Zetki Reactor Mk III Ship Component 20 Zetki Shield Array Mk III Ship Component 20 Total 292 (+5) Research x1 x3 x10 x30 x100 Last updated: Hotfix 29.9.1 (2021-02-24) Gathering Tips Since this item can be found in any area, it would be best to farm it like any other resource. It has been suggested to go to Posit Cluster on Earth Proxima, which would be the easiest mission. Trivia The name and description of this resource refers to the Fresnel lens, a special lens used primarily in lighthouses and theaters for the purpose of bending light into a straight beam, without using too much glass during production. Patch History Update 29.10 (2021-03-19) Removed from drop tables. We wanted to reduce the number of Resources we introduced in our original Railjack launch. They'll remain in your Inventory as we’ll be looking at possibly reintroducing them at a later date. All Blueprints that required these items have been updated to reflect viable Resources, and respective drops have been swapped to Ticor Plate where appropriate. These Resources have also been removed from Helminths Bile Feeding menu. If you spot any mentions of these Resources that we might have missed please let us know! For Tenno who have partially contributed these Resources to Wreckage, the percentage of your contribution will be honoured for the Resource that replaces it. Ex: If you contributed 60% of the needed Copernics for a Carcinnox, that 60% contribution will be carried over when the Blueprints are updated. Update 27.0 (2019-12-13) Introduced.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text:
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Upon completion of The Duviri Paradox and Angels of the Zariman , and unlocking The Steel Path , players can earn Furax Incarnon Genesis from The Circuit 's Steel Path to place on their Furax with the help of Cavalero in the Chrysalith . This Incarnon Genesis form grants it evolutions to a transformation and newfound strength.

• Purchased from Cavalero for 120 Platinum 120 , complete with the resources used in its installation. This purchase can only be made once .

evidence.excerpt:
Acquisition
[ edit | edit source ]
 • Upon completion of The Duviri Paradox and Angels of the Zariman , and unlocking The Steel Path , players can earn Furax Incarnon Genesis from The Circuit 's Steel Path to place on their Furax with the help of Cavalero in the Chrysalith . This Incarnon Genesis form grants it evolutions to a transformation and newfound strength.

• Purchased from Cavalero for 120 Platinum 120 , complete with the resources used in its installation. This purchase can only be made once .

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: This page redirects from Imprint. For the lore fragments, see Synthesis#Imprints. A Genetic Code Template is a medium used for saving genetic information of a living companion. Pet imprints can be traded or used for a combined incubation. Imprints are primarily used as "parents" for a baby pet. By applying two Genetic Code Templates before the incubation phase, the traits of each imprint will be passed onto the new Kubrow or Kavat. Note that imprints do not substitute the normally associated components for incubating a beast; it will still cost you a Kubrow Egg or 10 Kavat Genetic Code on top of the imprints. Players can carry up to a maximum of 200 stored imprints. Market and Manufacturing The blueprint can be bought for 50,000 Credits 50,000 and is re-usable indefinitely on all platforms. A crafted Genetic Code Template can also be bought on the Market for 15 Platinum 15. Manufacturing Requirements 10,000 700 300 1 Time: 6 hrs Rush: 5 Platinum 5 Market Price: 15 Blueprints Price: 50,000 Imprinting Genetic Information A Genetic Code Template with an imprint When constructed, Genetic Code Templates are empty. Tenno can use them to store the genetic information of an active pet anytime after it has matured. Imprinting a single genetic code for a pet will take 1.5 hours, but can be rushed for 10 Platinum 10. Up to three imprints can be taken of a single Kavat, and up to two of a single Kubrow. Note that your pet will be unusable while imprinting them, but are usable as normal after the process is over and the finished imprint has been claimed from the incubator. Stored Information The traits mentioned below are stored by a Genetic Code template, with each trait detail having a 50%-per-template weighting when used to create a descending pet.[1] Bear in mind that the information stored will only refer to the pet's original appearance, free of any changes to its coat colors or pattern. Kubrows Kavats Coat Colors (Primary, Secondary, Tertiary) Coat Pattern (Patchy, Hound, Lotus etc.) Eye Color Breed (Sahasa, Raksa, Huras, Sunika, or Chesa) Build (Skinny, Athletic, Bulky) Coat Colors (Primary, Secondary, Tertiary, Accents) Coat Pattern (Hyacinth, Vasca) Eye Color Breed (Smeeta, Adarza, or Vasca) Head Type Tail Type For details regarding which patterns and colors occur naturally, please refer to Kubrow Cosmetics or Kavat Cosmetics. Template Identification A Genetic Code Template contains the name of the pet it was created from and an icon indicating which type of pet it is. Huras KubrowProwling stance. Reflecting that the Huras is orientated around stalking. Raksa KubrowSitting in a loyal stoic pose. Reflecting Raksa specialty of protecting their master. Sahasa KubrowDigging pose. Indicating Sahasa's role in digging up items Sunika KubrowAggresive pouncing pose. Indicating Sunika's role as an attack Kubrow. Chesa KubrowRetrieving pose. Indicating Chesa's trait of fetching items. Smeeta KavatDashing while turning invisible. Indicating Smeeta's specialty of trickery. Adarza KavatFacing a reflection of itself. Indicating Adarza's specialty of reflection. Vasca KavatFacing forward with its body lowered and tail up. When Breeding A New Pet Go to the Incubator and choose the Breeding tab. Click the Begin Incubation button. Click on the empty imprint slots and add the desired imprints. You must enter two imprints; you cannot begin a combined incubation with only one imprint. You can use imprints from two male or two female specimens, or even two of the same companion; two imprints from different Kubrows or Kavats can be chosen for a chance to combine traits. Kubrow imprints cannot be used for breeding Kavats and vice versa. Click Begin A Combined Incubation and wait 48 hours ( Nutrio Incubator Upgrade Segment reduces this to 24 hours) for your new pet, or rush the incubation for 15 Platinum 15. Trivia The screen of the template bears similarities to the results of a Gel electrophoresis test, which is appropriate given the template's function. Prior to Hotfix 14.0.5 (2014-07-21), imprints weren't a surefire way to determine the result of a Kubrow as recessive genes were taken into account. This has since been changed to remove recessive genes, ensuring the imprints contain all the used information. Also, prior to 14.0.5, there was a feature known as a Kubrow Genetic Scrambler, which randomized the Kubrow's genes in exchange for Platinum, and was removed due to a serious resemblance to a slot machine, something that went against everything DE wanted. With the initial launch of Specters of the Rail, the blueprint was unavailable in the Market. This was later corrected in Hotfix 6. From Update: Specters of the Rail 0.0 (2016-07-08) until Hotfix: Specters of the Rail 0.10 (2016-07-15), it was possible to cross-breed Kavats with Kubrows by combining a Kubrow Genetic Code Template with a Kavat Genetic Code Template, resulting in Kubrows or Kavats that, while otherwise normal, had various visual glitches such as incorrect fur textures or malformed models. Players were allowed to keep the hybrids after Hotfix 10 was deployed, but any Genetic Code Templates from them cannot be used for further breeding. Imprints can be traded between players. Patch History Update 40.0 (2025-10-15) Fixed the Genetic Imprint selection menu list not scaling properly to the ratio of the screen. Update 34.0 (2023-10-18) Updated the Imprints error message to be more accurate: Was: "Cannot make imprints of pets when they have not yet matured. Wait for active pet to become an adult before creating imprint." Now: "Cannot make imprints of pets when they have not yet matured. Mature to an adult before creating imprint." Update 31.5 (2022-04-27) Added a cap of 200 for Companion Imprints. Imprints can also be sold from Inventory for Credits. The cap was added to prevent the game from crashing due to having a large amount of Imprints. If you have over 200 Imprints, you will be required to sell Imprints through your Inventory before creating new Imprints. Fixed crash related to the ‘Browse Genetic Imprints’ screen. Update 26.0 (2019-10-31) Each Kavat can now give you 3 Imprints (up from 2). This will apply to all Kavats that predate this update. If you’ve already used 2, you'll find 1 more ready to go! References
