sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
The burbling of spawning fish is heard a few seconds before a fish actually spawns in.
Depending on the species (color and size), water (clear or filled with plants), water effects (reflection and lighting), and time of day (some fish are luminescent at night) it can be difficult and take a while to spot them.
Luminous Dye is the easiest solution, costing 100 Standing 100 each and will clearly highlight all fish in a decent radius for a short time.
The player can bullet-jump above the water-surfaces in order to scan for fish; using aim gliding, mid-air slides, and double-jumps to maneuver back to shore.
 Ivara's Dashwire ability can create ziplines above the water to get a better spotting view, however, it is not possible to zoom in with the fishing spear while on the zipline. Additionally, you can throw Luminous Dye or fish bait while being balanced on the zipline, as long as you are aiming with the fishing spear at the same time of throwing the dye or bait. However, you still cannot change fishing spears while being on the zipline.
 Banshee's  Sonar ability can be used to highlight the fish with an outline briefly, though they won't get weak points or appear in minimaps.
 Limbo will see fish with an entity outline while inside the rift, although most spears will not work since the fish are not inside the rift as well, except for  Shockprod Fishing Spear and  Stunna Fishing Spear.
The Codex Scanner and Synthesis Scanner can be used to highlight fish briefly every few seconds.
Oxylus's  Scan Aquatic Lifeforms can highlight fish in the water similar to a Luminous Dye, and can highlight hotspots on the minimap.
All fish species have Codex entries located in the Objects section, taking 12 scans to complete their entries.
Spear type does not influence spawning species, and all currently known species can be caught by any spear, although non-specialized spears will require several hits to catch fish they are not designed for.
This can be counteracted by Warframe abilities that buff weapon damage, such as  Volt's passive, his  Electric Shield, or  Mesa's  Ballistic Battery.
The spears can benefit from Universal Weapon Bonuses.
Spears are affected by Flight Speed buffs, such as  Zephyr's  Jet Stream.
Ivara's  Navigator ability can be used to steer the fishing spear.
Fish tend to spawn at the moment when you are equipping the spear. If you can't see any fish around and hear no burbling try to equip the weapon, then re-equip your spear once or twice.
Wading into the water will scare away the fish. Contact with landlocked water on the Plains of Eidolon at night, will also apply a weak  Magnetic proc, quartering your shield capacity for 1 second and draining energy.
Fish are tradeable as crafting components, exception being the Cambion Drift fishes. This in spite of an announcement that they no longer would be and the individual fish pages stating that they are not tradeable.
The number of fish caught is affected by both Resource Boosters and Loyal Retriever or Resourceful Retriever.
The base number of fish spawns is affected by a Resource Drop Chance Booster.
Warframe abilities cannot be cast while wielding a Fishing Spear and will remain so until the player switches to a weapon. However, quick melee attacks can still be used.
While the normal ability buttons do not work with a Fishing Spear equipped, it is still possible to cast the "Currently Selected Ability" with a dedicated hotkey, as well as scroll through the list of abilities to select the desired ability.
Transference may still be activated, so long as the ability is not bound to the same keys as the fishing tools.
If the player misses the fish or randomly lobs the spear at the shoreline or underwater ground, there's a 3% chance of getting a Boot (Plains of Eidolon) or Crewman's Boot (Orb Vallis), and when reeled in, a sarcastic banjo tune will play the background. It can be sold for 400 Credits 400. The player will not get another boot until 5 minutes have passed.[citation needed]
Void Strike from the Madurai focus school does not appear to increase damage done by fishing spears (needs further testing)
Fishing cannot be performed on an Archwing or a K-Drive.
Unlike normal Fishing Spears, Shock Spears cannot deal damage on impact and are unable to kill low-health critters, break chests, or resource nodes.
The glowing silhouettes of Servofishes caused by Luminous Dye will always be those of the Magnificent variants, regardless of the actual variants.
This might be because all Servofish in the same species use the Magnificent model, but some textures are rendered invisible to depict lesser variants.
The rarity of a fish does not necessarily mean how often it is encountered.
It is used in Nightwave Acts like Earth Fisher (Catch 6 Rare Fish in the Plains of Eidolon/Orb Vallis) to make only certain fish count for the Act.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text: This item's blueprint and its components: Pulsating Tubercles, Infected Palpators, Chitinous Husk, Severed Bile Sac; can be obtained as drops from the Juggernaut, Juggernaut Behemoth, and Deimos Juggernaut. One random component is a guaranteed drop from each Juggernaut, and the blueprint may also drop as well. Each Pherliac Pod created has 50 charges. The blueprint currently has a bugged 90% drop chance from Deimos Juggernaut. DE has stated that it is intended to be only a 5% drop chance, and have already attempted (and failed) to fix this bug. However, the other components and drops have the correct drop chance as intended. The blueprint and components cannot be traded with other players.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text: Pherliac Pods are a required item to complete The Jordas Precept quest. Potent Pherliac Pods are also required to complete the quest and the blueprint is unobtainable otherwise. Potent Pherliac Pods do not actually need to be used to progress the quest however. They must only be crafted and equipped. For this reason, players may wish to keep their Potent Pherliac Pods as a souvenir, rather than using them. If used, Potent Pherliac Pods function identically to their normal variant. Blueprints are not reusable. When used, the Pherliac Pod is thrown to a targeted point on the ground. The Pod has a higher threat rating than players, and will thus attract enemy Infested to attack it instead. The pod will attract all Infested units, including Corrupted Ancients and Phorid. Against Lephantis, the Pherliac Pod will only attract the attention of the scythe-wielding Grineer head during the second phase, which will make it pull the rest of Lephantis' main body to the Pod's location as it attempts to attack it in melee. Otherwise, the Pod has no effect on the other heads, or on all the heads during the first phase. The blueprint can drop alongside one of the components upon killing a Juggernaut or Juggernaut Behemoth. The blueprint's dropping does not replace the random component. Pherliac Pods blueprints resemble other blueprint drops such as Gorgon. Non-Infested units will still attack Pherliac Pods, but the pods will not have the same threat rating as on the Infested. The word "grizzly" is incorrectly used in the description of the pherliac pod components; grisly is correct.

Luminous Dye is the easiest solution, costing 100 Standing 100 each and will clearly highlight all fish in a decent radius for a short time. sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text: Acquisition List ▾▾ The primary method for acquiring Kuva is through Kuva Siphon missions, of which there are two types available: the regular Kuva Siphon (mission level 25-35) and the Kuva Flood (mission level 80-100). Successfully destroying one Kuva Siphon will grant around 550-700 Kuva 550-700, while the Kuva Flood will award 1100-1400 Kuva 1100-1400. Kuva can be acquired in 100 Kuva 100 or 300 Kuva 300 units by doing Cetus Bounties, 500 Kuva 500 units from Fortuna Bounties, and 750 Kuva 750 from Necralisk Bounties. Sorties have a 12% chance to award 6,000 Kuva 6,000 on completion of the third mission. Archon Hunts have a 12% chance to award 12,000 Kuva 12,000 on completion of the third mission. Kuva Survival on Taveuni, Kuva Fortress rewards 200 Kuva 200 for converting a Life Support Capsule into a Kuva Harvester and then defending it. Disruption on Tamu, Kuva Fortress awards 100 Kuva 100 for Tier A, 200 Kuva 200 for Tier B, and 350 Kuva 350 for Tier C. In addition, Demolisher units drop 50 Kuva 50 when killed. Downing a Kuva Larvling rewards 100 Kuva 100. Using the Parazon on a Rank 1, 2, 3 and 4 Kuva Lich will respectively drop 150 Kuva 150, 200 Kuva 200, 250 Kuva 250 and 300 Kuva 300. Requiem Relics Have a chance to drop 1200 Kuva 1200. Nightwave awards 20,000 Kuva 20,000 or 10,000 Kuva 10,000 at certain ranks. Exact specifics may change with each new season. 10,000 Kuva 10,000 can be purchased from Nightwave Offerings for 50x Cred. 10,000 Kuva 10,000 can be purchased for 25 Vitus Essence from the Arbitrations Vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room of any relay. 10,000 Kuva 10,000 can be purchased for 15 Steel Essence from Teshin in the Conclave room of any relay. Kuva bought with Steel Essence has a purchase limit of 25 per week, for a total of 250,000 Kuva 250,000. 50,000 Kuva 50,000 can be purchased from The Steel Path Honors Shop for 55 Steel Essence when available once every 8 weeks (Check availability on the The Steel Path page). 35,000 Kuva 35,000 can be purchased weekly for 5 Voidplume Pinions from Archimedean Yonta in the Chrysalith Hub of the Zariman. 35,000 Kuva 35,000 can be purchased weekly for 10 Riven Slivers from Palladino in Iron Wake. 5,000 Kuva 5,000 can potentially be purchased weekly for 10 Pathos Clamps from Acrithis in the Dormizone after completing The Duviri Paradox, with a purchase limit of 1 per week. 6,000 Kuva 6,000 can potentially be acquired from the 1999 Calendar. 6,000 Kuva 6,000 can be purchased for 110 Beating Heartstrings from Flare in the Höllvania Central Mall after reaching Rank 4 - Hot & Fresh with The Hex, with a purchase limit of 7 per week, for a total of 42,000 Kuva 42,000. 6,000 Kuva 6,000 can be purchased for 100 Scuttler Husk from Acrithis in the Dormizone after completing The Hex quest, with a purchase limit of 7 per week, for a total of 42,000 Kuva 42,000. 6,000 Kuva 6,000 can be purchased for 75 Fergolyte from Nightcap in Fortuna after reaching Rank 3 - Seeker with Nightcap, with a purchase limit of 7 per week, for a total of 42,000 Kuva 42,000. 7,000 Kuva 7,000 can be purchased for 35 Crimson Talent and 35 Emerald Talent from Hunhow in Pontis Tower after completing Jade Shadows: Constellations quest, with a purchase limit of 7 per week, for a total of 49,000 Kuva 49,000. 20,000 Kuva 20,000 can be selected as a The Steel Path The Circuit Tier 5 or 10 reward once per week.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text: Kuva gain is affected by Resource Boosters. This does not apply to the mission rewards from Kuva Fortress Disruption, but does apply to the Kuva dropped by Demolishers and Kuva Liches. During Kuva Siphon missions, Kuva clouds can be captured with the Operator's Void abilities, namely Void Beam and Void Sling. Kuva has its own Codex entry, requiring 3 scans, though it does not contain any meaningful information.

evidence.excerpt:
Acquisition
Acquisition List ▾▾ The primary method for acquiring Kuva is through Kuva Siphon missions, of which there are two types available: the regular Kuva Siphon (mission level 25-35) and the Kuva Flood (mission level 80-100). Successfully destroying one Kuva Siphon will grant around 550-700 Kuva 550-700, while the Kuva Flood will award 1100-1400 Kuva 1100-1400. Kuva can be acquired in 100 Kuva 100 or 300 Kuva 300 units by doing Cetus Bounties, 500 Kuva 500 units from Fortuna Bounties, and 750 Kuva 750 from Necralisk Bounties. Sorties have a 12% chance to award 6,000 Kuva 6,000 on completion of the third mission. Archon Hunts have a 12% chance to award 12,000 Kuva 12,000 on completion of the third mission. Kuva Survival on Taveuni, Kuva Fortress rewards 200 Kuva 200 for converting a Life Support Capsule into a Kuva Harvester and then defending it. Disruption on Tamu, Kuva Fortress awards 100 Kuva 100 for Tier A, 200 Kuva 200 for Tier B, and 350 Kuva 350 for Tier C. In addition, Demolisher units drop 50 Kuva 50 when killed. Downing a Kuva Larvling rewards 100 Kuva 100. Using the Parazon on a Rank 1, 2, 3 and 4 Kuva Lich will respectively drop 150 Kuva 150, 200 Kuva 200, 250 Kuva 250 and 300 Kuva 300. Requiem Relics Have a chance to drop 1200 Kuva 1200. Nightwave awards 20,000 Kuva 20,000 or 10,000 Kuva 10,000 at certain ranks. Exact specifics may change with each new season. 10,000 Kuva 10,000 can be purchased from Nightwave Offerings for 50x Cred. 10,000 Kuva 10,000 can be purchased for 25 Vitus Essence from the Arbitrations Vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room of any relay. 10,000 Kuva 10,000 can be purchased for 15 Steel Essence from Teshin in the Conclave room of any relay. sections.title: Notes
sections.text: The burbling of spawning fish is heard a few seconds before a fish actually spawns in. Depending on the species (color and size), water (clear or filled with plants), water effects (reflection and lighting), and time of day (some fish are luminescent at night) it can be difficult and take a while to spot them. Luminous Dye is the easiest solution, costing 100 Standing 100 each and will clearly highlight all fish in a decent radius for a short time. The player can bullet-jump above the water-surfaces in order to scan for fish; using aim gliding, mid-air slides, and double-jumps to maneuver back to shore. Ivara's Dashwire ability can create ziplines above the water to get a better spotting view, however, it is not possible to zoom in with the fishing spear while on the zipline. Additionally, you can throw Luminous Dye or fish bait while being balanced on the zipline, as long as you are aiming with the fishing spear at the same time of throwing the dye or bait. However, you still cannot change fishing spears while being on the zipline. Banshee's Sonar ability can be used to highlight the fish with an outline briefly, though they won't get weak points or appear in minimaps. Limbo will see fish with an entity outline while inside the rift, although most spears will not work since the fish are not inside the rift as well, except for Shockprod Fishing Spear and Stunna Fishing Spear. The Codex Scanner and Synthesis Scanner can be used to highlight fish briefly every few seconds. Oxylus's Scan Aquatic Lifeforms can highlight fish in the water similar to a Luminous Dye, and can highlight hotspots on the minimap. All fish species have Codex entries located in the Objects section, taking 12 scans to complete their entries. Spear type does not influence spawning species, and all currently known species can be caught by any spear, although non-specialized spears will require several hits to catch fish they are not designed for. This can be counteracted by Warframe abilities that buff weapon damage, such as Volt's passive, his Electric Shield, or Mesa's Ballistic Battery. The spears can benefit from Universal Weapon Bonuses. Spears are affected by Flight Speed buffs, such as Zephyr's Jet Stream. Ivara's Navigator ability can be used to steer the fishing spear. Fish tend to spawn at the moment when you are equipping the spear. If you can't see any fish around and hear no burbling try to equip the weapon, then re-equip your spear once or twice. Wading into the water will scare away the fish. Contact with landlocked water on the Plains of Eidolon at night, will also apply a weak Magnetic proc, quartering your shield capacity for 1 second and draining energy. Fish are tradeable as crafting components, exception being the Cambion Drift fishes. This in spite of an announcement that they no longer would be and the individual fish pages stating that they are not tradeable. The number of fish caught is affected by both Resource Boosters and Loyal Retriever or Resourceful Retriever. The base number of fish spawns is affected by a Resource Drop Chance Booster. Warframe abilities cannot be cast while wielding a Fishing Spear and will remain so until the player switches to a weapon. However, quick melee attacks can still be used. While the normal ability buttons do not work with a Fishing Spear equipped, it is still possible to cast the "Currently Selected Ability" with a dedicated hotkey, as well as scroll through the list of abilities to select the desired ability. Transference may still be activated, so long as the ability is not bound to the same keys as the fishing tools. If the player misses the fish or randomly lobs the spear at the shoreline or underwater ground, there's a 3% chance of getting a Boot (Plains of Eidolon) or Crewman's Boot (Orb Vallis), and when reeled in, a sarcastic banjo tune will play the background. It can be sold for 400 Credits 400. The player will not get another boot until 5 minutes have passed.[citation needed] Void Strike from the Madurai focus school does not appear to increase damage done by fishing spears (needs further testing) Fishing cannot be performed on an Archwing or a K-Drive. Unlike normal Fishing Spears, Shock Spears cannot deal damage on impact and are unable to kill low-health critters, break chests, or resource nodes. The glowing silhouettes of Servofishes caused by Luminous Dye will always be those of the Magnificent variants, regardless of the actual variants. This might be because all Servofish in the same species use the Magnificent model, but some textures are rendered invisible to depict lesser variants. The rarity of a fish does not necessarily mean how often it is encountered. It is used in Nightwave Acts like Earth Fisher (Catch 6 Rare Fish in the Plains of Eidolon/Orb Vallis) to make only certain fish count for the Act.

However, you still cannot change fishing spears while being on the zipline. Banshee's Sonar ability can be used to highlight the fish with an outline briefly, though they won't get weak points or appear in minimaps. Limbo will see fish with an entity outline while inside the rift, although most spears will not work since the fish are not inside the rift as well, except for Shockprod Fishing Spear and Stunna Fishing Spear. The Codex Scanner and Synthesis Scanner can be used to highlight fish briefly every few seconds. Oxylus's Scan Aquatic Lifeforms can highlight fish in the water similar to a Luminous Dye, and can highlight hotspots on the minimap. All fish species have Codex entries located in the Objects section, taking 12 scans to complete their entries. Spear type does not influence spawning species, and all currently known species can be caught by any spear, although non-specialized spears will require several hits to catch fish they are not designed for. This can be counteracted by Warframe abilities that buff weapon damage, such as Volt's passive, his Electric Shield, or Mesa's Ballistic Battery. The spears can benefit from Universal Weapon Bonuses. Spears are affected by Flight Speed buffs, such as Zephyr's Jet Stream. Ivara's Navigator ability can be used to steer the fishing spear. Fish tend to spawn at the moment when you are equipping the spear. If you can't see any fish around and hear no burbling try to equip the weapon, then re-equip your spear once or twice. Wading into the water will scare away the fish. Contact with landlocked water on the Plains of Eidolon at night, will also apply a weak Magnetic proc, quartering your shield capacity for 1 second and draining energy. Fish are tradeable as crafting components, exception being the Cambion Drift fishes. This in spite of an announcement that they no longer would be and the individual fish pages stating that they are not tradeable. The number of fish caught is affected by both Resource Boosters and Loyal Retriever or Resourceful Retriever. The base number of fish spawns is affected by a Resource Drop Chance Booster. Warframe abilities cannot be cast while wielding a Fishing Spear and will remain so until the player switches to a weapon. However, quick melee attacks can still be used. While the normal ability buttons do not work with a Fishing Spear equipped, it is still possible to cast the "Currently Selected Ability" with a dedicated hotkey, as well as scroll through the list of abilities to select the desired ability. Transference may still be activated, so long as the ability is not bound to the same keys as the fishing tools. If the player misses the fish or randomly lobs the spear at the shoreline or underwater ground, there's a 3% chance of getting a Boot (Plains of Eidolon) or Crewman's Boot (Orb Vallis), and when reeled in, a sarcastic banjo tune will play the background. It can be sold for 400 Credits 400. The player will not get another boot until 5 minutes have passed.[citation needed] Void Strike from the Madurai focus school does not appear to increase damage done by fishing spears (needs further testing) Fishing cannot be performed on an Archwing or a K-Drive. Unlike normal Fishing Spears, Shock Spears cannot deal damage on impact and are unable to kill low-health critters, break chests, or resource nodes. The glowing silhouettes of Servofishes caused by Luminous Dye will always be those of the Magnificent variants, regardless of the actual variants. This might be because all Servofish in the same species use the Magnificent model, but some textures are rendered invisible to depict lesser variants. The rarity of a fish does not necessarily mean how often it is encountered. It is used in Nightwave Acts like Earth Fisher (Catch 6 Rare Fish in the Plains of Eidolon/Orb Vallis) to make only certain fish count for the Act.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text: If a player has purchased the Octavia Warframe from the Market with Platinum prior to completing the quest, they will receive a Pistol Riven Mod on completion of the quest in lieu of the normal blueprint reward. Once Suda has been taken over by Hunhow, players affiliated with her syndicate will not gain any visible standing with her until the quest is complete, nor will she speak to the player on entering a mission for her. The standing is still accumulated, however, and will be added to the existing amount after she has been restored. Oddly enough, if the player visited Suda before talking to Simaris in the final mission, she will somehow act normal as if nothing happened at all. Her syndicate can also be fully accessed as well (possibly a bug) Ordis' corruption in mission 4 does not prevent Ordis' purple overlay of Lotus. Talking to Suda while the quest is active (and before she was taken over by Hunhow) will occur a special dialogue, in which Suda will question if the player has constructed the Mandachord since she "can't hear its music". Completing the quest also unlocks a special mission called "Incident Archive 2318-H" in Suda's room, which is a replay of the quest's final level, but with a leaderboard. Incident Archive 2318-H is merely an obstacle challenge, and will not reward anything when completed.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text: The burbling of spawning fish is heard a few seconds before a fish actually spawns in. Depending on the species (color and size), water (clear or filled with plants), water effects (reflection and lighting), and time of day (some fish are luminescent at night) it can be difficult and take a while to spot them. Luminous Dye is the easiest solution, costing 100 Standing 100 each and will clearly highlight all fish in a decent radius for a short time. The player can bullet-jump above the water-surfaces in order to scan for fish; using aim gliding, mid-air slides, and double-jumps to maneuver back to shore. Ivara's Dashwire ability can create ziplines above the water to get a better spotting view, however, it is not possible to zoom in with the fishing spear while on the zipline. Additionally, you can throw Luminous Dye or fish bait while being balanced on the zipline, as long as you are aiming with the fishing spear at the same time of throwing the dye or bait. However, you still cannot change fishing spears while being on the zipline. Banshee's Sonar ability can be used to highlight the fish with an outline briefly, though they won't get weak points or appear in minimaps. Limbo will see fish with an entity outline while inside the rift, although most spears will not work since the fish are not inside the rift as well, except for Shockprod Fishing Spear and Stunna Fishing Spear. The Codex Scanner and Synthesis Scanner can be used to highlight fish briefly every few seconds. Oxylus's Scan Aquatic Lifeforms can highlight fish in the water similar to a Luminous Dye, and can highlight hotspots on the minimap. All fish species have Codex entries located in the Objects section, taking 12 scans to complete their entries. Spear type does not influence spawning species, and all currently known species can be caught by any spear, although non-specialized spears will require several hits to catch fish they are not designed for. This can be counteracted by Warframe abilities that buff weapon damage, such as Volt's passive, his Electric Shield, or Mesa's Ballistic Battery. The spears can benefit from Universal Weapon Bonuses. Spears are affected by Flight Speed buffs, such as Zephyr's Jet Stream. Ivara's Navigator ability can be used to steer the fishing spear. Fish tend to spawn at the moment when you are equipping the spear. If you can't see any fish around and hear no burbling try to equip the weapon, then re-equip your spear once or twice. Wading into the water will scare away the fish. Contact with landlocked water on the Plains of Eidolon at night, will also apply a weak Magnetic proc, quartering your shield capacity for 1 second and draining energy. Fish are tradeable as crafting components, exception being the Cambion Drift fishes. This in spite of an announcement that they no longer would be and the individual fish pages stating that they are not tradeable. The number of fish caught is affected by both Resource Boosters and Loyal Retriever or Resourceful Retriever. The base number of fish spawns is affected by a Resource Drop Chance Booster. Warframe abilities cannot be cast while wielding a Fishing Spear and will remain so until the player switches to a weapon. However, quick melee attacks can still be used. While the normal ability buttons do not work with a Fishing Spear equipped, it is still possible to cast the "Currently Selected Ability" with a dedicated hotkey, as well as scroll through the list of abilities to select the desired ability. Transference may still be activated, so long as the ability is not bound to the same keys as the fishing tools. If the player misses the fish or randomly lobs the spear at the shoreline or underwater ground, there's a 3% chance of getting a Boot (Plains of Eidolon) or Crewman's Boot (Orb Vallis), and when reeled in, a sarcastic banjo tune will play the background. It can be sold for 400 Credits 400. The player will not get another boot until 5 minutes have passed.[citation needed] Void Strike from the Madurai focus school does not appear to increase damage done by fishing spears (needs further testing) Fishing cannot be performed on an Archwing or a K-Drive. Unlike normal Fishing Spears, Shock Spears cannot deal damage on impact and are unable to kill low-health critters, break chests, or resource nodes. The glowing silhouettes of Servofishes caused by Luminous Dye will always be those of the Magnificent variants, regardless of the actual variants. This might be because all Servofish in the same species use the Magnificent model, but some textures are rendered invisible to depict lesser variants. The rarity of a fish does not necessarily mean how often it is encountered. It is used in Nightwave Acts like Earth Fisher (Catch 6 Rare Fish in the Plains of Eidolon/Orb Vallis) to make only certain fish count for the Act.

This in spite of an announcement that they no longer would be and the individual fish pages stating that they are not tradeable. The number of fish caught is affected by both Resource Boosters and Loyal Retriever or Resourceful Retriever. The base number of fish spawns is affected by a Resource Drop Chance Booster. Warframe abilities cannot be cast while wielding a Fishing Spear and will remain so until the player switches to a weapon. However, quick melee attacks can still be used. While the normal ability buttons do not work with a Fishing Spear equipped, it is still possible to cast the "Currently Selected Ability" with a dedicated hotkey, as well as scroll through the list of abilities to select the desired ability. Transference may still be activated, so long as the ability is not bound to the same keys as the fishing tools. If the player misses the fish or randomly lobs the spear at the shoreline or underwater ground, there's a 3% chance of getting a Boot (Plains of Eidolon) or Crewman's Boot (Orb Vallis), and when reeled in, a sarcastic banjo tune will play the background. It can be sold for 400 Credits 400. The player will not get another boot until 5 minutes have passed.[citation needed] Void Strike from the Madurai focus school does not appear to increase damage done by fishing spears (needs further testing) Fishing cannot be performed on an Archwing or a K-Drive. Unlike normal Fishing Spears, Shock Spears cannot deal damage on impact and are unable to kill low-health critters, break chests, or resource nodes. The glowing silhouettes of Servofishes caused by Luminous Dye will always be those of the Magnificent variants, regardless of the actual variants. This might be because all Servofish in the same species use the Magnificent model, but some textures are rendered invisible to depict lesser variants. The rarity of a fish does not necessarily mean how often it is encountered. It is used in Nightwave Acts like Earth Fisher (Catch 6 Rare Fish in the Plains of Eidolon/Orb Vallis) to make only certain fish count for the Act.

The player can bullet-jump above the water-surfaces in order to scan for fish; using aim gliding, mid-air slides, and double-jumps to maneuver back to shore. Ivara's Dashwire ability can create ziplines above the water to get a better spotting view, however, it is not possible to zoom in with the fishing spear while on the zipline. Additionally, you can throw Luminous Dye or fish bait while being balanced on the zipline, as long as you are aiming with the fishing spear at the same time of throwing the dye or bait. However, you still cannot change fishing spears while being on the zipline. Banshee's Sonar ability can be used to highlight the fish with an outline briefly, though they won't get weak points or appear in minimaps. It can be sold for 400 Credits 400. The player will not get another boot until 5 minutes have passed.[citation needed] Void Strike from the Madurai focus school does not appear to increase damage done by fishing spears (needs further testing) Fishing cannot be performed on an Archwing or a K-Drive. Unlike normal Fishing Spears, Shock Spears cannot deal damage on impact and are unable to kill low-health critters, break chests, or resource nodes. sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text: Vulpaphylas are acquired through Revivification with Son in the Necralisk, Deimos. The player must capture a Weakened Panzer Vulpaphyla via Conservation (found only during Fass cycle) after it has been attacked by Infested, as well as obtain a Mutagen and Antigen bought from Son with Entrati Standing, and 5,000 Credits 5,000 to initiate the Revivification process. A newly revived Vulpaphyla cannot be renamed, customized, Polarized or earn Mastery Rank experience, but can equip an Orokin Reactor. In order to unlock these features, the player must Gild their Vulpaphyla by achieving rank of Acquaintance with the Entrati, leveling the Vulpaphyla up to Rank 30 and then returning to Son along with 10 Son Token and 5,000 Standing 5,000.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text: This item's blueprint and its components: Pulsating Tubercles, Infected Palpators, Chitinous Husk, Severed Bile Sac; can be obtained as drops from the Juggernaut, Juggernaut Behemoth, and Deimos Juggernaut. One random component is a guaranteed drop from each Juggernaut, and the blueprint may also drop as well. Each Pherliac Pod created has 50 charges. The blueprint currently has a bugged 90% drop chance from Deimos Juggernaut. DE has stated that it is intended to be only a 5% drop chance, and have already attempted (and failed) to fix this bug. However, the other components and drops have the correct drop chance as intended. The blueprint and components cannot be traded with other players.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text: Pherliac Pods are a required item to complete The Jordas Precept quest. Potent Pherliac Pods are also required to complete the quest and the blueprint is unobtainable otherwise. Potent Pherliac Pods do not actually need to be used to progress the quest however. They must only be crafted and equipped. For this reason, players may wish to keep their Potent Pherliac Pods as a souvenir, rather than using them. If used, Potent Pherliac Pods function identically to their normal variant. Blueprints are not reusable. When used, the Pherliac Pod is thrown to a targeted point on the ground. The Pod has a higher threat rating than players, and will thus attract enemy Infested to attack it instead. The pod will attract all Infested units, including Corrupted Ancients and Phorid. Against Lephantis, the Pherliac Pod will only attract the attention of the scythe-wielding Grineer head during the second phase, which will make it pull the rest of Lephantis' main body to the Pod's location as it attempts to attack it in melee. Otherwise, the Pod has no effect on the other heads, or on all the heads during the first phase. The blueprint can drop alongside one of the components upon killing a Juggernaut or Juggernaut Behemoth. The blueprint's dropping does not replace the random component. Pherliac Pods blueprints resemble other blueprint drops such as Gorgon. Non-Infested units will still attack Pherliac Pods, but the pods will not have the same threat rating as on the Infested. The word "grizzly" is incorrectly used in the description of the pherliac pod components; grisly is correct.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text: This quest cannot be repeated by getting another Sands of Inaros key from Baro Ki'Teer. Thus, if you sell your Inaros after completing this quest, the only way to reacquire him is either by purchasing him through the Market with Platinum, purchasing his components from Cephalon Simaris for Standing, or acquiring his parts via The Circuit. This quest must be set as your active quest for the enemy kills to count. Simply having the vessel in your gear wheel is not sufficient. This may be the only chance to get Tomb Guardian, Tomb Protector, and Tomb Protector Effigy codex scans. The flames burning in jars throughout the temple will apply a Heat status on contact, even through the Rift Plane. Kills obtained within the Simulacrum do not count towards the Vessel's goal. Kills in Sanctuary Onslaught however, will. Although the Tomb Protector's abilities are largely identical to Inaros' abilities; there are some slight differences. Desiccation merely inflicts the damage over time and does not immobilize nor blind any player caught in its wake. Note that hacking Denial Bursas by itself will not count towards the Vessel's goal; they must actually die in order to fulfill the challenge. Buying the "Sands of Inaros Bundle" from the market can start this quest rather than having to purchase it from Baro Ki'Teer. The final fight with the Tomb Protector is timed; the player is given about fifteen minutes to complete the fight before extracting. Failure to do so will result in an overall mission failure. During the second portion of the Sands of Inaros quest up to 50 Feral Kavats will spawn, yielding around 5-10 Genetic Codes.

Notes
This quest cannot be repeated by getting another Sands of Inaros key from Baro Ki'Teer. Thus, if you sell your Inaros after completing this quest, the only way to reacquire him is either by purchasing him through the Market with Platinum, purchasing his components from Cephalon Simaris for Standing, or acquiring his parts via The Circuit. sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text: This item's blueprint and its components: Pulsating Tubercles, Infected Palpators, Chitinous Husk, Severed Bile Sac; can be obtained as drops from the Juggernaut, Juggernaut Behemoth, and Deimos Juggernaut. One random component is a guaranteed drop from each Juggernaut, and the blueprint may also drop as well. Each Pherliac Pod created has 50 charges. The blueprint currently has a bugged 90% drop chance from Deimos Juggernaut. DE has stated that it is intended to be only a 5% drop chance, and have already attempted (and failed) to fix this bug. However, the other components and drops have the correct drop chance as intended. The blueprint and components cannot be traded with other players.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text: Pherliac Pods are a required item to complete The Jordas Precept quest. Potent Pherliac Pods are also required to complete the quest and the blueprint is unobtainable otherwise. Potent Pherliac Pods do not actually need to be used to progress the quest however. They must only be crafted and equipped. For this reason, players may wish to keep their Potent Pherliac Pods as a souvenir, rather than using them. If used, Potent Pherliac Pods function identically to their normal variant. Blueprints are not reusable. When used, the Pherliac Pod is thrown to a targeted point on the ground. The Pod has a higher threat rating than players, and will thus attract enemy Infested to attack it instead. The pod will attract all Infested units, including Corrupted Ancients and Phorid. Against Lephantis, the Pherliac Pod will only attract the attention of the scythe-wielding Grineer head during the second phase, which will make it pull the rest of Lephantis' main body to the Pod's location as it attempts to attack it in melee. Otherwise, the Pod has no effect on the other heads, or on all the heads during the first phase. The blueprint can drop alongside one of the components upon killing a Juggernaut or Juggernaut Behemoth. The blueprint's dropping does not replace the random component. Pherliac Pods blueprints resemble other blueprint drops such as Gorgon. Non-Infested units will still attack Pherliac Pods, but the pods will not have the same threat rating as on the Infested. The word "grizzly" is incorrectly used in the description of the pherliac pod components; grisly is correct.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text: The burbling of spawning fish is heard a few seconds before a fish actually spawns in. Depending on the species (color and size), water (clear or filled with plants), water effects (reflection and lighting), and time of day (some fish are luminescent at night) it can be difficult and take a while to spot them. Luminous Dye is the easiest solution, costing 100 Standing 100 each and will clearly highlight all fish in a decent radius for a short time. The player can bullet-jump above the water-surfaces in order to scan for fish; using aim gliding, mid-air slides, and double-jumps to maneuver back to shore. Ivara's Dashwire ability can create ziplines above the water to get a better spotting view, however, it is not possible to zoom in with the fishing spear while on the zipline. Additionally, you can throw Luminous Dye or fish bait while being balanced on the zipline, as long as you are aiming with the fishing spear at the same time of throwing the dye or bait. However, you still cannot change fishing spears while being on the zipline. Banshee's Sonar ability can be used to highlight the fish with an outline briefly, though they won't get weak points or appear in minimaps. Limbo will see fish with an entity outline while inside the rift, although most spears will not work since the fish are not inside the rift as well, except for Shockprod Fishing Spear and Stunna Fishing Spear. The Codex Scanner and Synthesis Scanner can be used to highlight fish briefly every few seconds. Oxylus's Scan Aquatic Lifeforms can highlight fish in the water similar to a Luminous Dye, and can highlight hotspots on the minimap. All fish species have Codex entries located in the Objects section, taking 12 scans to complete their entries. Spear type does not influence spawning species, and all currently known species can be caught by any spear, although non-specialized spears will require several hits to catch fish they are not designed for. This can be counteracted by Warframe abilities that buff weapon damage, such as Volt's passive, his Electric Shield, or Mesa's Ballistic Battery. The spears can benefit from Universal Weapon Bonuses. Spears are affected by Flight Speed buffs, such as Zephyr's Jet Stream. Ivara's Navigator ability can be used to steer the fishing spear. Fish tend to spawn at the moment when you are equipping the spear. If you can't see any fish around and hear no burbling try to equip the weapon, then re-equip your spear once or twice. Wading into the water will scare away the fish. Contact with landlocked water on the Plains of Eidolon at night, will also apply a weak Magnetic proc, quartering your shield capacity for 1 second and draining energy. Fish are tradeable as crafting components, exception being the Cambion Drift fishes. This in spite of an announcement that they no longer would be and the individual fish pages stating that they are not tradeable. The number of fish caught is affected by both Resource Boosters and Loyal Retriever or Resourceful Retriever. The base number of fish spawns is affected by a Resource Drop Chance Booster. Warframe abilities cannot be cast while wielding a Fishing Spear and will remain so until the player switches to a weapon. However, quick melee attacks can still be used. While the normal ability buttons do not work with a Fishing Spear equipped, it is still possible to cast the "Currently Selected Ability" with a dedicated hotkey, as well as scroll through the list of abilities to select the desired ability. Transference may still be activated, so long as the ability is not bound to the same keys as the fishing tools. If the player misses the fish or randomly lobs the spear at the shoreline or underwater ground, there's a 3% chance of getting a Boot (Plains of Eidolon) or Crewman's Boot (Orb Vallis), and when reeled in, a sarcastic banjo tune will play the background. It can be sold for 400 Credits 400. The player will not get another boot until 5 minutes have passed.[citation needed] Void Strike from the Madurai focus school does not appear to increase damage done by fishing spears (needs further testing) Fishing cannot be performed on an Archwing or a K-Drive. Unlike normal Fishing Spears, Shock Spears cannot deal damage on impact and are unable to kill low-health critters, break chests, or resource nodes. The glowing silhouettes of Servofishes caused by Luminous Dye will always be those of the Magnificent variants, regardless of the actual variants. This might be because all Servofish in the same species use the Magnificent model, but some textures are rendered invisible to depict lesser variants. The rarity of a fish does not necessarily mean how often it is encountered. It is used in Nightwave Acts like Earth Fisher (Catch 6 Rare Fish in the Plains of Eidolon/Orb Vallis) to make only certain fish count for the Act.

All fish species have Codex entries located in the Objects section, taking 12 scans to complete their entries. Spear type does not influence spawning species, and all currently known species can be caught by any spear, although non-specialized spears will require several hits to catch fish they are not designed for. This can be counteracted by Warframe abilities that buff weapon damage, such as Volt's passive, his Electric Shield, or Mesa's Ballistic Battery. The spears can benefit from Universal Weapon Bonuses. Spears are affected by Flight Speed buffs, such as Zephyr's Jet Stream. Ivara's Navigator ability can be used to steer the fishing spear. Fish tend to spawn at the moment when you are equipping the spear. If you can't see any fish around and hear no burbling try to equip the weapon, then re-equip your spear once or twice. Wading into the water will scare away the fish. Contact with landlocked water on the Plains of Eidolon at night, will also apply a weak Magnetic proc, quartering your shield capacity for 1 second and draining energy. Fish are tradeable as crafting components, exception being the Cambion Drift fishes. This in spite of an announcement that they no longer would be and the individual fish pages stating that they are not tradeable. The number of fish caught is affected by both Resource Boosters and Loyal Retriever or Resourceful Retriever. The base number of fish spawns is affected by a Resource Drop Chance Booster. Warframe abilities cannot be cast while wielding a Fishing Spear and will remain so until the player switches to a weapon. However, quick melee attacks can still be used. While the normal ability buttons do not work with a Fishing Spear equipped, it is still possible to cast the "Currently Selected Ability" with a dedicated hotkey, as well as scroll through the list of abilities to select the desired ability. Transference may still be activated, so long as the ability is not bound to the same keys as the fishing tools. If the player misses the fish or randomly lobs the spear at the shoreline or underwater ground, there's a 3% chance of getting a Boot (Plains of Eidolon) or Crewman's Boot (Orb Vallis), and when reeled in, a sarcastic banjo tune will play the background. It can be sold for 400 Credits 400. The player will not get another boot until 5 minutes have passed.[citation needed] Void Strike from the Madurai focus school does not appear to increase damage done by fishing spears (needs further testing) Fishing cannot be performed on an Archwing or a K-Drive. Unlike normal Fishing Spears, Shock Spears cannot deal damage on impact and are unable to kill low-health critters, break chests, or resource nodes. The glowing silhouettes of Servofishes caused by Luminous Dye will always be those of the Magnificent variants, regardless of the actual variants. This might be because all Servofish in the same species use the Magnificent model, but some textures are rendered invisible to depict lesser variants. The rarity of a fish does not necessarily mean how often it is encountered. It is used in Nightwave Acts like Earth Fisher (Catch 6 Rare Fish in the Plains of Eidolon/Orb Vallis) to make only certain fish count for the Act.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text: Although Treasurers can spawn prior to the completion of The Deadlock Protocol, they will not drop Granum Crowns until The Deadlock Protocol has been completed. Treasurers can spawn in Grineer Galleon tileset as well, given that the particular mission node is occupied by the Corpus. The tier of Granum Crown dropped is determined by enemy level. To prevent the Granum Crown from being dropped in Extraction zones and therefore unreachable, the Treasurer will move away from it if he is within 35 meters. In addition, if the Granum Crown is dropped in Extraction, it will be teleported to a safe spot. Granum Crown drops are not affected by Nekros Desecrate, Hydroid's Pilfering Swarm, Ivara's Prowl, Atlas Ore Gaze, Khora's Pilfering Strangledome and Chesa Kubrow's Retrieve. Granum Crowns are not affected by Resource Boosters. Nightmare Mode mission level modifier of +10 will affect the tier of the Granum Crown dropped. Example: Nightmare version of the Jackal fight on Venus is level 16-18 (instead of 6-8) and the Treasurer will drop an Exemplar Granum Crown (instead of standard Granum Crown). Players cannot interact with the Golden Hand Tribute, Solaris captives, or Corpus Temple Relief console when there are enemies nearby. Only one Corpus Temple Relief room can spawn per mission. The Sergeant has a high chance of spawning in a Corpus Temple Relief room.

In addition, if the Granum Crown is dropped in Extraction, it will be teleported to a safe spot. Example: Nightmare version of the Jackal fight on Venus is level 16-18 (instead of 6-8) and the Treasurer will drop an Exemplar Granum Crown (instead of standard Granum Crown). Players cannot interact with the Golden Hand Tribute, Solaris captives, or Corpus Temple Relief console when there are enemies nearby. Only one Corpus Temple Relief room can spawn per mission. The Sergeant has a high chance of spawning in a Corpus Temple Relief room.

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: Akimbo Slip Shot is a Secondary Arcane that increases Ammo Efficiency while sliding or aim gliding with Dual Pistols. Effect Rank Ammo Efficiency 0 +15% 1 +25% 2 +35% 3 +45% 4 +55% 5 +65% Acquisition May appear as a Steel Path Circuit Tier 1, 3, 4, 6, or 7 reward on a weekly rotational basis. May be sold by Acrithis for 10 Pathos Clamp on a daily rotational basis, requiring Drifter Intrinsics Opportunity Rank 9 for the Arcanes to appear in her shop. May drop from side Undercroft portals in The Duviri Experience while on The Steel Path. Notes Cannot be equipped on Exalted Weapons like Dex Pixia. Can be equipped on the ( Prisma) Dual Decurion. Additive with Zazvat-Kar for maximum Ammo Efficiency on the Akstiletto Prime. Patch History Hotfix 40.0.3 (2025-10-21) Akbimo Slipshot[sic] Arcane can now be equipped on the (Prisma) Dual Decurions Archguns. Update 33.5 (2023-06-21) Introduced.

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: Arcane Acceleration is an Arcane Enhancement that provides a 30% chance on each Critical Hit to increase the fire rate of non-shotgun primary weapons for 9 seconds. Can be sold for 500 Credits 500. Effect Rank Fire Rate Bonus 0 15% 1 30% 2 45% 3 60% 4 75% 5 90% Acquisition Awarded for killing or capturing the Eidolon Gantulyst. Dropped in Rotation C of Orphix Venus Proxima or Veil Proxima missions. Awarded at Tier 9 in The Duviri Paradox Circuit for Week 1. Item Source Chance Expected Nearly Guaranteed Veil Proxima Orphix / C 5.63% ~ 17 C Rotations 119 ± 39 C Rotations Tier 1 The Circuit (Normal) 0.67% ~ 149 A Rotations 1027 ± 342 A Rotations Venus Proxima Orphix / C 6.90% ~ 14 C Rotations 96 ± 32 C Rotations Tier 9 The Circuit (Normal) 4% ~ 25 A Rotations 169 ± 56 A Rotations Tier 3 The Circuit (Normal) 0.67% ~ 149 A Rotations 1027 ± 342 A Rotations Eidolon Gantulyst Capture 9.25% ~ 10 Kills 71 ± 23 Kills Eidolon Gantulyst Kill 10.20% ~ 9 Kills 64 ± 21 Kills All drop rates data is obtained from DE's official drop tables stored on Module:DropTables/data. See Mission Rewards#Standard Missions for definitions on reward table rotations. For more detailed definitions and information, visit here. History Arcane Acceleration was first introduced in the Trial mission The Law of Retribution, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01). The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Acceleration from Little Duck for 1,500 Scarlet Credits. The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Acceleration from Father for 300 Phasic Cells. The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Acceleration from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 5 Grotesque Splinter. The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Acceleration from Ordis for 3 Volatile Motes in the first run of the event, and 4 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42. The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Acceleration from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 7, costing 2 Dominus Aureus, with a purchase limit of 3 (21 Arcanes in total). Notes Additively stacks with mods that increase fire rate (e.g. Speed Trigger). Does not double its bonus for bows Bonus applies to most primary weapons, including snipers & launchers, but not shotguns. Can be triggered by critical hits from almost any source, including all weapon types, beast and sentinel attacks, and even Warframe abilities (such as Wisp's Breach Surge sparks and all of Gyre's damaging abilities) Patch History Update 31.6 (2022-06-09) ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff. Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result. Arcane Acceleration On Critical Hit: 30% (was 5%) chance for 15%/90% Fire Rate to Primary Weapons (excluding Shotguns) for 9s (was 2s) Update 22.12 (2018-02-09) Now has a base effect of 15% with a base chance of 5%. Update 16.0 (2015-03-19) Introduced.

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: Arcane Aegis is an Arcane Enhancement that provides a 3% chance whenever the player receives damage to shields to regenerate Warframe Shields for 12 seconds. Can be sold for 1,000 Credits 1,000. Effect Rank Shield Recharge Rate 0 +5% 1 +10% 2 +15% 3 +20% 4 +25% 5 +30% Acquisition Awarded for capturing the Eidolon Gantulyst. Dropped in Rotation C of Orphix Venus Proxima or Veil Proxima missions. Item Source Chance Expected Nearly Guaranteed Veil Proxima Orphix / C 2.82% ~ 35 C Rotations 241 ± 80 C Rotations Venus Proxima Orphix / C 3.45% ~ 28 C Rotations 196 ± 65 C Rotations Eidolon Gantulyst Capture 5.29% ~ 18 Kills 127 ± 42 Kills All drop rates data is obtained from DE's official drop tables stored on Module:DropTables/data. See Mission Rewards#Standard Missions for definitions on reward table rotations. For more detailed definitions and information, visit here. History Arcane Aegis was first introduced in the Trial mission The Jordas Verdict, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01). The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Aegis from Little Duck for 2,000 Scarlet Credits. The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Aegis from Father for 400 Phasic Cells. The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Aegis from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 6 Grotesque Splinter. The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Aegis from Ordis for 5 Volatile Motes in the first run of the event, and 6 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42. The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Aegis from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 7, costing 4 Dominus Aureus, with a purchase limit of 3 (21 Arcanes total). The Operation: Blood of Perita event sold Arcane Aegis from Roathe for 4 Marks of Valiance with a purchase limit of 21 per week. Notes Has a hidden stat that sets shield recharge delay to 0 seconds while active. This causes the players shields to constantly regenerate, even when being damaged. Magnetic status effect still prevents shield regeneration with this Arcane active. Shield Recharge bonus is additive to the innate 5% shield recharge rate. Shield recharge bonus stacks multiplicatively with Fast Deflection and Vigilante Vigor. A Hildryn with Redirection, Fast Deflection, and Arcane Aegis equipped will have a shield recharge rate of [ 15 + ( 5% + 30% ) * 1780 * ( 1 + 100% ) ] * (1 + 90%) = 2,395.9 shields per second while Arcane Aegis is active. Does not generate overshields. Since this Arcane only triggers when shields are damaged, it has no benefit on Inaros, Kullervo, and Nidus, as they have no shields to begin with, or during a Nightmare Mode mission with the No Shields modifier. Bugs Activating Arcane Aegis once will break the 80% Shield Recharge cap for the rest of the mission or until death. After the cap is lifted, Gauss's Passive, Jade's Symphony of Mercy, and Vazarin ability Guardian Break when paired with Fast Deflection and Vigilante Vigor will set Shield Recharge Delay to 0. Due to Shield Gating, this makes shields effectively impossible to bypass except through Toxin or Magnetic status. Media Patch History Hotfix 34.0.2 (2023-10-19) Fixed Arcane Aegis exclusively triggering when not being attacked instead of when Shields are damaged. As reported here: https://forums.warframe.com/topic/1368186-arcane-aegis-massive-nerf Update 31.6 (2022-06-09) ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff. Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result. Arcane Aegis On Shield Damaged: 3% (was 1%) chance for 5%/30% Shield Recharge for 12s (was 2s) Update 17.9.1 (2015-10-29) Introduced.

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: Arcane Arachne is an Arcane Enhancement that increases the player's weapon damage for 30 seconds whenever they wall latch. This cannot be refreshed while active. Can be sold for 1,000 Credits 1,000. Effect Rank Damage Bonus 0 +25% 1 +50% 2 +75% 3 +100% 4 +125% 5 +150% Acquisition Awarded for killing or capturing the Eidolon Hydrolyst. Dropped in Rotation C of Orphix Pluto Proxima or Veil Proxima missions. Awarded from the completion of a Side Objective Undercroft Portal. History Arcane Arachne was first introduced in the Trial mission The Jordas Verdict, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01). The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Arachne from Little Duck for 2,000 Scarlet Credits. The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Arachne from Father for 400 Phasic Cells. The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Rage from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 6 Grotesque Splinter. The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Arachne from Ordis for 5 Volatile Motes, with a purchase limit of 42. The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Arachne from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 7, costing 4 Dominus Aureus, with a purchase limit of 3 (21 Arcanes in total). Notes Stacks additively with mods like Serration and Hornet Strike as well as Holster Amp and Vigorous Swap. The buff duration cannot be refreshed, and must be triggered again through a new wall latch, after it expires. Special Interactions Main article: Universal Weapon Bonuses Does not apply to regular melee attacks or exalted melee weapons like Exalted Blade. However, applies to certain melee projectiles Multiplicatively Exodia Contagion (but additively with mods that are multiplicatively on exodia contagion such as Vigorous Swap or Holster Amp). Thrown Glaive damage from direct hit and heavy explosion Stacks additively with Power Throw. Additively Exodia Epidemic Tenet Agendus Tenet Grigori Wolf Sledge Ceramic Dagger Incarnon Ack & Brunt Incarnon Hate Incarnon Applies to certain abilities and is multiplicative to Ability Strength Shuriken Desolate Hands Spectral Scream Fireball Radial Javelin Dread Mirror's explosion damage and Seeking Talons's contact damage Redline's bolts Kumihimo's threads Warding Halo's damage Null Star Enthrall's projectiles Pyrotechnics Razorwing's Dex Pixia, but not Diwata Minelayer Tether-Flechette Orb's damage Breach Surge sparks Sol Gate's basic ability and Cataclysmic Gate Augment Final Stand Widow's Brood's darts Trivia Prior to Update 24.2 (2018-12-18), Arcane Arachne damage multiplier could be stacked upon subsequent wall latches, allowing for a large damage increase on the next hit. Patch History Update 31.6 (2022-06-09) ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff. Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result. Arcane Arachne On Wall Latch: +25/150% damage for 30s (was 5s) Update 24.2 (2018-12-18) Arcane Arachne changed to: On Wall Latch for 2 seconds: 100% Weapon Damage for 20 seconds. Update 17.9.1 (2015-10-29) Introduced with The Jordas Verdict.

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: Arcane Avenger is an Arcane Enhancement that provides a 21% chance whenever the player receives damage to increase the absolute critical chance of all attacks by 45% for 12 seconds (additive to the final critical chance). Can be sold for 1,000 Credits 1,000. Effect Rank Critical Chance Bonus 0 7.5% 1 15% 2 22.5% 3 30% 4 37.5% 5 45% Acquisition Awarded for killing or capturing the Eidolon Hydrolyst. Dropped in Rotation C of Orphix Pluto Proxima or Veil Proxima missions. Item Source Chance Expected Nearly Guaranteed Veil Proxima Orphix / C 2.82% ~ 35 C Rotations 241 ± 80 C Rotations Pluto Proxima Orphix / C 3.33% ~ 30 C Rotations 203 ± 67 C Rotations Tier 1 The Circuit (Normal) 0.67% ~ 149 A Rotations 1027 ± 342 A Rotations Tier 9 The Circuit (Normal) 4% ~ 25 A Rotations 169 ± 56 A Rotations Tier 3 The Circuit (Normal) 0.67% ~ 149 A Rotations 1027 ± 342 A Rotations Eidolon Hydrolyst Kill 11.70% ~ 8 Kills 55 ± 18 Kills Eidolon Hydrolyst Capture 11.24% ~ 8 Kills 57 ± 19 Kills All drop rates data is obtained from DE's official drop tables stored on Module:DropTables/data. See Mission Rewards#Standard Missions for definitions on reward table rotations. For more detailed definitions and information, visit here. History Arcane Avenger was first introduced in the Trial mission The Law of Retribution, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01). The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Avenger from Little Duck for 2,000 Scarlet Credits. The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Avenger from Father for 400 Phasic Cells. The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Avenger from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 6 Grotesque Splinter. The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Avenger from Ordis for 5 Volatile Motes in the first run of the event, and 6 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42. The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Avenger from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 7, costing 4 Dominus Aureus, with a purchase limit of 3 (21 Arcanes in total). Notes Capable of making some abilities crit. Main article: Universal Weapon Bonuses A Rank 5 Arcane Avenger adds 45 percentage points to a weapon's critical chance after other bonuses. Arcane Avenger stacks additively with other percentage point bonuses, such as Covenant and Cat's Eye. For example, an Atterax with a max rank True Steel, Cat's Eye, and Arcane Avenger, will have a [25% × (1 + 120%) + 60% + 45%] = 160% critical chance on attacks. Highly effective with weapons with high critical damage multipliers. Weapons that have 0% critical chance can crit with this enhancement, although due to their lack of a critical multiplier, their damage will not be altered unless the shot lands on the head, which yields 2x critical multiplier. Aside from damage to shields and health, this Arcane can be triggered by the following instances of damage: Damage to player Overguard. Self-damage from Combat Discipline. Damage while the player is invulnerable, such as Absorb. Damage that is rounded down to 0 due to damage reduction or redirection can trigger this Arcane. For example, with a Rank 0 Combat Discipline equipped and Eclipse active for 75% damage reduction, kills will deal [1 × (1 - 75%)] = 0.25 self-damage, which is rounded down to 0, but this Arcane can still trigger. For example, with a Rank 2 Combat Discipline equipped while on Merulina providing 90% damage redirection, kills will deal [4 × (1 - 90%)] = 0.4 self-damage, which is rounded down to 0, but this Arcane can still trigger. Damage while Mesmer Skin or Mesmer Shield is active will not trigger this Arcane, unless Combat Discipline is equipped. Damage from Despoil does not trigger this Arcane. Patch History Update 35.1 (2024-02-20) Fixed certain Arcanes (including but not limited to: Arcane Avenger) having “0” in their Buff icons instead of the actual stat. Update 31.6 (2022-06-09) ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff. Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result. Arcane Avenger On Damaged: 21% (was 4%) chance for +7.5%/45% Critical Chance for 12s (was 2s) Update 16.0 (2015-03-19) Introduced.

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: Arcane Awakening is an Arcane Enhancement that provides a 60% chance on reloading any weapon to increase the damage of secondary weapons for 24 seconds. Can be sold for 500 Credits 500. Effect Rank Secondary Damage Bonus 0 +25% 1 +50% 2 +75% 3 +100% 4 +125% 5 +150% Acquisition Awarded for killing or capturing the Eidolon Gantulyst. Dropped in Rotation C of Orphix Neptune Proxima or Veil Proxima missions. Item Source Chance Expected Nearly Guaranteed Veil Proxima Orphix / C 5.63% ~ 17 C Rotations 119 ± 39 C Rotations Neptune Proxima Orphix / C 6.90% ~ 14 C Rotations 96 ± 32 C Rotations Tier 1 The Circuit (Normal) 0.67% ~ 149 A Rotations 1027 ± 342 A Rotations Tier 9 The Circuit (Normal) 4% ~ 25 A Rotations 169 ± 56 A Rotations Tier 3 The Circuit (Normal) 0.67% ~ 149 A Rotations 1027 ± 342 A Rotations Eidolon Gantulyst Capture 14.63% ~ 6 Kills 43 ± 14 Kills Eidolon Gantulyst Kill 18.25% ~ 5 Kills 34 ± 11 Kills All drop rates data is obtained from DE's official drop tables stored on Module:DropTables/data. See Mission Rewards#Standard Missions for definitions on reward table rotations. For more detailed definitions and information, visit here. History Arcane Awakening was first introduced in the Trial mission The Law of Retribution, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01). The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Awakening from Little Duck for 1,500 Scarlet Credits. The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Awakening from Father for 300 Phasic Cells. The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Awakening from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 5 Grotesque Splinter. The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Awakening from Ordis for 3 Volatile Motes in the first run of the event, and 4 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42. The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Awakening from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 7, costing 2 Dominus Aureus, with a purchase limit of 3 (21 Arcanes in total). Notes The increase in damage stacks additively with mods that increase base damage e.g. Hornet Strike. Triggers at the end of the reload. Weapons that cannot reload, such as Bows, Epitaph, and Battery Weapons, cannot activate the buff. However, they can benefit from it once it has been activated by reloading a different weapon. This also applies to Mesa's Regulators/ Regulators Prime. Weapons that reload per shell have a chance of activating Awakening each time a shell is loaded. The passive reload from mods like Eject Magazine or the Synth Set will activate Awakening. Bugs Base Regulators receive double the base damage bonus. Regulators Prime work normally however. Patch History Hotfix 34.0.8 (2023-11-09) Fixed Arcane Awakening buff description showing up as a file path. Update 31.6 (2022-06-09) ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff. Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result. Arcane Awakening On Reload: 60% (was 10%) chance for +25%/150% Damage to Pistols for 24s (was 4s) Update 22.12 (2018-02-09) Increased from Damage 15% to 25% and bumped chance. Update 16.0 (2015-03-19) Introduced.

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: Arcane Barrier is an Arcane Enhancement that provides a chance to instantly restore Warframe Shields whenever the player receives damage to their Warframe Shields. Can be sold for 3,000 Credits 3,000. Effect Rank Chance to fully restore shields Cooldown 0 1% 1s 1 2% 2s 2 3% 3s 3 4% 4s 4 5% 5s 5 6% 6s Acquisition Awarded for capturing the Eidolon Hydrolyst. Dropped in Rotation C of Orphix Veil Proxima missions. Item Source Chance Expected Nearly Guaranteed Veil Proxima Orphix / C 1.41% ~ 70 C Rotations 486 ± 162 C Rotations Eidolon Hydrolyst Capture 5% ~ 20 Kills 134 ± 44 Kills All drop rates data is obtained from DE's official drop tables stored on Module:DropTables/data. See Mission Rewards#Standard Missions for definitions on reward table rotations. For more detailed definitions and information, visit here. History Arcane Barrier was first introduced in the Trial mission The Law of Retribution, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01). The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Barrier from Little Duck for 4,000 Scarlet Credits. The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Barrier from Father for 800 Phasic Cells. The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Barrier from Jahu Gargoyle for 12 Grotesque Splinter The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Barrier from Ordis for 46 Volatile Motes in the first run of the event, and 30 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42. The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Barrier from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 3, costing 22 Dominus Aureus, with a purchase limit of 7 (21 Arcanes in total). Notes Can proc even while channeling abilities that drain shields over time (such as Hildryn's Haven or Aegis Storm), although the drain itself will not trigger it Trading Arcane Barrier requires the trader to be Mastery Rank 11 or higher. Tips Strong synergy with high shield frames, such as Caliban, Harrow, Hildryn, Mag, or Styanax, and Adaptation, allowing shields to resist high amounts of damage and continually be restored to full. Patch History Update 36.0 (2024-06-18) Trading Legendary Arcanes now requires you to be Mastery Rank 11. It’s important to note that only the player trading requires MR 11 and not the receiver. We needed to address an issue with exploitative Legendary Arcanes farming and ban-evasion account creation used to trade Legendary Arcanes. Other options we experimented with had the risk of innocent players getting caught in the crossfire of the ban-evasion subjects. When we took a look at Trading stats we found that a very small number of players below Mastery Rank 10 trade Legendary Arcanes, and therefore decided to go this route. This is purely a means of deterring the bad actors and ensuring legitimate trading for players. Update 27.2 (2020-03-05) Added 2 additional ranks for a maximum of 6% chance to activate from a 4% chance to activate. Now specifies On Shield Damage (can no longer activate when receiving damage to health). Update 16.0 (2015-03-19) Introduced.

The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Barrier from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 3, costing 22 Dominus Aureus, with a purchase limit of 7 (21 Arcanes in total). Notes Can proc even while channeling abilities that drain shields over time (such as Hildryn's Haven or Aegis Storm), although the drain itself will not trigger it Trading Arcane Barrier requires the trader to be Mastery Rank 11 or higher. Tips Strong synergy with high shield frames, such as Caliban, Harrow, Hildryn, Mag, or Styanax, and Adaptation, allowing shields to resist high amounts of damage and continually be restored to full. Patch History Update 36.0 (2024-06-18) Trading Legendary Arcanes now requires you to be Mastery Rank 11. It’s important to note that only the player trading requires MR 11 and not the receiver. We needed to address an issue with exploitative Legendary Arcanes farming and ban-evasion account creation used to trade Legendary Arcanes. Other options we experimented with had the risk of innocent players getting caught in the crossfire of the ban-evasion subjects. When we took a look at Trading stats we found that a very small number of players below Mastery Rank 10 trade Legendary Arcanes, and therefore decided to go this route. This is purely a means of deterring the bad actors and ensuring legitimate trading for players. Update 27.2 (2020-03-05) Added 2 additional ranks for a maximum of 6% chance to activate from a 4% chance to activate. Now specifies On Shield Damage (can no longer activate when receiving damage to health). Update 16.0 (2015-03-19) Introduced.

sections.title: Article body
sections.text: Arcane Battery is an Arcane Enhancement for Warframes that increases maximum energy per point of armor, up to 1,000 additional energy. Effect Rank Maximum Energy per Armor Point Armor needed to Cap 0 0.05 20000 1 0.10 10000 2 0.15 6667 3 0.20 5000 4 0.25 4000 5 0.30 3334 Acquisition Can be obtained from Ascension's optional Sister of Parvos, summoned by acquiring 3 Sister Beacons during the elevator ride. Can be purchased from Ordis in the Drifter's Camp for 10 Vestigial Motes, for a total of 210 for max rank. Dropped by Captain Vor in Tolstoj (Mercury) in The Steel Path. Item Source Chance Expected Nearly Guaranteed Sisters of Parvos (Ascension Hard Mode) 20% ~ 5 Kills 30 ± 10 Kills Sisters of Parvos (Ascension Mode) 6.60% ~ 15 Kills 101 ± 33 Kills All drop rates data is obtained from DE's official drop tables stored on Module:DropTables/data. See Mission Rewards#Standard Missions for definitions on reward table rotations. For more detailed definitions and information, visit here. Notes Maximum energy changes dynamically based on any changes to armor. As such, being inflicted with a Corrosive or Heat status effect will remove armor and some of your bonus energy, requiring you to regain it. Heat procs are far more common and more deadly, as they will remove up to half of your total armor while burning. Health Conversion is unwieldy on Simulacrum and might look like it isn't properly updating, but it does work mostly reliably in normal missions. If armor is reduced, such as losing a stack of Health Conversion, any energy overflow will immediately disappear. When combined with Preparation or similar effects, the increased energy maximum is applied first, resulting in a higher starting energy as well. Tips Works well with mods like Quick Thinking and Rage. Qorvex, Valkyr Prime, and Kullervo have high base armor that grants them effective use of this arcane. Effects that can grant increased armor include: Atlas's Passive Chroma's Vex Armor and Cold Elemental Ward Frost's Passive Grendel's Passive and Gourmand Helminth's Parasitic Armor Hydroid's Plunder Inaros' Scarab Shell Nyx's Psychic Bolts Oberon's Renewal and Hallowed Reckoning Octavia's Metronome Rhino's Ironclad Charge Valkyr's Warcry Vauban's Bastille Wukong's Defy Steel Fiber Health Conversion Arcane Guardian Arcane Reaper Arcane Tanker Arcane Ultimatum Melee Fortification Unairu's Stone Skin Syndicate Justice effects (+15%). Patch History Update 42.0 (2026-03-25) Fixed Arcane Battery not interacting with the Melee Critical Damage bonus from Violet Archon Shards. Fixed max energy increase from Arcane Battery’s not counting towards the energy needed for Violet Archon Shard’s damage boost. Update 40.0 (2025-10-15) The following Arcane Collections are now available for Vosfor in Loid’s Arcane Dissolution: Cavia Arcane Collection (includes the Cavia and Ascension Arcanes) Arcane Battery Update 39.0 (2025-06-25) We’ve added Arcanes to the end of mission rewards in the Vor fight on the Steel Path. Defeat him for a guaranteed Arcane from the following list Arcane Battery Update 38.0 (2024-12-13) Fixed equipping Arcane Battery causing Companion previews to not work properly in the Arsenal. Update 37.0 (2024-10-02) Fixed Shield-based Warframes (i.e Hildryn) gaining an Energy stat in the UI when Arcane Battery is equipped. Update 36.0 (2024-06-18) Introduced.
